name: Dan Goldman
subject: Thank youPeter,
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dan Goldman for New York
body:
Dan Goldman for New York [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP31/1/94087/images/Logo-White-BG.png]
Peter,

I know you probably received a lot of fundraising emails from campaigns like ours at the end of the month. And trust me, I understand they can be overwhelming.

But today, I’m not reaching out to ask for your support in the form of a donation. I’m just emailing to say thank you.

Thanks to this team, our campaign closed out April with strong fundraising numbers and real momentum — and not a moment too soon. This month underscored what we already knew in our bones: the threats posed by the Trump-MAGA agenda are immediate and serious, and getting worse as he gets more desperate.

From attempts to roll back our fundamental rights to attacks on institutions that are designed to protect American democracy, the future of our country is on the line.

Your support in April didn’t just help our campaign reach a fundraising goal. It makes a real difference in our ability to keep team members on the ground, talking to New Yorkers about what’s at stake in this election. Because you showed up — by donating, volunteering, or spreading the word — we’re able to reach even more voters.

I don’t take that for granted.

Serving in Congress right now means making sure New Yorkers are protected and that the people trying to undermine our rights are held accountable.

Your support makes that work possible.

It gives us the runway to keep fighting — for democracy, for justice, for families squeezed by rising costs, and for the basic rights generations before us fought to secure.

From all of us here at Team Goldman, thank you for being part of this effort.

We’ll keep you updated on what’s next, but for today, please know how grateful I am for your partnership and trust.

Thank you,

Dan

P.S. If you weren't able to contribute before the end of April, it's still not too late to chip in and make a difference. You can click here to donate any amount to send me back to Congress, where I will continue fighting back against this corrupt administration. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dsg-em-may26]
Dan Goldman for New York
PO BOX 3306
New York, NY 10008
United States

New York, NY 10008

It’s Xavier. I’m writing today to say thank you. Last night, we closed out our end-of-month fundraising deadline with strength, and I could not have done it without you and this grassroots team. Over 65,000 people donated to our campaign just in the month of April, and let me tell you, every contribution made a huge difference. This campaign is built on people stepping up and doing what they can, and that’s exactly what this grassroots team proved last night. Because of the strong show of support, we’re able to keep moving forward. I’m able to stay out in communities, keep reaching voters on the ground and on TV, and continue building the kind of campaign it takes to win. We still have our work cut out ahead of us as we enter the final month of this primary. So I am counting on your continued support, because that is the only way we can prove that a campaign that isn't powered by big money, but powered by people, can win. Together. Muchas gracias, Tío Xavier **Wait... before you click away! 🇺🇸** Xavier Becerra has spent over three decades in public service. He’s courageously stood up to Donald Trump by suing his administration 122 times and fought to expand healthcare access for all Americans through the ACA. **Now, Xavier is running to become California’s next governor, but his self-funding billionaire opponent is pummeling him with attacks and smears to stop him from serving California.** The California primary is just over a month away, and Xavier urgently needs your help to fend off these attacks. clean_body: | It’s Xavier. I’m writing today to say thank you. Last night, we closed out our end-of-month fundraising deadline with strength, and I could not have done it without you and this grassroots team. Over 65,000 people donated to our campaign just in the month of April, and let me tell you, every contribution made a huge difference. This campaign is built on people stepping up and doing what they can, and that’s exactly what this grassroots team proved last night. Because of the strong show of support, we’re able to keep moving forward. I’m able to stay out in communities, keep reaching voters on the ground and on TV, and continue building the kind of campaign it takes to win. We still have our work cut out ahead of us as we enter the final month of this primary. So I am counting on your continued support, because that is the only way we can prove that a campaign that isn't powered by big money, but powered by people, can win. Together. Muchas gracias, Tío Xavier Wait... before you click away! 🇺🇸 Xavier Becerra has spent over three decades in public service. He’s courageously stood up to Donald Trump by suing his administration 122 times and fought to expand healthcare access for all Americans through the ACA. Now, Xavier is running to become California’s next governor, but his self-funding billionaire opponent is pummeling him with attacks and smears to stop him from serving California. The California primary is just over a month away, and Xavier urgently needs your help to fend off these attacks. = | | = Becerra for Governor 2026 330 N Brand Blvd Suite 700 Glendale, CA 91203 United States mailto:info@xavierbecerra2026.com | Paid for by Becerra for Governor 2026

body:
https://tnhousedems.com/ [https://tnhousedems.com/]
Peter,
It’s official. Lame duck Governor Bill Lee bent the knee to lame duck Donald Trump and agreed to call the recently adjourned legislature back for a special session. Lee and Republican leaders want to redraw our state’s congressional districts to allow a corrupt president to continue abusing the power of his office.
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP03/1/68714/images/headline%20image.jpg]
This is a total assault on what remains of fair and equal representation in Tennessee and, with the Supreme Court removing any guardrails, the disenfranchisement of black voters in this state. We won’t give up without a fight. Tennessee House Democrats are planning to fight like hell in this special session. We need your help - can you chip in $5, $10, or even $50 to help us resist this attempt to strip Tennessee of any representation at the Federal level? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tndems_email26?refcode=260501email]
DONATE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tndems_email26?refcode=260501email]
We aren’t ready to give up, and I know you aren’t either.
- TN House Dems

name: Harry Dunn
subject: Peter... hard email to write
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Blue Amp Action
body:
| My pain and my trauma didn't end on January 7.
---
_You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you only want our most important email updates,[click here.](https://act.blueampaction.com/go/108?t=2&akid=2172%2E259176%2E6C0yEx) If you'd like to unsubscribe, [click here.](https://act.blueampaction.com/go/109?t=3&akid=2172%2E259176%2E6C0yEx)_

[](https://act.blueampaction.com/go/10889?source=LOC_EM_FR_2026.05.01_Dunn-Straight_B5_F1_S5_C5_90AC-365DON_RS-T1&t=4&akid=2172%2E259176%2E6C0yEx) Hey Peter, I'll be straight with you: I've done a lot of tough things in my life. I played college football, tried to make it in the NFL. That was tough, mentally, physically, emotionally. I served as a Capitol police officer for over 15 years, and those were tough some days. But the aftermath of the January 6th insurrection was one of the toughest things I've been through. My pain and my trauma didn't end on January 7. Even after the violence of that day, I dealt with death threats. I dealt with people harassing me for speaking out. And when I decided to testify in front of Congress? It was one of the toughest obstacles I've ever faced. But I did testify, because it was my duty not just as an officer, but as an American. I made a big step the day I testified for the January 6th committee. I stepped in the direction of protecting our democracy. And Peter, I've never looked back. [**I'm ready to protect our democracy as a Congressman, and I need you to join me. I wouldn’t ask if this fight wasn’t absolutely critical – will you split a donation of just $10 between my campaign and Blue Amp Action to help me hit my $100,000 weekly goal?**](https://act.blueampaction.com/go/10889?source=LOC_EM_FR_2026.05.01_Dunn-Straight_B5_F1_S5_C5_90AC-365DON_RS-T1&t=5&akid=2172%2E259176%2E6C0yEx) _If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation to Harry Dunn and Blue Amp Action will go through immediately:_ | [DONATE $10 >>](https://act.blueampaction.com/go/10890?source=LOC_EM_FR_2026.05.01_Dunn-Straight_B5_F1_S5_C5_90AC-365DON_RS-T1&t=6&akid=2172%2E259176%2E6C0yEx)

Harry Dunn

We’re facing a critical moment in American history, which means what we do right now will define the future of our nation and our world.

Here at Blue Amp, we’re dedicated to uplifting the movers and shakers who will defend our shared democratic values and beat back against right-wing authoritarianism. We’re amplifying good candidates who are passionate about protecting civil rights, making life affordable, and leaving a better world for future generations.

The midterms are coming up, and we can’t do this work without you. With your support, we'll work to halt the right-wing agenda and support strong Democrats who will fight for us across the country.

**[Chip in to Blue Amp Action today to support our mission and help us continue boosting Democratic champions and holding extremists accountable. name: Annie Andrews
subject: Tylenol does NOT cause autism…
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY RESIST RECLAIM REBUILD PAC (WWW
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rrr-annieandrews [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rrr-annieandrews]
I'm a pediatrician. I’m pissed. I’m scared. And I need your help to defeat Lindsey Graham in the U.S. Senate.
I’m not the “monsters-under-the-bed” kind of scared. I’m the “I’ve watched children suffer from preventable illness and injury while politicians do absolutely nothing” kind of scared.
I’ve treated kids who’ve been shot. Kids whose families struggle to afford their medications.
Kids who live with mold that causes asthma attacks, miss school for climate disasters, and watch politicians use them as political pawns.
And now? Trump is causing chaos and Lindsey Graham is right by his side.
And Medicaid — the program that keeps half our pediatric patients insured, healthy, and...alive — is on the chopping block to pay for tax breaks for billionaires.
So yeah, I never planned to run for Senate.
But I’m scared. I’m pissed. I’m motivated. And I’m 100% in.
I’m running for Senate in South Carolina to defeat Lindsey Graham and to fight for the kids no one in D.C. seems to be thinking about. So I need you with me.
We need to hit 10,000 donations before midnight to prove that this campaign can go the distance. Can I count on you to split a donation with my campaign and Resist Reclaim Rebuild? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rrr-annieandrews]
HELP ME DEFEAT LINDSEY GRAHAM! >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rrr-annieandrews]
I’m running because the Republican Party, led by people like Lindsey Graham, is dismantling the very systems that keep our kids safe here in South Carolina.
I’m running because the issues I care about — children’s healthcare, vaccines, gun violence, public education, Social Security, Medicaid and Medicare — are under direct attack right now.
And I’m running because I consider it my professional duty as a pediatrician — to give children a strong voice at the national level. Too many are comfortable on the sidelines. That won’t work for me.
We need bold leaders who aren’t afraid to fight. I am ready.
Even though the path will be hard, I know Lindsey Graham must be held accountable. He certainly doesn’t deserve a free pass back to D.C. for six more years.
That’s why I’m running for US Senate. To defeat Trump’s lapdog and fight for everyday Americans. But, I need your support today. Can you rush a split donation of $10 (or whatever you can spare) between Resist Reclaim Rebuild and me to become a Founding Donor to my campaign? >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rrr-annieandrews]
CHIP IN TO STOP MAGA EXTREMISM!>> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rrr-annieandrews]
Thank you for believing in this campaign,
Dr. Annie Andrews
Resist Reclaim Rebuild is an organization committed to helping Democrats win up and down the ballot, nationwide.
PAID FOR BY RESIST RECLAIM REBUILD PAC (WWW.RESISTRECLAIMREBUILD.COM) AND NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR CANDIDATE COMMITTEE.
Resist Reclaim Rebuild
1331 FLORIDA AVE NW
Washington, DC 20009
United States

And I’m running because I consider it my professional duty as a pediatrician — to give children a strong voice at the national level. Too many are comfortable on the sidelines. That won’t work for me. We need bold leaders who aren’t afraid to fight. I am ready. Even though the path will be hard, I know Lindsey Graham must be held accountable. He certainly doesn’t deserve a free pass back to D.C. for six more years. That’s why I’m running for US Senate. To defeat Trump’s lapdog and fight for everyday Americans. But, I need your support today. Can you rush a split donation of $10 (or whatever you can spare) between Resist Reclaim Rebuild and me to become a Founding Donor to my campaign? >> [] CHIP IN TO STOP MAGA EXTREMISM!>> [] Thank you for believing in this campaign, Dr. Annie Andrews Resist Reclaim Rebuild is an organization committed to helping Democrats win up and down the ballot, nationwide. PAID FOR BY RESIST RECLAIM REBUILD PAC (WWW.RESISTRECLAIMREBUILD.COM) AND NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR CANDIDATE COMMITTEE. Friend, I’ve spent my career staring down threats to our nation, but what keeps me up at night isn’t just a single policy—it’s the prospect of a Senate that no longer serves as a check on overreach.
[**The Senate is supposed to be the anchor of our democracy. It is the last line of defense for our values. But right now, that line is wearing thin.**](https://links.rogersforsenate.com/u/click?_t=293de5a6223e4436ab53fb8020578890&_m=2ece474e1aae42948ad8f19d217f32e9&_e=TjfrOikzfxvlHVT_G8vNVmwOaj9vzFpPMCtrseXuMxxx0ONtbn9wrj6Atnh4ITltGvVgoOA0g2_Yt-IV3k5F2qr0WdSqLJP7Q7lT_wkSOAedGFQs3-DtttdhfYgfOhV15NiYoXoaky9kYqZx8TUbJWZzLGTZw6VSyQvICGv8pDVcLFg7aMYKY0AYqzkhneprG0rWRVa_QvOgG1k1dumKphKyk0sfqeCfs556jFmW6DRhHXRv7rddDB2FCh-usC0lfBTIWUo5M-q-CTuGbZNRDpxh2pn4dyRiN_2G7ieYQGJzsqH8EjPOO2TJiI2H69TyYEzAY0YJ9awUzK37nHUQZfmsasHIJe8fh6FZUG3AHdRdTK3XVg7VAeEk5YLZePGrrtfUrvtWRwNxLu9NZHrA7BmPxKd9tOHC60IV-I8-L0O1UbcQB13rGytBaUaU0Tboc6b9i8SBHTD2RXw0YGAW-MsFfcT_QHs7upaGEFbmrG13fFdInLepKBPzxqkqiEX3FXQFhwaCuOqAmeKnv-zQrgKn5OTeRliznmikoB57uzojEvzcAYMf9YCYLP7SoGHPSJm9JLyMyGlfSCtphuzjCi6FD5I7lKj3WIsGLw5150_lWvM6-CMvGTv_aRE2rmuZ70iuK3iS1dMd9gHWebj9npsnRWE2XUoM5a4cq9RC--0mGzynpqU5IH5K5BNEAdj7wZ1VlkuYc6qQzsk_RP1_M_W1H86rk5GFhscEwNp_m2xG5iPnGuNYFbPOEtSSc4NkG7qXWoqKdVN3DHXZNRYchG4jO5MmKAu_W94w8kVw5q6XLplmGjWKKOtIIVxZ8eitOsiwatw0HUAXoXw9LDKFuw%3D%3D)
[_**This Senate seat is the tipping point.**_](https://links.rogersforsenate.com/u/click?_t=293de5a6223e4436ab53fb8020578890&_m=2ece474e1aae42948ad8f19d217f32e9&_e=TjfrOikzfxvlHVT_G8vNVmwOaj9vzFpPMCtrseXuMxxx0ONtbn9wrj6Atnh4ITltGvVgoOA0g2_Yt-IV3k5F2qr0WdSqLJP7Q7lT_wkSOAedGFQs3-DtttdhfYgfOhV15NiYoXoaky9kYqZx8TUbJWZzLGTZw6VSyQvICGv8pDVcLFg7aMYKY0AYqzkhneprG0rWRVa_QvOgG1k1dumKphKyk0sfqeCfs556jFmW6DRhHXRv7rddDB2FCh-usC0lfBTIWUo5M-q-CTuGbZNRDpxh2pn4dyRiN_2G7ieYQGJzsqH8EjPOO2TJiI2H69TyMHgADe2fbu48Bt4vElit5hk9ynJfe3xCeTHbVR6KR6c3cn6Y3_QW2tPEnUUJnKR4rifdwIbyPCF4inBIXLLKQbPRYT6UOKwK0iDBr_bav335FdqyQRQmVDb4HhoLjyntfamsnKKNyjsw2SHs9LT54dZvuWu9OtR-NjeKxiLaWxKJB57bd5LDW5he6E6QexgnverdktTw0zzGMixnSGciYhc5fjB1w6FsQj4PQmWfVuMh6qlqKGJYg87QVxSi5W20qEmKBGDxnIQNPbfAxkE65kwTkfPp_VkXAy-YvhFRwufBPkQdFgV5Z0MSshL78bHd0SPCmwL_rjHV6qZ1R_qK5XSqlYgM8mwt1s0x_xDxmxr03K0SZqGQaDf_nNA8x_kfeHu66htZ6UBs_jIDV_Ai8VwwQPQZ53oyEFU-DcpP-T4FBn1OvZyOAGF-3I1KXGL4zANMVbpkzGy42Rr71gyx1k27y8TVprCt83sZhgfSkQNHlKwJ6KmmaaCjEu_uXcogvpsN39V3E7ZaTMHp2zXqJw%3D%3D) If we don't hold the line here in Michigan, we lose the majority. And if the majority falls, the gate is wide open for a radical agenda that will reshape our institutions for a generation.

clean_body: This is about the future of the Senate majority. | | | Friend, I’ve spent my career staring down threats to our nation, but what keeps me up at night isn’t just a single policy—it’s the prospect of a Senate that no longer serves as a check on overreach. If we don't hold the line here in Michigan, we lose the majority. And if the majority falls, the gate is wide open for a radical agenda that will reshape our institutions for a generation. I am finalizing my 2026 battle plan to keep the Senate in steady hands, but I won’t move an inch without your input. I need to know that you understand the stakes of this institutional fight. Let’s make sure we aren't the generation that watched the majority slip away. I’m counting on you, Mike Rogers | | Text JOIN to 22033 | | | | This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. | | Happy May everyone! In this edition, I will talk about an Xcel town hall, drought declarations, thank you for protecting right to repair, the status of my bills, local & constituent events, and the news.
---
|
---
|
---
| |
# [XCEL TOWN HALL]()
---
|
---
| | On May 16th, Xcel Energy is hosting a JeffCo Red Truck town hall. Xcel Energy's RED Truck program, a mobile community service, will be on-site, bringing energy experts to the neighborhood to help residents lower their bills, navigate assistance programs, and learn about renewable energy options.
State legislators will also share an update on the 2026 Colorado legislative session, including key bills and outcomes, followed by a local update from your county commissioners.
Bring your questions and a neighbor!
It will be at the West Metro Fire Protection District (433 S Allison Pkwy, Lakewood, CO 80226) from 2-4pm.
---
|
---
|
---
| |
# [DROUGHT DECLARED]()
---
| | Both Golden and Arvada have announced drought restrictions.
**Drought Stage 1 Declared by GOLDEN**
* Restrictions Go Into Effect **May 1**
* Fines will be implemented for not following regulations associated with drought.
* **DO:**
* [ Monitor water usage via Aquahawk](https://us.list-manage.com/zJqQKj9GxyW?e=8249791cac&c2id=e763ef12f194e81aba6520b7d8766332), the free tool provided by the City of Golden. It will assist in catching any leaks.
* **Continue to hand water trees during allowed times** , as drought-stressed trees are more likely to contract disease or pests.
* Use the cycle and soak method for outdoor irrigation. [View this helpful explainer to learn more about it](https://us.list-manage.com/12HoQ5HTA44?e=8249791cac&c2id=e763ef12f194e81aba6520b7d8766332).
* Hand water perennials once per week, during allowed times.
* **DO NOT:**
* **Do not use automatic sprinkler irrigation more than twice per week**. During those two days, watering is only permitted from 6:00 p.m. to 10:00 a.m.
* **Do not hand wash your vehicle**. Utilize local car wash businesses (who recycle water).
* **Do not allow water to pool or runoff** into gutters, storm sewers, ditches, channels, etc. Any irrigation system leak must be prepared within 10 days of the City providing notice of a leak.
* Thank you for respecting drought restrictions. Please refer to the [City's Water Conservation Page for a full list of rules and regulations](https://us.list-manage.com/lpAciitZTx4?e=8249791cac&c2id=e763ef12f194e81aba6520b7d8766332).

**Stage 1 Water Restrictions Declared by ARVADA**
* Stage 1 water restrictions went into effect on **April 15** for the City of Arvada. Across both City services, residential, and business uses, the goal is to reduce water use by 20%. Conserving water is a community effort, and every step makes a difference. Together, we can protect the water supply we all share. Waiting to turn on sprinkler systems until May will help conserve water by leaving your lawn dormant for longer.
* Key details include:
* Lawn watering is limited to **two assigned days per week** (see schedule on our website).
* **No watering 10 a.m. to 6 p.m**.
* **Trees, shrubs, perennials, vegetable gardens, and flowers** can be watered any day using a handheld hose or drip irrigation, outside of 10 a.m. to 6 p.m.
* **New grass seed or sod installation** is subject to the 2-day watering schedule from June to August.
* **Do not waste water** by letting it spray on concrete and asphalt or pool in gutters, streets, and alleys.
* **Do not irrigate during** rain or high winds.
* Visit the [water restrictions webpage](https://us.list-manage.com/BwMH_g1VyAx?e=8249791cac&c2id=e763ef12f194e81aba6520b7d8766332) for more information.

---
| |
# [THANK YOU FOR PROTECTING RIGHT TO REPAIR!]()
---
|
---
| | I wanted to say a huge thank you to everyone who testified and talked to their legislators.
Because of your help, [SB26-090](https://us.list-manage.com/OLmZvTNSNzo?e=8249791cac&c2id=e763ef12f194e81aba6520b7d8766332) (which would’ve gutted my work on right to repair) was postponed indefinitely on April 27th. Colorado will continue to be the best state in the country to fix your stuff. Thank you so much again for your help!
[State lawmakers stand up for Colorado’s landmark Right to Repair consumer protections](https://us.list-manage.com/3xUmJbkjaBt?e=8249791cac&c2id=e763ef12f194e81aba6520b7d8766332)

---
| |
# BIL[L TRACKER]()
---
|
---
| | I currently have 8 bills introduced. They are:
[**HB26-1036 Local Taxes on Vacant Residential Property**](https://us.list-manage.com/1B3vP5GI20X?e=8249791cac&c2id=e763ef12f194e81aba6520b7d8766332)
* Unfortunately, this bill was killed in the House Finance committee on Monday, February 9th. You can read about it [here](https://us.list-manage.com/N16rfoKoYiR?e=8249791cac&c2id=e763ef12f194e81aba6520b7d8766332).

[**HB26-1099 Protect Financial Condition of Homeowners Association**](https://us.list-manage.com/dvvY7uX4Rg6?e=8249791cac&c2id=e763ef12f194e81aba6520b7d8766332)
* The bill was signed into law on the 13th!

[**HB26-1137 Requirements for Campaign Consultants**](https://us.list-manage.com/f7ob5odq2Sn?e=8249791cac&c2id=e763ef12f194e81aba6520b7d8766332)
* Unfortunately, this bill was killed during its final reading on March 10th. You can read an article about the bill [here](https://us.list-manage.com/dYvhtVx0vuC?e=8249791cac&c2id=e763ef12f194e81aba6520b7d8766332).

[**HB26-1188 Sunset Process Securities Regulation**](https://us.list-manage.com/eotkw6ZSyPL?e=8249791cac&c2id=e763ef12f194e81aba6520b7d8766332)
* This bill passed the Senate on 3rd reading and will now be heard in the House one last time for approval.

[**HB26-1258 Changes to Practices Relating to Death**](https://us.list-manage.com/vwH4cjbWKZc?e=8249791cac&c2id=e763ef12f194e81aba6520b7d8766332)
* This bill passed the Senate on 3rd reading and will now be heard in the House one last time for approval.

[**HB26-1270 Ownership of Agricultural Data**](https://us.list-manage.com/imPpEFtt6ps?e=8249791cac&c2id=e763ef12f194e81aba6520b7d8766332)
* Unfortunately, this bill was killed in the House Agriculture, Water, & Natural Resources Committee on March 16th.

[**SB26-114 Spirituous Liquor Manufacturer Sales Rooms & Other Alcohol**](https://us.list-manage.com/Dg3V3kKyVwQ?e=8249791cac&c2id=e763ef12f194e81aba6520b7d8766332)
* It passed the Senate Appropriations Committee and is now scheduled to be heard in the House Finance Committee on Thursday, May 7th. You can listen in [here](https://us.list-manage.com/f69bEjKbxxJ?e=8249791cac&c2id=e763ef12f194e81aba6520b7d8766332).

[**HB26-1346 Transfer of Unsold Insurance Premium Tax Credits**](https://us.list-manage.com/k34Ay4QzNsw?e=8249791cac&c2id=e763ef12f194e81aba6520b7d8766332)
* Scheduled to be heard for 2nd reading on the Senate floor on May 1st.

The Stamp Out Hunger Food Drive provides the food needed to feed families in need during the busy summer months. Community Table is looking for over 150 volunteers to help collect, sort, and pack food at three locations in Arvada and Wheat Ridge on Saturday, May 9.
[Sign up to be a driver or driver helper](https://us.list-manage.com/vQYl51kdHA9?e=8249791cac&c2id=e763ef12f194e81aba6520b7d8766332)

name: Vikram Mansharamani
subject: 🤔 Mansharamani Musings for April: Review of Vikram's latest thoughts 💭
body:
A review of Vikram's April posts...

| | | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/mansharamani/vikram-update-april?e=93b2c2b824)
---
[|
---](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=a6951dc308&e=93b2c2b824)
| | | | | |
# Mansharamani Musings
---
| | [](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=b3bd4be440&e=93b2c2b824)April was an action-packed month during which geopolitics and technology continued to dominate headlines. I had the opportunity to meet with business leaders from many different industries and almost every one of them was concerned about rising inflation, higher energy prices, and workforce challenges. See below for links to my April musings...
Happy May!_**
- Vikram**_

---
[|
---](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=ef4750d0b6&e=93b2c2b824)
| | **APRIL 5, 2026**

In July 1971, Henry Kissinger [boarded a flight from Islamabad](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=a5ee227453&e=93b2c2b824) to Beijing that supposedly didn’t exist. After feigning illness, the National Security Advisor took a covert trip to China that laid the groundwork for Nixon’s later visit. All of this was possible due to Pakistan’s president efforts to create a secret backchannel—one that ultimately reshaped the Cold War as Kissinger managed to cleave Beijing away from Moscow. It may have been the most consequential piece of shuttle diplomacy of the 20th century...and it ran through a country that didn’t exist in 1946 and that most Americans couldn’t find on a map.
And now, 55 years later, Pakistan appears poised to once again play a [key role](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=7cb6e0480e&e=93b2c2b824) in global affairs. With the U.S. on the verge of another Forever War, a politically problematic development for the Trump Administration, the US President is eager to find an off ramp. Turns out America’s “frenemy” might be the only country in the world capable of throwing Washington a lifeline...
---
| [CLICK HERE TO CONTINUE READING](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=b1c18c124a&e=93b2c2b824)
---
|
---
[|
---](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=042255ea1b&e=93b2c2b824)
| | **APRIL 12, 2026**

Like many of us, I have been thinking – [and writing](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=b514ab1390&e=93b2c2b824) – a lot about AI. I have been exploring its potential and the pitfalls, and have settled on an idea I call [Schrödinger’s AI](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=f9b493bebf&e=93b2c2b824), that AI is both revolutionary and dangerous at the same time. (It’s a tip of the hat to [Schrödinger’s Cat](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=3b8632339c&e=93b2c2b824), a famous thought experiment in quantum mechanics in which a cat is both alive and dead). Like the hypothetical cat, AI is currently both promising and terrifying. Human decisions (in the form of policies, accepted norms, or other guardrails), I have argued, will push the AI development trajectory into one scenario or another.
A recent development has tipped the scales, however, and it’s something that should alarm everyone. Anthropic released a model last week called [Claude Mythos Preview](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=618665bee4&e=93b2c2b824). It is, by every measure, the most capable AI system ever built. It can find and exploit software vulnerabilities that have evaded human experts for decades—including a [27-year-old bug in OpenBSD](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=a25ffa143a&e=93b2c2b824) that had, until last week, gone completely undetected. It scored **93.9% on SWE-bench** – an evaluation site that tests Large Language Models and their ability to solve problems -**** far exceeding the scores earned by every other model in existence...
---
| [CLICK HERE TO CONTINUE READING](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=843ae1e197&e=93b2c2b824)
---
|
---
[|
---](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=4f3cb432b6&e=93b2c2b824)
| | **APRIL 19, 2026**

Every conversation I have with business owners, investors, and fellow executives lately seems to begin the same way: prices keep going up, and there doesn’t seem to be any reason for the trend to stop. Oil, food, housing, and labor costs all appear to be on a one way path.
At Goodwell Foods, the frozen pizza manufacturer I run in New Hampshire, I see it daily. Freight has skyrocketed on the back of higher fuel prices. Labor in rural New England remains stubbornly tight. Even whey prices, which we use in our high-protein offerings, seem to only go in one direction.
In other words, inflation is real, self-reinforcing, and disruptive. For the next one-to-three years, it deserves to be prominent on boardroom agendas. Politicians are probably on-point to worry that “affordability” is a galvanizing, anti-incumbent driver that will impact the future elections...
---
| [CLICK HERE TO CONTINUE READING](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=0f4a29aa92&e=93b2c2b824)
---
|
---
[|
---](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=87d38d79b6&e=93b2c2b824)
| | **APRIL 26, 2026**

I have a weakness for seemingly irrelevant indicators. Nearly fifteen years ago, [I wrote in _Forbes_ that the race to build the world’s tallest skyscraper was a more robust indicator of forthcoming chaos than most traditional measures that economists track](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=ba554bb59a&e=93b2c2b824). As luck would have, the **world’s tallest skyscraper neatly captures overconfidenceand hubris** , loose money conditions that encourages excess liquidity to find a home, and ubiquitous speculative tendencies as “build it and they will come” logic runs roughshod over more conservative thinking.
Let’s revisit some skyscraper history. Weeks after the Burj Dubai was deemed the world’s tallest freestanding structure, the bursting of a credit bubble began which led to the Global Financial Crisis. Before the Internet bubble peaked, Taipei 101 (home of the hardware side of the tech boom) claimed the title of the world’s tallest tower, displacing the Petronas Towers, a position the Malaysian skyscrapers took in 1997, as the Asian Tigers imploded and spurred the Asian Financial Crisis. In the 1970s, the Willis Tower (aka the Sears Tower) redefined the Chicago skyline and stagflation followed. And perhaps the most iconic skyscraper to telegraph a financial crisis was the Empire State Building, which took the crown of the world’s tallest in 1931 as the Great Depression took hold.

I love unconventional indicators because they capture something official data often miss...they capture real time human behavior while traditional information is usually backward-looking. Today I’d argue that pizza — specifically, **frozen pizza** — is telling us something equally important about the American consumer. And very few people seem to be listening...
---

# EXCLUSIVE SUBSCRIBER CONTENT: INTERVIEW WITH JAMES LITINSKY OF MP MATERIALS
---
[|
---](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=de20fa21ee&e=93b2c2b824)
| | **APRIL 9, 2026**

On March 9th, 2026, Dr. Vikram Mansharamani spoke with Jim Litinsky, Chairman and CEO of MP Materials, America’s leading rare earth and magnet company. Recorded at MP’s magnet plant in Fort Worth, Texas, topics covered in the conversation include:
* Jim’s background as an investor and his path to running a vertically integrated rare earth magnetics company.
* The critical role of human capital and the importance of identifying talent in times of uncertainty.
* How MP has competed against a geopolitical competitor (China) and secured funding to create an American champion.
* How MP, despite being a _de facto_ startup, secured GM and Apple, as well as the Pentagon, as customers.
* Why developing resilience and staying power when dynamics are unfavorable is key to navigating uncertainty...

---
| [CLICK HERE TO WATCH THE CONVERSATION](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=20d900faa8&e=93b2c2b824)

# EXCLUSIVE SUBSCRIBER CONTENT: INTERVIEW WITH OIL INDUSTRY EXPERT TOM PETRIE
---
[|
---](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=e2e7dd4073&e=93b2c2b824)
| |
**APRIL 14, 2026**
On April 12th at 11am, Dr. Vikram Mansharamani spoke with oil industry veteran Tom Petrie about current Iran-driven geopolitics and oil markets. Tom has been an active student of commodity cycles and the energy industry for more than 50 years and is among the world’s most informed experts on oil dynamics. Tom is Chairman of Petrie Partners, former Vice Chairman of Bank of America Merrill Lynch, and a cofounder of Petrie Parkman & Co, an energy-focused investment bank that was sold to Merrill Lynch in 2006. Tom is a graduate of West Point, has a graduate degree from Boston University, and was awarded an honorary doctorate of engineering from The Colorado School of Mines. Topics covered in the conversation include:

* The similarities between today’s oil dynamics and the 1973 Yom Kippur War era when OPEC initially gained power.
* Tom’s advisory work for the Saudi government and how he thinks other Gulf states are thinking about today’s regional conflict.
* Trump’s recently announced intention to blockade the Strait of Hormuz and whether it’s a negotiating tactic
* The outlook for the price of oil, informed by past cycles and how $200 oil is possible but will almost certainly be self-defeating to those who drive it there.
* How domestic political pressures will constrain the Trump Administration’s ability to finance another “forever war” and tolerate higher energy prices.
* How US-China negotiations may be a contributing factor to America’s actions in both Iran and Venezuela.
* What Tom’s watching going forward

---

Corporate executives, government leaders (including several heads of state!), journalists, academics, and others eagerly read, reread, and look forward to his annual piece. If you reread his prior pieces, you’ll find some developments he noted come to fruition, and others simply did not happen. But as Dr. Mansharamani repeatedly notes, the goal of the predictions is not **accuracy** , but instead **usefulness** in provoking thoughts about possible futures. # NAVIGATING UNCERTAINTY
---
| | As many of you are aware, I’ve returned to writing my weekly newsletter and have been pleased by the rapidly growing interest in my thoughts. As I have in the past, I’m addressing a wide range of topics, ranging from geopolitics and economics to social trends to technology. The overall theme of my work is focused on “navigating uncertainty” and how a generalist approach can help us all make sense of what seems like chaos.
If you have not already done so, please consider subscribing!

# About Vikram
---
[|
---](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=baa43becf1&e=93b2c2b824)
| | **VIKRAM MANSHARAMANI** is an entrepreneur, consultant, scholar, neighbor, husband, father, volunteer, and professional generalist who thinks in multiple-dimensions and looks beyond the short-term. Self-taught to think around corners and connect original dots, he spends his time speaking with global leaders in business, government, academia, and journalism. **He’s currently the Chairman and CEO of Goodwell Foods, a manufacturer of private label frozen pizza**. _LinkedIn_ has twice listed him as its #1 Top Voice in Money & Finance, and _Worth_ profiled him as one of the 100 Most Powerful People in Global Finance. Vikram earned a PhD From MIT, has taught at Yale and Harvard, and is the author of three books, [ _**The Making of a Generalist: An Independent Thinker Finds Unconventional Success in an Uncertain World**_](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=17bea548bf&e=93b2c2b824) , [ _**Think for Yourself: Restoring Common Sense in an Age of Experts and Artificial Intelligence**_](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=350e3efae2&e=93b2c2b824) and [ _**Boombustology: Spotting Financial Bubbles Before They Burst**_](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=a19ece2e60&e=93b2c2b824). Vikram lives in Lincoln, New Hampshire with his wife and two children, where they can usually be found hiking or skiing.

See below for links to my April musings... Happy May! - Vikram | | APRIL 5, 2026 In July 1971, Henry Kissinger to Beijing that supposedly didn’t exist. After feigning illness, the National Security Advisor took a covert trip to China that laid the groundwork for Nixon’s later visit. All of this was possible due to Pakistan’s president efforts to create a secret backchannel—one that ultimately reshaped the Cold War as Kissinger managed to cleave Beijing away from Moscow. It may have been the most consequential piece of shuttle diplomacy of the 20th century...and it ran through a country that didn’t exist in 1946 and that most Americans couldn’t find on a map. And now, 55 years later, Pakistan appears poised to once again play a in global affairs. With the U.S. on the verge of another Forever War, a politically problematic development for the Trump Administration, the US President is eager to find an off ramp. Turns out America’s “frenemy” might be the only country in the world capable of throwing Washington a lifeline... | | | | APRIL 12, 2026 Like many of us, I have been thinking – – a lot about AI. I have been exploring its potential and the pitfalls, and have settled on an idea I call , that AI is both revolutionary and dangerous at the same time. (It’s a tip of the hat to , a famous thought experiment in quantum mechanics in which a cat is both alive and dead). Like the hypothetical cat, AI is currently both promising and terrifying. Human decisions (in the form of policies, accepted norms, or other guardrails), I have argued, will push the AI development trajectory into one scenario or another. A recent development has tipped the scales, however, and it’s something that should alarm everyone. Anthropic released a model last week called . It is, by every measure, the most capable AI system ever built. It can find and exploit software vulnerabilities that have evaded human experts for decades—including a that had, until last week, gone completely undetected. It scored 93.9% on SWE-bench – an evaluation site that tests Large Language Models and their ability to solve problems -** far exceeding the scores earned by every other model in existence... | | | | APRIL 19, 2026 Every conversation I have with business owners, investors, and fellow executives lately seems to begin the same way: prices keep going up, and there doesn’t seem to be any reason for the trend to stop. Oil, food, housing, and labor costs all appear to be on a one way path. At Goodwell Foods, the frozen pizza manufacturer I run in New Hampshire, I see it daily. Freight has skyrocketed on the back of higher fuel prices. Labor in rural New England remains stubbornly tight. Even whey prices, which we use in our high-protein offerings, seem to only go in one direction. In other words, inflation is real, self-reinforcing, and disruptive. For the next one-to-three years, it deserves to be prominent on boardroom agendas. Politicians are probably on-point to worry that “affordability” is a galvanizing, anti-incumbent driver that will impact the future elections... | | | | APRIL 26, 2026 I have a weakness for seemingly irrelevant indicators. Nearly fifteen years ago, . As luck would have, the world’s tallest skyscraper neatly captures overconfidenceand hubris , loose money conditions that encourages excess liquidity to find a home, and ubiquitous speculative tendencies as “build it and they will come” logic runs roughshod over more conservative thinking. Let’s revisit some skyscraper history. Weeks after the Burj Dubai was deemed the world’s tallest freestanding structure, the bursting of a credit bubble began which led to the Global Financial Crisis. Before the Internet bubble peaked, Taipei 101 (home of the hardware side of the tech boom) claimed the title of the world’s tallest tower, displacing the Petronas Towers, a position the Malaysian skyscrapers took in 1997, as the Asian Tigers imploded and spurred the Asian Financial Crisis. In the 1970s, the Willis Tower (aka the Sears Tower) redefined the Chicago skyline and stagflation followed. And perhaps the most iconic skyscraper to telegraph a financial crisis was the Empire State Building, which took the crown of the world’s tallest in 1931 as the Great Depression took hold. I love unconventional indicators because they capture something official data often miss...they capture real time human behavior while traditional information is usually backward-looking. Today I’d argue that pizza — specifically, frozen pizza — is telling us something equally important about the American consumer. And very few people seem to be listening... | | | | | EXCLUSIVE SUBSCRIBER CONTENT: INTERVIEW WITH JAMES LITINSKY OF MP MATERIALS | | APRIL 9, 2026 On March 9th, 2026, Dr. Vikram Mansharamani spoke with Jim Litinsky, Chairman and CEO of MP Materials, America’s leading rare earth and magnet company. Recorded at MP’s magnet plant in Fort Worth, Texas, topics covered in the conversation include: Jim’s background as an investor and his path to running a vertically integrated rare earth magnetics company. The critical role of human capital and the importance of identifying talent in times of uncertainty. How MP has competed against a geopolitical competitor (China) and secured funding to create an American champion. How MP, despite being a de facto startup, secured GM and Apple, as well as the Pentagon, as customers. Why developing resilience and staying power when dynamics are unfavorable is key to navigating uncertainty... | | | | | EXCLUSIVE SUBSCRIBER CONTENT: INTERVIEW WITH OIL INDUSTRY EXPERT TOM PETRIE | | APRIL 14, 2026 On April 12th at 11am, Dr. Vikram Mansharamani spoke with oil industry veteran Tom Petrie about current Iran-driven geopolitics and oil markets. Tom has been an active student of commodity cycles and the energy industry for more than 50 years and is among the world’s most informed experts on oil dynamics. Tom is Chairman of Petrie Partners, former Vice Chairman of Bank of America Merrill Lynch, and a cofounder of Petrie Parkman & Co, an energy-focused investment bank that was sold to Merrill Lynch in 2006. Tom is a graduate of West Point, has a graduate degree from Boston University, and was awarded an honorary doctorate of engineering from The Colorado School of Mines. Topics covered in the conversation include: The similarities between today’s oil dynamics and the 1973 Yom Kippur War era when OPEC initially gained power. Tom’s advisory work for the Saudi government and how he thinks other Gulf states are thinking about today’s regional conflict. Trump’s recently announced intention to blockade the Strait of Hormuz and whether it’s a negotiating tactic The outlook for the price of oil, informed by past cycles and how $200 oil is possible but will almost certainly be self-defeating to those who drive it there. How domestic political pressures will constrain the Trump Administration’s ability to finance another “forever war” and tolerate higher energy prices. How US-China negotiations may be a contributing factor to America’s actions in both Iran and Venezuela. What Tom’s watching going forward | | | | | | 2026-2031 Predictions | | | | Read Vikram’s Prior Predictions! | | Since 2015, Dr. Mansharamani has publicly posted a set of global developments to watch over the following five years. Corporate executives, government leaders (including several heads of state!), journalists, academics, and others eagerly read, reread, and look forward to his annual piece. If you reread his prior pieces, you’ll find some developments he noted come to fruition, and others simply did not happen. But as Dr. Mansharamani repeatedly notes, the goal of the predictions is not accuracy , but instead usefulness in provoking thoughts about possible futures. Click on the link below to review his posts from 2015 through 2025. | | | | NAVIGATING UNCERTAINTY | | As many of you are aware, I’ve returned to writing my weekly newsletter and have been pleased by the rapidly growing interest in my thoughts. As I have in the past, I’m addressing a wide range of topics, ranging from geopolitics and economics to social trends to technology. The overall theme of my work is focused on “navigating uncertainty” and how a generalist approach can help us all make sense of what seems like chaos. If you have not already done so, please consider subscribing! | | | | Want Vikram to speak at your event? | | Have an upcoming board meeting, corporate offsite, conference, or other event in which you might want to have Vikram share his views about the global economy, geopolitical dynamics, technological developments, or social pressures? Click below to inquire about his availability! | mailto:vikram@mansharamani.com?subject=SPEAKING%20INQUIRY&body= | | About Vikram | | VIKRAM MANSHARAMANI is an entrepreneur, consultant, scholar, neighbor, husband, father, volunteer, and professional generalist who thinks in multiple-dimensions and looks beyond the short-term. Self-taught to think around corners and connect original dots, he spends his time speaking with global leaders in business, government, academia, and journalism. He’s currently the Chairman and CEO of Goodwell Foods, a manufacturer of private label frozen pizza**. LinkedIn has twice listed him as its #1 Top Voice in Money & Finance, and Worth profiled him as one of the 100 Most Powerful People in Global Finance. Vikram earned a PhD From MIT, has taught at Yale and Harvard, and is the author of three books, , and . Vikram lives in Lincoln, New Hampshire with his wife and two children, where they can usually be found hiking or skiing. | | | | | mailto:vikram@mansharamani.com | | | | | | | | | Copyright (C) 2026 Vikram for New Hampshire. All rights reserved.

| | | Dear Friend,

Today is the deadline.

At midnight tonight, our May 1st fundraising deadline closes, and we’re still working to reach our goal. If you’ve been considering supporting our campaign, now is the moment to act:

[DONATE NOW](https://us.list-manage.com/2aBWmXxWKBj?e=c0babbc643&c2id=bfcaacdd1ef4033edfed3d4b1d472a02)

This deadline is critical to ensuring we have the resources to communicate our message and reach voters before the election.

As a proud fourth-generation San Mateo County resident with over 20 years of public service, I’m running for Assessor-County Clerk-Recorder and Chief Elections Officer to modernize this office and restore confidence in how it serves our community. A prior review made it clear that stronger leadership, greater transparency, and improved trust are needed, and I’m ready to deliver.

As a former Mayor and current member of the Board of Supervisors, I oversee a $5.5 billion organization with 5,800 employees and strong financial reserves. I understand that accountability, sound management, and collaboration are essential to delivering reliable public services.

As your Chief Elections Officer, I will ensure elections are secure, efficient, and accessible to all eligible voters, while strengthening transparency and public trust.

We’re in the final hours, and every contribution today makes a real difference in helping us reach more voters before Election Day.
Please don’t wait, contribute before midnight tonight:

name: Katie Porter
subject: I don’t change my values when powerful people pressure me
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Katie Porter for Governor 2026
body:
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21486?t=1&akid=41258%2E3422442%2EJR_CWT)
Peter, here’s something Californians are tired of: **Politicians who promise one thing in public—and another thing behind closed doors.**
For years my opponent, Xavier Becerra, said he supported single-payer health care. _He was still posting about it just weeks ago._
But now that he needs support from powerful health care corporations and insiders to win this race? Suddenly, he’s “not supportive” anymore.
Working families don’t get to “backpedal” when groceries cost too much. Parents don’t get to “pivot” when child care costs more than rent. Californians rationing insulin don’t get to tell the pharmacy they’ve decided health care isn’t a priority right now.
**Too many politicians decide their principles are negotiable the minute powerful interests lean on them.**
_Not me._
I’ve spent my career holding powerful people accountable—Big Oil executives, Wall Street banks, giant corporations, and the politicians who protect them.
I’m fighting for single-payer health care like Medicare for All because health care is a right.
I’m fighting for free child care because parents shouldn’t choose between their kids and their careers.
I’m fighting for tuition-free public college because students shouldn’t start adulthood buried in debt.
And unlike some people in this race, I’m not selling out my values because it’s what lobbyists or wealthy donors want.
I’m fighting for working Californians. _Period._
**[If you want a governor who will stand up to powerful interests instead of folding under pressure, I’m asking you to help power this campaign with a $5 contribution today.](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20260501_3_ak.NxAXrA&refcode=em20260501-41258&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=41258%2E3422442%2EJR_CWT)**
[Donate ›››](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20260501_3_ak.NxAXrA&refcode=em20260501-41258&t=22&akid=41258%2E3422442%2EJR_CWT)
Thank you,
Katie

Katie is running to be California’s next governor because California and our country need a leader who will stand up to Trump to lower costs, protect our rights, and combat climate change. **[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4g-em-footer?refcode=em20260501-41258&amounts=&t=23&refcode2=41258_3422442_JR_CWT&akid=41258%2E3422442%2EJR_CWT)**

name: Sarah McBride
subject: Every worker deserves a living wage and basic protections
disclaimer_text: Paid for by McBride for Delaware
body:
Delaware sent me to Congress to fight for working families, and that’s exactly what I’ve been doing.
# [](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/go/1269?t=1&akid=3202%2E21716%2E3_7K9Y)
It’s May Day, a day to lift up workers and stand in solidarity with those bravely fighting to unionize their workplaces.
Unfortunately, Donald Trump and Republicans in Congress are using their power to do the opposite. They’ve consistently put the interests of their billionaire campaign donors first.
**But Delaware sent me to Congress to fight for working families, and that’s exactly what I’ve been doing.**
I’m proud to be an original cosponsor of the _Protecting the Right to Organize (PRO) Act_ , which expands and strengthens protections for workers’ right to organize a union and bargain for higher wages, better benefits, and safer working conditions.
I’ve also fought to pass paid family and medical leave, and stood with the educators union against Trump’s cuts to the Department of Education.
Regardless of what Trump and Republicans in Congress do, I’m committed to keeping up this fight. Because I know that every worker deserves a living wage, basic protections, good benefits, and the support necessary to thrive.
[**But I need you on my team too. If you’re with me this May Day, can you add your name to show your support for pro-worker legislation like the PRO Act?**](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/signup/20260501-support-the-pro-act-petition/?source=em20260501-3202&t=2&akid=3202%2E21716%2E3_7K9Y)
**[Add Your Name »](https://act.sarahmcbride.com/signup/20260501-support-the-pro-act-petition/?source=em20260501-3202&t=3&akid=3202%2E21716%2E3_7K9Y)**
In solidarity,
Sarah McBride

name: The Permanent Campaign
subject: Dems Furious With Schumer, Redistricting Battle Heats Up
body:
Trump hits EU autos with 25% tariffs as SCOTUS rewrites the redistricting map.

|
---
|
---
Sponsored By:
[](https://email.alert.republicantaskforce.com/c/eJyc0Mtq6zAUheGnkWcx2luO5Aw0OCT4QGlKKYFAJ0LXWFS-IMn165f0ETr_WAv--1VddUxxfrzpyctcg3KLU85PJWw5LlvZYx2LHbfJ5-ydQor8QI8HCmpOChonnXUabOMlCCH4CTrGm1HyU8-Dgd4YGzRjTHMfEEMnoAdkJjRRPqfokSJlQFnXBmGtDUHznsIJqSMd1cnn2ma_biZFq-eqy1dYsvWtXaYmybHWtRD2j-BAcNj3vTV-rqvPdnwKggP-R7x_nm9ngsPL6wfApX9iNpTNAGGX2_u5ydKte0wpFi5IRx-Tjun3oP6hx7fEnwAAAP__vjltSA)
The Permanent Campaign is brought to you today in partnership with
Republican Task Force Issue: 5/1/2026
Trump Raises EU Auto Tariffs

President Trump [announced Friday](https://email.alert.republicantaskforce.com/c/eJyckLHO3CAQhJ8Gd5xgbeNzQZHG3Z82pbWG5Yx-bKwFYuXto7smfbopPo1mvl9f6xfGFM_XTzzIcg2rz371dJTQOOZW7lj34vZ2EDP5FRQYqUap9HqmVXfeeudRu46snqbJzHroTbfbod8MzAD0BANebTRrNIObcKDnOJPron1XqVGB6rXqh0eYnHMhoHkqPYPyYlCYiOuD6Wpbig7PiuU7ZHb0cPnokt1rvYrofwhYBCz3fT-2lPOxEb_ehIDlpLsIWJBrdIne8d8BAUvldlyy4J8iW5E1S8ZYSFbkGIJkrCTzKalJh1xk5ea-PxiMHVt_3TGlWMwkBvU6MKbPrPofFn9b-BsAAP__sfuCHA) he will raise tariffs on European Union cars and trucks to 25%, effective next week. Trump posted to social media accusing the EU of failing to comply with an existing trade agreement, escalating a transatlantic standoff that has roiled global markets for months. The announcement did not specify the [legal authority](https://email.alert.republicantaskforce.com/c/eJyc0DtuxiAQBODT4M7WAn4WFGnc_WlTovUCv1HAtnjE14-cI6SZZqRvpPl66Rf64I_3J0arUnHanEYbG7OryZ81377smfYabUrWaAFibGFogesjaN4YZcggp8YqPk3TuPBejs2uzLINbgbZD0gzoYTBzVJKSWYGu1DfePVQMIAAyUH2nZuIyDkcZ-CLAMN6wGBT6ZK96hY84VEwf7szke3ojE1QeylXZvKDiZWJ9b7vjo6NnpKJ9eGZWGF4gjOxllTj1draYi1nWzB553K3lxiapMx1-xB8HifWwzuiD38b5R-X_CjxGwAA__8uAG97) Trump would invoke to impose the new rate. The existing tariff on EU-made vehicles stood at 2.5% before Trump's presidency; his first term raised it, and this move would push rates to levels not seen in modern trade history. European officials immediately condemned the decision and warned of retaliatory measures. The tariff escalation comes as Trump has also [renewed threats](https://email.alert.republicantaskforce.com/c/eJyckLtu9SAQhJ8Gd1iwvhcUf-Pu_G1KC8NyjIIv2oVYefvoJEX61KNvNPO9PZaHjSkez_92R0M5LP70i8edQ6F4Fr5j3thtZUci9Aso6KXqpNLLkRZdeeOdt9pVaPQwDP2k26avNjOOk0cA12lAtyrdrKHpVA928t20-rGK5lWlOgWq0app6zA450Kw_aj0BMqLVtmElGvCq6wpOntky-_hJIe1O_cqmS3ni0XzT8AsYL7vuyYsGYlfuYB5LRwPZBYw25JPlpnswddJ2eZ4HgLmTGW_JNtPlhvKO6YkyUZGmS3FEFhikT8odPL3u4C5IuOvFxC5H0SrnruN6XtX_oPGDwNfAQAA__9zLoNx) to pull U.S. troops from NATO, adding military pressure to an already strained alliance. EU leaders are expected to convene an emergency session to coordinate a response. ★★★
Republicans Announce Redistricting Plan

The Supreme Court's six-justice conservative majority ruled in [Louisiana v. Callais](https://email.alert.republicantaskforce.com/c/eJyc0L1uxCAQBOCnwR3Wsubsu4IijbtLm9LiYLFR8I8WOOfxI-cR0o9mRt_Xc3ramOI2f9qVDJcw-d1PntYcKse95jOWJbulrsRMfkLAXsJNgpq2NKnGG--8Va4ho4Zh6B9Kd32zGK3poV_gOgQd3B2CdsHbm9ao1L0bQhPNVQU3QOgUdLoNg3MuBNvfQT0QvNBgE3FpmY76StHZrdj8HXZ21Lp9bZJZSjmy6D4EjgLH8zzbstBcLftotysjcKxZbnRmgeO1J3C0BwscOxA45nowrSTdXrnI917iNkuO81Ky5HqpSPo5ko0bccPGH2dMKeZ-EBrm1cb096P8g-1t8DcAAP__m_h98g) that race-based redistricting is unconstitutional, effectively gutting Section 2 of the Voting Rights Act. The 6-3 decision bars federal courts from ordering states to draw majority-minority congressional districts. [Republicans are pursuing](https://email.alert.republicantaskforce.com/c/eJyckDGPpSAURn-NdhgEhWdBsY3d23ZLg5erkkUwF3zM_PvJm2rq6b7kS05Ozr_n8rQ--Lj_tScaKtviklscnnm7yac7V1-ODMd9IhG6RXChGB8Z75cYlr51xoGzPbRoeq21mvpBqvYwUslBqYeaVpz0KtZBS9BSTyBB6PGxtt68UXzkgsuey6HbNABsm1UP3k-Cu2bgNiCVjvC61-DBxmLz_y0RYAfpbIM5SrlyI_80Ym7EXGvt4goRa37_jZivFHzxkBsxQ4o7Yf45GX5cCAUdw-gYISRyTI_M2U92WSreBranF1I8MRaWj7u4VCMjiFYOYuKyJeOu6kPwWelm4Ptpffh2K79I-TLiKwAA__83aIXZ) ICE and Border Patrol resources through a separate budget reconciliation bill, where they can move the spending with a simple majority and without Democratic cooperation. The bipartisan measure passed the House on Thursday with a voice vote. The shutdown began in February after Democrats blocked ICE funding, and became the longest partial agency shutdown in U.S. history.

clean_body: Trump hits EU autos with 25% tariffs as SCOTUS rewrites the redistricting map. | | Sponsored By: The Permanent Campaign is brought to you today in partnership with Republican Task Force Issue: 5/1/2026 Trump Raises EU Auto Tariffs President Trump he will raise tariffs on European Union cars and trucks to 25%, effective next week. Trump posted to social media accusing the EU of failing to comply with an existing trade agreement, escalating a transatlantic standoff that has roiled global markets for months. The announcement did not specify the Trump would invoke to impose the new rate. The existing tariff on EU-made vehicles stood at 2.5% before Trump's presidency; his first term raised it, and this move would push rates to levels not seen in modern trade history. European officials immediately condemned the decision and warned of retaliatory measures. The tariff escalation comes as Trump has also to pull U.S. troops from NATO, adding military pressure to an already strained alliance. EU leaders are expected to convene an emergency session to coordinate a response. ★★★ Republicans Announce Redistricting Plan The Supreme Court's six-justice conservative majority ruled in that race-based redistricting is unconstitutional, effectively gutting Section 2 of the Voting Rights Act. The 6-3 decision bars federal courts from ordering states to draw majority-minority congressional districts. Alabama Republican Governor Kay Ivy announced she would call a to draw a new congressional district map expected to net the GOP at least one seat. Georgia's Governor, , said his state won't enact a new map for 2026, but will draw new district lines for 2028. And that should benefit the GOP. ★★★ 76-Day DHS Shutdown Ends Trump signed legislation Thursday of the Department of Homeland Security — a 76-day standoff triggered when Democrats refused to fund ICE operations. The bill restores funding for the TSA, Secret Service, and Coast Guard through September, and includes a 45-day FISA extension. ICE is deliberately excluded from the new funding package. ICE and Border Patrol resources through a separate budget reconciliation bill, where they can move the spending with a simple majority and without Democratic cooperation. The bipartisan measure passed the House on Thursday with a voice vote. The deal ends weeks of and furloughs for tens of thousands of DHS employees, as well as disruptions to airport screening and border port operations. The shutdown began in February after Democrats blocked ICE funding, and became the longest partial agency shutdown in U.S. history. Mills Out, Collins Vulnerable Maine Gov. Janet Mills suspended her Senate campaign Thursday, citing financial constraints, all but handing the Democratic nomination to oyster farmer Graham Platner. Democrats are for wasting resources by recruiting Mills to run a doomed campaign. ★★★ Trump: Kill the Filibuster Now Trump demanded Senate Republicans nuke the filibuster Thursday to pass the SAVE America Act — a voter ID bill requiring citizenship proof — warning holdouts not to be Sen. Thune and others are resisting, leaving the path forward uncertain. ★★★ May Primaries Kick Off The 2026 primary season opens in earnest today as voters across multiple states head to the polls. Open seats from a wave of incumbent retirements are reshaping the map ahead of November's midterm battles for House and Senate control. All eyes are on the , where incumbent John Cornyn faces State Attorney General Ken Paxton. DHS Bill Signed, ICE Still Unfunded Trump on Thursday, ending a 76-day shutdown for agencies including TSA and FEMA. ICE and Border Patrol remain unfunded -- Republicans plan to deliver their $70 billion through a filibuster-proof reconciliation package. ★★★ Farm Bill Clears House The House passed the , 224-200, on Thursday. The bill now heads to the Senate, where Republicans need Democratic votes to advance it -- and the path forward remains uncertain. ★★★ FISA Spying Extended Again Congress passed a on Thursday -- no reforms attached. The House voted 261-111; the Senate approved unanimously. Reform advocates on both sides get nothing; the intelligence community gets everything. Today's section is brought to you by: Reveal smoother, brighter skin in minutes with the. A powerful at-home dermaplaning device that removes peach fuzz and exfoliates dead skin using gentle sonic technology. With customizable speeds and a safe design, it delivers spa-like results, helping your makeup glide on flawlessly and skincare absorb better. () 1. Trump Hits Second-Term Approval Low — (Apr. 24–27, n=1,629, MoE ±2.9 pts) clocks Trump at 34% approval — a second-term low, down 2 points from mid-April. The economy and the Iran war are the main drivers. 2. Pew: Immigration Views Souring — (Apr. 20–26, n=5,103) finds Americans' views on Trump's immigration policy and use of military force have grown more negative. His overall approval sits at 34%, a second-term low. 3. Generic Ballot: Dems Lead by 6 — rounds up current polling averages showing Democrats with a roughly 6-point national lead on the generic congressional ballot, flipped from Republican advantages at the start of the term. 4. Maine: Platner Leads Collins by 4 — The sizes up the Maine Senate matchup: Platner 47%, Collins 43% in the general. Striking for a five-term incumbent with decades of brand equity in the state. 1. EU Learned Nothing From Maduro Trump , and Brussels is playing the victim. The EU signed a deal and didn't comply — that's not a negotiation, it's a breach. The terms were clear. 2. SCOTUS Did What It Had To The left is screaming "Jim Crow 2.0." What the Court actually did was toss . Race can't be the dominant factor in drawing lines. That's a strategy problem — not a constitutional crisis. 3. Thune Is the Problem Trump asked nicely — then loudly. Thune to pass voter ID legislation backed by 95% of Republican voters. Six months out from the midterms, Senate leadership is protecting the procedure, not the party. 4. An Oyster Farmer Could Beat Collins Susan Collins has held her Maine seat for 30 years. show Graham Platner — an oyster farmer with zero political experience — up 4. If Republicans lose this seat because of how they've governed, everyone owns it. 5. 76 Days and a Bill That Funds Half After a , Congress passed a bill funding TSA and FEMA — but not ICE. Then took a bow. The agency running Trump's signature policy still runs on fumes. A majority should govern better than this. "The Dems will do it on the first hour of their first day. name: Danielle Bethell for Governor
subject: Another debate. More people watching.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Danielle Bethell PAC 20220 [Preferences](https://daniellebethell
body:
Monday, May 4 at 7:00pm, hosted by KOIN 6 and the Portland City Club.
[](https://daniellebethell.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akliuud-hkijuteih-r/) Friends,We have another debate coming up Monday, May 4, at 7:00 pm, hosted by KOIN 6 and the Portland City Club.Before I get into it, I'm asking for your help.We're in the final weeks of this race, and if we're going to reach the voters we need before May 19, we have to expand our reach right now.[If you're able, please make a contribution today.](https://daniellebethell.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akliuud-hkijuteih-y/) [](https://daniellebethell.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akliuud-hkijuteih-j/) This next debate matters.After the last one, something shifted. Once voters saw the candidates side by side, answering the same questions, the contrast became clear.There's a difference between talking about these issues and actually doing the work, managing systems, making decisions, and being accountable for results. That's what I've been doing as a county commissioner, and it shows when people get a chance to see it directly.The challenge now is making sure enough voters see it.Some of the other candidates in this race have run statewide before. They have built-in name ID. We don't have that advantage, but what we do have is a message that connects when people hear it. [Can You Chip In Now?](https://daniellebethell.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akliuud-hkijuteih-t/) That means this final stretch is about one thing: getting in front of as many voters as possible while they're making their decision.That's what your support helps us do.If you've already given, thank you. If you can give again in these final weeks, it goes directly toward reaching the voters who will decide this race.You can watch the debate live Monday night, and I hope you do.Danielle Bethell
Republican for Governor**P.S.**[We're close, but we need to keep building. If you can, please chip in here.](https://daniellebethell.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akliuud-hkijuteih-i/) [](https://daniellebethell.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akliuud-hkijuteih-d/)[](https://daniellebethell.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akliuud-hkijuteih-h/)[](https://daniellebethell.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akliuud-hkijuteih-k/)[](https://daniellebethell.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akliuud-hkijuteih-x/) | [ Share ](https://daniellebethell.cmail20.com/t/y-fb-akliuud-hkijuteih-m/) | [ Tweet ](https://daniellebethell.cmail20.com/t/y-tw-akliuud-hkijuteih-c/) | [ Share ](https://daniellebethell.cmail20.com/t/y-li-akliuud-hkijuteih-q/) | [ Forward ](https://daniellebethell.forwardtomyfriend.com/y-hkijuteih-EBDF613C-akliuud-l-a)

name: Tom Fitton
subject: Supreme Court Victory!
body:
Victory in J6 Bodycam Case

[INSIDE JW]

SUPREME COURT BLOCKS RACE-BASED GERRYMANDERING

[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81221&L=37720&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly95b3V0dS5iZS92aVNJN2lzWVhWUT91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=ee6e77c394d2503fc82b6666866a55d06374612692d5a66673a84be3828c177e]

Big news out of the courts this week—U.S. Supreme Court has issued a
major 6-3 decision
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81221&L=37701&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuc3VwcmVtZWNvdXJ0Lmdvdi9vcGluaW9ucy8yNXBkZi8yNC0xMDlfMjFvMy5wZGY/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=090626b2abf00702717425bd6e246063208ce7afeb95462f2d1cfdf97d8c801d] that
could reshape how congressional districts are drawn across the
country.

This is a great decision for democracy. It affirms that the U.S.
Constitution requires the government to be colorblind in its decision
making. The decision effectively ends the egregious racial segregation
of voters. We are prepared to enforce this decision and end any racial
gerrymandering that treats voters as cogs in a racial spoils system.

Most Americans would agree with the court’s conclusion that
“allowing race to play any part in government decision-making
represents a departure from the constitutional rule that applies in
almost every other context.”

In January 2025, Judicial Watch and AEF filed an initial _amici_ brief
(friends of the court) in this case, asking the court
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81221&L=34483&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZWxpbWluYXRlLXdva2UtY29uZ3Jlc3Npb25hbC1kaXN0cmljdHMvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=a529c23c9ec5a9f2db11fb72e3b54c9653c62bbeda5bce474331a3fb2d8c11a8]
to affirm a lower court ruling that Louisiana violated the
constitution when it crowded minority voters into congressional
districts. Judicial Watch and AEF filed a supplemental _amici curiae_
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81221&L=36163&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvcmFjZS1iYXNlZC1jb25ncmVzc2lvbmFsLWRpc3RyaWN0cy8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=a9264f7f310eef371bc62e5f2d92da7bfa17c065729933ae75fc542b82d74e69]
brief in September 2025.

We argued:

> This Court has compared race-based districting to segregation of
> “public parks, ... buses, ... and schools,” and warned that we
> “should not be carving electorates into racial blocs.” ... That
> is because “[c]lassifications of citizens solely on the basis of
> race ‘are by their very nature odious to a free people whose
> institutions are founded upon the doctrine of equality.’” ...
> Racial gerrymandering, like all “[r]acial classifications of any
> sort” cause “lasting harm to our society” because “[t]hey
> reinforce the belief, held by too many for too much of our history,
> that individuals should be judged by the color of their skin.” ...

> There should be no question that race-based division of citizens for
> purposes of compliance with § 2 and _Gingles_ is a violation of the
> Equal Protection Clause, the “central purpose” of which “is to
> prevent the States from purposefully discriminating between
> individuals on the basis of race.”... The same may be said of the
> Voting Rights Act.

In his concurring opinion, Justice Thomas agreed, denouncing the
practice where:

> “Blacks [were] drawn into ‘black districts’ and given ‘black
> representatives’; Hispanics [were] drawn into Hispanic districts
> and given ‘Hispanic representatives’; and so on.” . . . That
> interpretation is “repugnant to any nation that strives for the
> ideal of a color-blind Constitution.” . . . Today’s decision
> should largely put an end to this “disastrous misadventure” in
> voting-rights jurisprudence.

AEF is a charitable and educational foundation dedicated to improving
the quality of life through education. In furtherance of that goal,
the Foundation has engaged in a number of projects, which include, but
are not limited to, educational and health conferences domestically
and abroad. AEF has partnered frequently with Judicial Watch to fight
government and judicial corruption and to promote a return to ethics
and morality in the nation’s public life.

We are a national leader in election integrity and voting rights
litigation, with a record of successful lawsuits
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81221&L=35695&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvdGFnL2VsZWN0aW9uK2xhdy8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=9397a9e3d745abe0c3f21cda7e7ee80355607c49f190c9e5632b403f671aff4b] enforcing
constitutional redistricting standards and cleaning voter rolls
nationwide.

We pointed out

COURT VICTORY: D.C. BLOCKED FROM HIDING JANUARY 6 BODYCAM FOOTAGE

In a major victory for Judicial Watch and the rule of law, a
Washington, D.C. court ruled
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81221&L=37721&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtZGMtYm9keWNhbXMtcnVsaW5nLTAwMzQ1My8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=1e0c97a29243f49e63f560542880786ea507afa08db7bda59f7ebc07fce906bc]
that Washington D.C.’s Metropolitan Police Department (MPD) cannot
broadly blur and censor body-worn camera (BWC) footage from January 6,
2021.

The MPD had previously claimed
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81221&L=34692&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZGMtcG9saWNlLXdhbnQtb3Zlci0xbS8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=e6d4a5cb8285700e7afac2822cf660c4df01b57d2e0e238ce435f527061e0c85] that such
redactions were necessary to protect personal privacy and asserted
that complying with the request would cost more than $1.5 million due
to the volume of footage, which reportedly exceeds one thousand hours.

The ruling follows a January 8, 2026, hearing
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81221&L=36807&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZGMtcG9saWNlLWJvZHljYW0tZm9vdGFnZS1qNi8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=ea6534b3a5b8566a0ca522fa7fb03fd754743bdaf9bf5c54102f52d1bdd02b35] before
Judge Veronica Sanchez in our Freedom of Information Act (FOIA)
lawsuit [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81221&L=32956&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZGMtcG9saWNlLWJvZHljYW0tdmlkZW9zLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=e69c615d7e59ff03b61359b65794da52671752484aa2822a739628a6041553ee] for
all bodycam footage recorded by Metropolitan Police officers who
responded to the U.S. Capitol on January 6 (_Judicial Watch v.
District of Columbia_
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81221&L=32934&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtZGMtYm9keWNhbS1mb290YWdlLWNvbXBsYWludC0wMDM0NTMvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=c93a09ccf2cc4f03f7c391db4f7acf6f506c690b32486c8845b608e1102b79d8]
(No. 2024-CAB-003453)).

In its order
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81221&L=37721&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtZGMtYm9keWNhbXMtcnVsaW5nLTAwMzQ1My8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=1e0c97a29243f49e63f560542880786ea507afa08db7bda59f7ebc07fce906bc],
the court rejected the District’s argument that it could justify
redacting, under D.C. FOIA exemptions, the faces and voices of all
non-law enforcement individuals captured in the footage.

The court found that while individuals appearing in the footage may
have a limited privacy interest, that interest is minimal and does not
outweigh the strong public interest in disclosure. The court
emphasized that the events of January 6 occurred largely in public
settings, were widely recorded, and remain a matter of significant
national concern.

Concerning the individuals’ privacy interests, the court explained
that the individuals present could not reasonably expect that their
appearances would remain private:

> The circumstances leading up to the events at the U.S. Capitol
> Complex included a large, public, televised event on the National
> Mall. Individuals were gathered in a public arena. The crowd then
> marched to the U.S. Capitol Complex where members of congress were
> partaking in the certification of the Presidential election results
> live on television.

***

> The individuals on the [body-worn cameras] could not “reasonably
> expect that their appearances on the [BWC] would remain private.”
> ... A person’s privacy interest is greater in the release of
> images and video footage that were taken without their “knowing
> consent.” ... Even though the individuals recorded on the subject
> BWCs may not have consented to their images being recorded, they
> certainly had reason to believe that the many law enforcement
> officers, as well as the surveillance cameras at the government
> owned property, were recording them. Additionally, during the events
> of January 6, simultaneous media images from both inside the U.S.
> Capitol and around the U.S. Capitol Complex were being broadcast,
> streamed, and uploaded. While the individuals may have not all
> “knowingly consented” to the images being recorded, they had
> reason to know they were.

Concerning the public interest, the court stated:

> The events of January 6, 2021, have been the topic of much debate,
> discussion, and scrutiny in the public consciousness by not just the
> residents of the District of Columbia but also the nation. BWC
> footage of the events at the Capitol on January 6 could
> “contribute significantly” to the public’s understanding of
> the events on that day and the MPD’s actions.

The court concluded, “In balancing the privacy interests which are
little more than _de minimis_ with the public interest, the public
interest outweighs the privacy interests. ... [T]he faces and voices
of individuals captured by MPD’s BWCs on January 6, 2021, are not
exempt from disclosure.”

This a major victory for transparency and the rule of law. As the
court found, the American people have a right to see this secret D.C.
police January 6 footage. We will now push for the immediate release
of the videos.

We have extensively investigated the events of January 6.

In April 2026, we filed a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) lawsuit
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81221&L=35132&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvaXJzLXRhcmdldGluZy1vZi1qYW51YXJ5LTYtcHJvdGVzdGVycy8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=1e5d731413c9251f919ba5045b6c5d1bc3fcb26d009e73666e68e01463f117bd]
against the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) to obtain records related
to possible improper targeting of January 6, 2021, Capitol protesters,
their supporters, and related nonprofits

In August 2025, we announced that the U.S. Air Force would finally
provide full military funeral honors
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81221&L=35989&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZnVuZXJhbC1ob25vcnMtYXNobGktYmFiYml0dC8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=238d6da3fc522617b83f0f5ba9a1a2f959fb37351b5cd24d9e71224ef4f9a75c] to Ashli

In July 2025, we sued

In March 2024, we received Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and
