OREGON SETTLEMENT TARGETS 800,000 INELIGIBLE VOTER REGISTRATIONS

Election victory in Oregon! We have a settlement
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81221&L=37690&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtb3JlZ29uLW52cmEtc2V0dGxlbWVudC0wMTc4My8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=0db90bf28be67984b82a982c9cd4bed6a8cd5ecc26297790a85efb1585def35f]
in our federal lawsuit against Oregon election officials, which
confirms 800,000
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81221&L=37691&F=T&&hash=a207e05c22492d0f69d0ea746d1753c1c73c3c59e45b105c1e3ae1d20219197f]
ineligible voter names are slated for review and removal from voter
registration lists. The settlement requires state officials to produce
detailed data and enforce federal voter roll clean-up procedures under
the National Voter Registration Act of 1993 (NVRA).

We filed the lawsuit
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81221&L=33760&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvb3JlZ29uLWNsZWFuLXVwLXZvdGVyLXJvbGxzLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=39b27e83ca79bb9fe9d5ce44e69256c7fe13213ae7767178329c679176397cf3] in
October 2024, alleging Oregon failed to remove ineligible voters and
seeking to enforce Section 8 of the NVRA after identifying
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81221&L=33275&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvb3JlZ29uLXRvLWNsZWFuLXZvdGVyLXJlZ2lzdHJhdGlvbi8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=4b43ca6f53ac732e6ac912193dbd2df24c9aaa2819573a121c552a382a40df3e]
widespread voter roll maintenance failures across dozens of counties
(_Judicial Watch, et al. v. The State of Oregon et al._
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81221&L=33744&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL29yLW52cmEtY29tcGxhaW50LTAxNzgzLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=3bcbb1a7c80c081e8328230be286e16ae73fc909d5cf59db4101e40b2f3923cd]
(No. 6:24-cv-01783)).

In our complaint, we argued that Oregon’s voter rolls contain large
numbers of old, inactive registrations; and that 29 of Oregon’s 36
counties removed few or no registrations as required by federal
election law. We asserted that Oregon and 35 of its counties had
overall registration rates exceeding 100%; and that Oregon had the
highest known inactive registration rate of any state in the nation.
In combination, all of these facts showed that Oregon was failing to
remove inactive registrations as required by federal law.

In August 2025, a federal court in Oregon denied a motion to dismiss
by Oregon and ruled
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81221&L=35821&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtb3JlZ29uLW52cmEtZGVjaXNpb24tMDE3ODMvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=0faf82ffee65be2a49c7e358fa92494c834c0776327474f8185ae1789940cd18]
the lawsuit could proceed.

In response to the lawsuit, Oregon Secretary of State Tobias Read
announced
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81221&L=37692&F=T&&hash=f470428cdeb0649f0a6666f7c00f058b68901e4cb278c4759352fa6c8c00d5ea]
earlier this year that Oregon has about 800,000 inactive
registrations, which are kept separately from the active voter rolls
and do not receive ballots. Of those, roughly 160,000 already meet
federal and state criteria for removal—having received confirmation
notices, failed to respond, and not voted in two federal
elections—and are slated for cancellation. The remaining
approximately 640,000 inactive records do not yet qualify for removal
and will be processed through future list maintenance efforts.

In its press release, Oregon acknowledged that routine removal of
outdated records effectively stalled in 2017, leaving a large pool of
long-dormant registrations on the rolls without being fully processed
for removal. The scale of the backlog underscores a gap in routine
list maintenance that is only now being addressed. “These directives
are about cleaning up old data that’s no longer in use so Oregonians
can be confident that our voter records are up to date,” said Read.

This is another historic election integrity success. Our lawsuit
caused Oregon to finally cleanup 800,000 outdated voter names, adding
to the more than six million ineligible voters removed by Judicial
Watch lawsuits and legal action nationwide. Dirty voter rolls can mean
dirty elections. Oregon’s Secretary of State, Tobias Read, is to be
commended for responding to our lawsuit with a massive voter roll
clean-up and commitment to continued voter list maintenance, which
will only increase voter confidence.”

Our lawsuits and legal actions have caused the removal
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81221&L=37547&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvaW5hY3RpdmUtdm90ZXJzLXJlbW92ZWQtY29sb3JhZG8tdm90ZXItcm9sbHMvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=bb1fa1fddf4eb8f9ed8e979f56a60d8cc8c60777d1282c1f5d4fc059cbceac3b]
of six million ineligible names from voter lists nationwide.

Under the National Voter Registration Act of 1993 (NVRA
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81221&L=4368&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuY29uZ3Jlc3MuZ292L2JpbGwvMTAzcmQtY29uZ3Jlc3MvaG91c2UtYmlsbC8yP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=0923439d0da9956dc97059c7401429fd89f8965482b4ab1687b84695fc861158]), states
must take reasonable steps to remove ineligible voters—such as those
who have died, moved, or become otherwise inactive.

The settlement
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81221&L=37690&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtb3JlZ29uLW52cmEtc2V0dGxlbWVudC0wMTc4My8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=0db90bf28be67984b82a982c9cd4bed6a8cd5ecc26297790a85efb1585def35f]
with Oregon remains in effect for more than five years, with a federal
court retaining jurisdiction to enforce its terms. While the
settlement resolves the litigation, it explicitly allows future legal
action if Oregon fails to comply with voter list clean-up requirements
going forward.

The settlement requires Oregon to open its voter roll maintenance
processes to unprecedented scrutiny. State officials must now
regularly provide detailed, county-level data on voter registrations,
removals, confirmation notices, and inactive voters—including those
eligible for removal under federal law. This includes data reported to
the Election Assistance Commission, as well as additional datasets
that will allow ongoing monitoring of compliance. The agreement
ensures that this information will not be hidden behind bureaucratic
barriers, requiring timely disclosure and identification of data
sources.

Oregon will also provide annual reports on inactive voters and those
eligible for removal under federal law, as well as provide free access
to the state’s voter registration list upon request to Judicial
Watch.

Oregon committed to implementing Read’s new list maintenance
directives
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81221&L=37693&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly9zb3Mub3JlZ29uLmdvdi9lbGVjdGlvbnMvRG9jdW1lbnRzL0RpcmVjdGl2ZS0yMDI2LTAxLUxpc3QtTWFpbnRlbmFuY2UucGRmP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=9309803c55f4cab55a0fec0236f89c69bdf2fbc27d0c6b075777db34039a9ddc],
which were issued
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81221&L=37694&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly9zb3Mub3JlZ29uLmdvdi9lbGVjdGlvbnMvRG9jdW1lbnRzL0RpcmVjdGl2ZS0yMDI2LTAyLVZvdGVyLUNvbmZpcm1hdGlvbi1DYXJkcy5wZGY/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=4174fea449dff7847e22e005c8ee029a6f66835fb0ddc32a3c1823d7011c4fd4]
in response to our lawsuit.

The agreement also gives us the authority to request detailed records
and monitor Oregon’s compliance, including documentation related to
county performance and voter list maintenance procedures.

We are a national leader in election integrity and voting rights
litigation, with a record of successful lawsuits
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81221&L=35695&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvdGFnL2VsZWN0aW9uK2xhdy8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=9397a9e3d745abe0c3f21cda7e7ee80355607c49f190c9e5632b403f671aff4b] enforcing
constitutional redistricting standards and cleaning voter rolls
nationwide.

Robert Popper, a Judicial Watch senior attorney, leads our election
law program. Popper was previously in the Voting Section of the Civil
Rights Division of the Justice Department, where he managed voting
rights investigations, litigations, consent decrees, and settlements
in dozens of states.

We are assisted by Stephen Joncus of Joncus Law PC in Happy Valley,
Oregon.

Colorado recently removed 372,000 ineligible voter names thanks to a
Judicial Watch lawsuit and settlement
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81221&L=37547&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvaW5hY3RpdmUtdm90ZXJzLXJlbW92ZWQtY29sb3JhZG8tdm90ZXItcm9sbHMvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=bb1fa1fddf4eb8f9ed8e979f56a60d8cc8c60777d1282c1f5d4fc059cbceac3b]
addressing the state’s compliance with federal voter list

In Kentucky, state election board officials reported
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81221&L=34903&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtYWRhbXMta3ktbnZyYS1jb21wbGlhbmNlLW5vdGljZS0wMDA5NC8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=bc0d19050231e9e2e7b38d34efcd9fa4ab74a2999b47431616febf3c8d30105d]
that “roughly 735,000 ineligible voter registrations” have been

As part of its 2022 settlement
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81221&L=28646&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvanctYW5kLW55Yy1zZXR0bGUtbGF3c3VpdC8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=f85eb002f4b9dfa8334e25686194139c3301a613cd8d8d197cbaf3272fc1aa08], New York
City alone has removed 918,139 ineligible names from its rolls: data
show 477,056
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81221&L=34904&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtdmFsZW50aW5lLW55Yy1udnJhLXJlbW92YWxzLTIwMjMtMjAyNS0wMzk1Mi8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=e75255b60e0d9142650fb9fd77ab73add57a5816b3083f563ddb2f3e405036e4]

A federal court in Illinois
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81221&L=35799&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvbGF3c3VpdHMtdG8tY2xlYW4tdXAtdm90ZXItcm9sbHMvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=3ffca2533bc2dd25e07948d2d4a3492d4dca7643bff693d9ece8d8bc8133401b] has

proceed in that state. We have sent a notice-of-violation letter to

voter rolls is imminent.

In March 2026, the Supreme Court of the United States held oral
argument
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81221&L=37415&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvc3VwcmVtZS1jb3VydC1hcmd1bWVudC1vbi1tYXJjaC0yMy8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=87e58d9275fd7bdd0ab7429e8654c731a5006840db6b8ee01017b047d0316aee] in
a landmark election integrity case over whether the federal Election
Day laws prohibit the counting of mail-in ballots that arrive after
Election Day. We brought the underlying lawsuit on behalf of the
Libertarian Party of Mississippi.

In January 2026, in a historic case we filed, the Supreme Court
decided 7-2
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81221&L=36862&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvc3RhdGVtZW50LW9uLXN1cHJlbWUtY291cnQtdmljdG9yeS8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=de636c6707638922dd9e766bf7322dceeb9b555f08c46cc6b4e1b808dae9061c] in
favor of Congressman Mike Bost and two presidential electors who were
before the court to vindicate their standing to challenge an Illinois
law allowing the counting of ballots received up to 14 days after
Election Day.

The States United Democracy Center was accused
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81221&L=37378&F=T&&hash=704add0a304760b8a7f9b7b7348be5ed584af5de89bfa6284d2bdf24ff1eb8a8]
of helping to develop legal theories and materials related to the

JUDICIAL WATCH SUES FOR RECORDS ON TRUMP GOLF CLUB SECURITY BREACH

President Trump has survived multiple
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81221&L=37711&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYXhpb3MuY29tLzIwMjYvMDQvMjYvdHJ1bXAtYXNzYXNzaW5hdGlvbi1hdHRlbXB0cy1wbG90cy10aW1lbGluZS13aGNkP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=72dc1c107a521a4efdbac8c0481a8b374eb12be52da4ed6ca395932d51a4df4f]
assassination attempts, including the Butler, Pennsylvania Rally –
July 13, 2024; West Palm Beach, Florida – September 15, 2024; and
most recently at the White House Correspondents’ Dinner, Washington,
D.C. – April 25, 2026.

We are suing for records on a serious security breach at Trump
National Golf Club in Sterling, VA.

We filed a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) lawsuit
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81221&L=37708&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtZGhzLXRydW1wLW5hdGlvbmFsLWNvbXBsYWludC0wMTQ0Ny8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=5b3ba8ce70682f31d92f9506f006d3664af9192361bc148e1371cb54599c7a52]
for U.S. Secret Service (USSS) records related to an August 31, 2025,
incident in which a club member allegedly carried a loaded
semi-automatic handgun past screening checkpoints at Trump National
Golf Club in Virginia while President Donald Trump was on site
(_Judicial Watch Inc. v. U.S. Department of Homeland Security_
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81221&L=37709&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtZGhzLXRydW1wLW5hdGlvbmFsLWNvbXBsYWludC0wMTQ0Ny9dXz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=6d787f0b521d3dfbbe295ad9047dd28ee1f918881c408c38c52808e8f9acd90f _(No.
1:26-cv-01447)).

According to reports
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81221&L=37710&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly9ueXBvc3QuY29tLzIwMjUvMDkvMTAvdXMtbmV3cy9zZWNyZXQtc2VydmljZS1taXNzZWQtZ2xvY2staW4tYmFnLWF0LXRydW1wcy12aXJnaW5pYS1nb2xmLWNvdXJzZS8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=b0fcaa250a1aa46f98b33682101bd5102b67b7e78ff876e394b3699607574303],
a club member was able to bring a loaded semi-automatic handgun onto
the premises while Trump was present, after passing through Secret
Service screening checkpoints. A Secret Service spokesperson said that
handheld magnetometers were used instead of walkthrough devices when
screening guests at the president’s golf resort, located about 25
miles northwest of the White House.

It’s very disturbing that a security lapse of this magnitude could
occur, particularly given recent threats against the president. The
public has a right to know how this happened and what is being done to
prevent it from happening again.

The agent in charge of searching the guest’s bag at the golf
facility was placed on administrative leave amid an ongoing review by
the Secret Service.

We filed the lawsuit in the U.S. District Court for the District of
Columbia after the Secret Service failed to respond to a November 18,
2025, FOIA request for:

> All records related to the internal investigation of the August 31,
> 2025, incident at the Trump National Golf Club in Sterling,
> Virginia, in which a club member was able to get a semi-automatic
> hand gun into the club premises while the President was present
> without initial detection, including but not limited to
> investigative reports, agents’ notes, witness interview,
> audio-video recordings and other records.
>
> All emails and text messages sent between members of the
> Presidential protective detail regarding the August 31, 2025,
> incident at the golf club.

In April 2026, our FOIA lawsuit forced the release of records
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81221&L=37637&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvYWx0ZXJjYXRpb24tYXQtdHJ1bXAtcmFsbHktc2l0ZS8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=ded3f47c2adf5dab2aee5fb7d658459816924a45b4d7b317379b652ce97c2072] from

with a group of people and making “hateful comments” directed at

2024, assassination attempt.

In February 2026, our lawsuit forced the release of the first FBI
records [https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=81221&L=37141&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvdHJ1bXAtc2hvb3RpbmctaW4tYnV0bGVyLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=dba1aa3624062f799df481b18d9ae4ccee42aa30650a98e7d072cb1a6c1f1d52]

clean_body: Victory in J6 Bodycam Case [INSIDE JW] SUPREME COURT BLOCKS RACE-BASED GERRYMANDERING [] Big news out of the courts this week—U.S. Supreme Court has issued a major 6-3 decision [] that could reshape how congressional districts are drawn across the country. This is a great decision for democracy. It affirms that the U.S. Constitution requires the government to be colorblind in its decision making. The decision effectively ends the egregious racial segregation of voters. We are prepared to enforce this decision and end any racial gerrymandering that treats voters as cogs in a racial spoils system. Most Americans would agree with the court’s conclusion that “allowing race to play any part in government decision-making represents a departure from the constitutional rule that applies in almost every other context.” In January 2025, Judicial Watch and AEF filed an initial amici brief (friends of the court) in this case, asking the court [] to affirm a lower court ruling that Louisiana violated the constitution when it crowded minority voters into congressional districts. Judicial Watch and AEF filed a supplemental amici curiae [] brief in September 2025. We argued: This Court has compared race-based districting to segregation of “public parks, ... buses, ... and schools,” and warned that we “should not be carving electorates into racial blocs.” ... That is because “[c]lassifications of citizens solely on the basis of race ‘are by their very nature odious to a free people whose institutions are founded upon the doctrine of equality.’” ... Racial gerrymandering, like all “[r]acial classifications of any sort” cause “lasting harm to our society” because “[t]hey reinforce the belief, held by too many for too much of our history, that individuals should be judged by the color of their skin.” ... There should be no question that race-based division of citizens for purposes of compliance with § 2 and Gingles is a violation of the Equal Protection Clause, the “central purpose” of which “is to prevent the States from purposefully discriminating between individuals on the basis of race.”... The same may be said of the Voting Rights Act. In his concurring opinion, Justice Thomas agreed, denouncing the practice where: “Blacks [were] drawn into ‘black districts’ and given ‘black representatives’; Hispanics [were] drawn into Hispanic districts and given ‘Hispanic representatives’; and so on.” . . . That interpretation is “repugnant to any nation that strives for the ideal of a color-blind Constitution.” . . . Today’s decision should largely put an end to this “disastrous misadventure” in voting-rights jurisprudence. AEF is a charitable and educational foundation dedicated to improving the quality of life through education. In furtherance of that goal, the Foundation has engaged in a number of projects, which include, but are not limited to, educational and health conferences domestically and abroad. AEF has partnered frequently with Judicial Watch to fight government and judicial corruption and to promote a return to ethics and morality in the nation’s public life. We are a national leader in election integrity and voting rights litigation, with a record of successful lawsuits [] enforcing constitutional redistricting standards and cleaning voter rolls nationwide. We pointed out [] earlier this year that Maryland’s Democrat-proposed 2026 congressional redistricting plan [] is in key respects identical to the unconstitutional gerrymander struck down in a prior Judicial Watch lawsuit [ is even more partisan and less compact than the invalidated 2021 map. Our lawsuits and legal actions have caused the removal [] of six million ineligible names from voter lists nationwide. In March 2026, the Supreme Court of the United States held oral argument [] in a landmark election integrity case over whether the federal Election Day laws prohibit the counting of mail-in ballots that arrive after Election Day. We brought the underlying lawsuit on behalf of the Libertarian Party of Mississippi. In January 2026, in a historic case we filed, the Supreme Court decided 7-2 [] in favor of Congressman Mike Bost and two presidential electors who were before the court to vindicate their standing to challenge an Illinois law allowing the counting of ballots received up to 14 days after Election Day. COURT VICTORY: D.C. BLOCKED FROM HIDING JANUARY 6 BODYCAM FOOTAGE In a major victory for Judicial Watch and the rule of law, a Washington, D.C. court ruled [] that Washington D.C.’s Metropolitan Police Department (MPD) cannot broadly blur and censor body-worn camera (BWC) footage from January 6, 2021. The MPD had previously claimed [] that such redactions were necessary to protect personal privacy and asserted that complying with the request would cost more than $1.5 million due to the volume of footage, which reportedly exceeds one thousand hours. The ruling follows a January 8, 2026, hearing [] before Judge Veronica Sanchez in our Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) lawsuit [] for all bodycam footage recorded by Metropolitan Police officers who responded to the U.S. Capitol on January 6 (Judicial Watch v. District of Columbia [] (No. 2024-CAB-003453)). In its order [], the court rejected the District’s argument that it could justify redacting, under D.C. FOIA exemptions, the faces and voices of all non-law enforcement individuals captured in the footage. The court found that while individuals appearing in the footage may have a limited privacy interest, that interest is minimal and does not outweigh the strong public interest in disclosure. The court emphasized that the events of January 6 occurred largely in public settings, were widely recorded, and remain a matter of significant national concern. Concerning the individuals’ privacy interests, the court explained that the individuals present could not reasonably expect that their appearances would remain private: The circumstances leading up to the events at the U.S. Capitol Complex included a large, public, televised event on the National Mall. Individuals were gathered in a public arena. The crowd then marched to the U.S. Capitol Complex where members of congress were partaking in the certification of the Presidential election results live on television. The individuals on the [body-worn cameras] could not “reasonably expect that their appearances on the [BWC] would remain private.” ... A person’s privacy interest is greater in the release of images and video footage that were taken without their “knowing consent.” ... Even though the individuals recorded on the subject BWCs may not have consented to their images being recorded, they certainly had reason to believe that the many law enforcement officers, as well as the surveillance cameras at the government owned property, were recording them. Additionally, during the events of January 6, simultaneous media images from both inside the U.S. Capitol and around the U.S. Capitol Complex were being broadcast, streamed, and uploaded. While the individuals may have not all “knowingly consented” to the images being recorded, they had reason to know they were. Concerning the public interest, the court stated: The events of January 6, 2021, have been the topic of much debate, discussion, and scrutiny in the public consciousness by not just the residents of the District of Columbia but also the nation. BWC footage of the events at the Capitol on January 6 could “contribute significantly” to the public’s understanding of the events on that day and the MPD’s actions. The court concluded, “In balancing the privacy interests which are little more than de minimis with the public interest, the public interest outweighs the privacy interests. ... [T]he faces and voices of individuals captured by MPD’s BWCs on January 6, 2021, are not exempt from disclosure.” This a major victory for transparency and the rule of law. As the court found, the American people have a right to see this secret D.C. police January 6 footage. We will now push for the immediate release of the videos. We have extensively investigated the events of January 6. In April 2026, we filed a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) lawsuit [] against the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) to obtain records related to possible improper targeting of January 6, 2021, Capitol protesters, their supporters, and related nonprofits In August 2025, we announced that the U.S. Air Force would finally provide full military funeral honors [] to Ashli Babbitt, the Air Force veteran who was shot and killed inside the U.S. Capitol by then-Capitol Police Lt. Michael Byrd on January 6, 2021. Babbitt was the only official January 6 homicide victim. The Biden administration had previously denied Babbitt and her family these honors in retaliation for being at the U.S. Capitol that day. This decision came on the heels of a massive, nearly $5 million Trump administration settlement [] to her family for wrongful death and other claims against the U.S. Government. In July 2025, we sued [] the U.S. Department of Justice for records on accelerated January 6 prosecutions after Donald Trump was elected president in November 2024. The Biden administration, anticipating President-elect Donald Trump’s promise to issue pardons for January 6, 2021, defendants, is believed to have accelerated prosecutions in the final months of Biden’s term. In March 2024, we received Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives (ATF) records [] from the Department of Justice in a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) lawsuit that show the Central Intelligence Agency (CIA) deployed personnel to Washington, DC, on January 6, 2021. In October 2023, we received the declaration [] of James W. Joyce, senior counsel in the Office of the General Counsel for the Capitol Police, in which he describes emails among senior officials of the United States Capitol Police (USCP) in January 2021 that show warnings of possible January 6 protests that could lead to serious disruptions at the U.S. Capitol. OREGON SETTLEMENT TARGETS 800,000 INELIGIBLE VOTER REGISTRATIONS Election victory in Oregon! We have a settlement [] in our federal lawsuit against Oregon election officials, which confirms 800,000 [] ineligible voter names are slated for review and removal from voter registration lists. The settlement requires state officials to produce detailed data and enforce federal voter roll clean-up procedures under the National Voter Registration Act of 1993 (NVRA). We filed the lawsuit [] in October 2024, alleging Oregon failed to remove ineligible voters and seeking to enforce Section 8 of the NVRA after identifying [] widespread voter roll maintenance failures across dozens of counties (Judicial Watch, et al. v. The State of Oregon et al. [] (No. 6:24-cv-01783)). In our complaint, we argued that Oregon’s voter rolls contain large numbers of old, inactive registrations; and that 29 of Oregon’s 36 counties removed few or no registrations as required by federal election law. We asserted that Oregon and 35 of its counties had overall registration rates exceeding 100%; and that Oregon had the highest known inactive registration rate of any state in the nation. In combination, all of these facts showed that Oregon was failing to remove inactive registrations as required by federal law. In August 2025, a federal court in Oregon denied a motion to dismiss by Oregon and ruled [] the lawsuit could proceed. In response to the lawsuit, Oregon Secretary of State Tobias Read announced [] earlier this year that Oregon has about 800,000 inactive registrations, which are kept separately from the active voter rolls and do not receive ballots. Of those, roughly 160,000 already meet federal and state criteria for removal—having received confirmation notices, failed to respond, and not voted in two federal elections—and are slated for cancellation. The remaining approximately 640,000 inactive records do not yet qualify for removal and will be processed through future list maintenance efforts. In its press release, Oregon acknowledged that routine removal of outdated records effectively stalled in 2017, leaving a large pool of long-dormant registrations on the rolls without being fully processed for removal. The scale of the backlog underscores a gap in routine list maintenance that is only now being addressed. “These directives are about cleaning up old data that’s no longer in use so Oregonians can be confident that our voter records are up to date,” said Read. This is another historic election integrity success. Our lawsuit caused Oregon to finally cleanup 800,000 outdated voter names, adding to the more than six million ineligible voters removed by Judicial Watch lawsuits and legal action nationwide. Dirty voter rolls can mean dirty elections. Oregon’s Secretary of State, Tobias Read, is to be commended for responding to our lawsuit with a massive voter roll clean-up and commitment to continued voter list maintenance, which will only increase voter confidence.” Our lawsuits and legal actions have caused the removal [] of six million ineligible names from voter lists nationwide. Under the National Voter Registration Act of 1993 (NVRA []), states must take reasonable steps to remove ineligible voters—such as those who have died, moved, or become otherwise inactive. The settlement [] with Oregon remains in effect for more than five years, with a federal court retaining jurisdiction to enforce its terms. While the settlement resolves the litigation, it explicitly allows future legal action if Oregon fails to comply with voter list clean-up requirements going forward. The settlement requires Oregon to open its voter roll maintenance processes to unprecedented scrutiny. State officials must now regularly provide detailed, county-level data on voter registrations, removals, confirmation notices, and inactive voters—including those eligible for removal under federal law. This includes data reported to the Election Assistance Commission, as well as additional datasets that will allow ongoing monitoring of compliance. The agreement ensures that this information will not be hidden behind bureaucratic barriers, requiring timely disclosure and identification of data sources. Oregon will also provide annual reports on inactive voters and those eligible for removal under federal law, as well as provide free access to the state’s voter registration list upon request to Judicial Watch. Oregon committed to implementing Read’s new list maintenance directives [], which were issued [] in response to our lawsuit. The agreement also gives us the authority to request detailed records and monitor Oregon’s compliance, including documentation related to county performance and voter list maintenance procedures. We are a national leader in election integrity and voting rights litigation, with a record of successful lawsuits [] enforcing constitutional redistricting standards and cleaning voter rolls nationwide. Robert Popper, a Judicial Watch senior attorney, leads our election law program. Popper was previously in the Voting Section of the Civil Rights Division of the Justice Department, where he managed voting rights investigations, litigations, consent decrees, and settlements in dozens of states. We are assisted by Stephen Joncus of Joncus Law PC in Happy Valley, Oregon. Colorado recently removed 372,000 ineligible voter names thanks to a Judicial Watch lawsuit and settlement [] addressing the state’s compliance with federal voter list maintenance requirements. In Kentucky, state election board officials reported [] that “roughly 735,000 ineligible voter registrations” have been removed from voter rolls, as part of a 2018 consent decree settling a Judicial Watch lawsuit. As part of its 2022 settlement [], New York City alone has removed 918,139 ineligible names from its rolls: data show 477,056 [] removals between March 2023 and February 2025, which is in addition to the 441,083 previously reported removals. In Los Angeles, county officials confirmed the removal of more than 1.2 million names from voter rolls as part of a settlement. Judicial Watch legal pressure also resulted in election roll clean-ups in Pennsylvania [], North Carolina [], and Ohio []. A federal court in Illinois [] has ruled that our lawsuit to force the cleaning of voter rolls may proceed in that state. We have sent a notice-of-violation letter to election officials in California, and legal action over the state’s voter rolls is imminent. In March 2026, the Supreme Court of the United States held oral argument [] in a landmark election integrity case over whether the federal Election Day laws prohibit the counting of mail-in ballots that arrive after Election Day. We brought the underlying lawsuit on behalf of the Libertarian Party of Mississippi. In January 2026, in a historic case we filed, the Supreme Court decided 7-2 [] in favor of Congressman Mike Bost and two presidential electors who were before the court to vindicate their standing to challenge an Illinois law allowing the counting of ballots received up to 14 days after Election Day. NEVADA ATTORNEY GENERAL COORDINATED WITH LEFTISTS ON ELECTION ISSUES Judicial Watch received 238 pages [] of records from the State of Nevada Office of Attorney General in a Nevada Public Records Act lawsuit that show the state’s close coordination with the nonprofit States United Democracy Center [], which has pushed indictments [] of President Donald Trump’s supporters, lawyers, activists, and Republican Party officials who challenged the results of the 2020 election. We sued [] [ state of Nevada attorney general’s office after it failed to respond to a February 2025 records request for documents and communications involving the States United Democracy Center (SUDC) and the Voter Protection Project (VPP) (Judicial Watch Inc. v. State of Nevada ex. rel. Office of the Attorney General [] [. A-26-941044)). The States United Democracy Center was accused [] of helping to develop legal theories and materials related to the prosecution of alternate slates of Republican electors in the 2020 election. The Voter Protection Project [] is a left-wing political action committee that donates to far-left candidates who support eliminating many U.S. election security laws. The records include a January 2021 internal email [] from States United Democracy Center’s Fiona Dwyer-McNulty [] referencing a one-hour “AG Briefing” featuring outside participants, including Mary McCord [ representatives from 21CP Solutions [ Jared Holt [] of DFR Labs; Shannon Hiller [] of the Bridging Divides Initiative; and Norman Eisen []. In an April 15, 2021, email chain [] labeled “Bipartisan Sign-on Letter re Corporate Leadership,” Joanna Lydgate [], president and CEO of Voter Protection Program/States United Democracy Center, writes to Nevada Attorney General Aaron D. Ford: As you know, I’m the CEO of the Voter Protection Program ... As of Monday, VPP will become a project of our new umbrella 501(c)(3) organization, the States United Democracy Center, but our mission will remain the same. We are looking forward to continuing our work with you under our new name. One of the first projects of the States United Democracy Center is organizing an OPEN LETTER TO THE BUSINESS COMMUNITY FROM A BIPARTISAN COALITION OF CURRENT AND FORMER STATE OFFICIALS — governors, lieutenant governors, attorneys general, and secretaries of state — urging businesses to continue to speak out in support of voting rights and applauding those who have done so already. [Emphasis in original] I have attached a PDF of the letter, which is co-sponsored by current Governors Roy Cooper (NC) and Gretchen Whitmer (MI) and former Governors Arne Carlson (MN), Bill Weld (MA), and Christine Todd Whitman (NJ). I’m writing to ask you to join these governors in expressing your support for the freedom to vote and your concern about the voting restrictions being proposed across the country. Lydgate welcomes Nevada General Counsel Leslie M. Nino Piro to the email chain [] and Piro responds: “It's great to meet you, Joanna! I look forward to working with you as well.” Later, Attorney General Aaron Ford writes that Piro will “be attending with and/or for me going forward.” Renee D. Carreau, executive assistant to the solicitor general, writes [] to Lydgate and others: “Nevada would like to sign on to this letter. Please see our signature block attached. Please let me know if you need anything further & send us the final letter after it has been sent.” She later writes: Ms. Lydgate & Dwyer-McNulty, I would like to request to add our Solicitor General, Heidi Parry Stern, cc'd above, and me to any of these types of communications. We handle all multistate litigation and sign-ons to Amici briefs and letters. Either Ms. Stern or I will be responding to the below request, before COB tomorrow. Please let me know if you have any questions. The letter [], addressed to “Dear Business Leaders,” opposes a “a wave of voter restrictions sweeping the country.” In a February 2025 email [] to Dwyer-McNulty, Nevada Chief Deputy Attorney General Greg D. Ott asks to have Deputy Attorney General Devin Oliver added to States United Democracy Center’s monthly conference calls as “He’s coordinating much of the multistate litigation here and I think it would be beneficial for him to hear the litigation summaries that your team provides.” Dwyer-McNulty responds that Oliver was added to the list. These records further expose a coordinated leftist lawfare machine—leftist state attorneys general working hand-in-glove with allied activist groups to target political opponents and undermine our election system. In November 2025, we received 5,789 pages of records [] from the Michigan Department of Attorney General in a Michigan Freedom of Information Act request that shows the state’s coordination with States United Democracy Center. We continue to work to obtain records from Arizona [], and Wisconsin [], which appear to have been part of the same coordinated efforts led by States United Democracy Center. In July 2025, we filed a Georgia Public Records lawsuit [] against Fani Willis in her capacity as district attorney for the Atlanta Circuit for communications and agreements with the States United Democracy Center and/or the Voter Protection Project regarding the investigation and prosecution of Republican state electors for the 2020 presidential election and/or anti-racketeering for conspiring to nullify the election. JUDICIAL WATCH SUES FOR RECORDS ON TRUMP GOLF CLUB SECURITY BREACH President Trump has survived multiple [] assassination attempts, including the Butler, Pennsylvania Rally – July 13, 2024; West Palm Beach, Florida – September 15, 2024; and most recently at the White House Correspondents’ Dinner, Washington, D.C. – April 25, 2026. We are suing for records on a serious security breach at Trump National Golf Club in Sterling, VA. We filed a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) lawsuit [] for U.S. Secret Service (USSS) records related to an August 31, 2025, incident in which a club member allegedly carried a loaded semi-automatic handgun past screening checkpoints at Trump National Golf Club in Virginia while President Donald Trump was on site (Judicial Watch Inc. v. U.S. Department of Homeland Security [ (No. 1:26-cv-01447)). According to reports [], a club member was able to bring a loaded semi-automatic handgun onto the premises while Trump was present, after passing through Secret Service screening checkpoints. A Secret Service spokesperson said that handheld magnetometers were used instead of walkthrough devices when screening guests at the president’s golf resort, located about 25 miles northwest of the White House. It’s very disturbing that a security lapse of this magnitude could occur, particularly given recent threats against the president. The public has a right to know how this happened and what is being done to prevent it from happening again. The agent in charge of searching the guest’s bag at the golf facility was placed on administrative leave amid an ongoing review by the Secret Service. We filed the lawsuit in the U.S. District Court for the District of Columbia after the Secret Service failed to respond to a November 18, 2025, FOIA request for: All records related to the internal investigation of the August 31, 2025, incident at the Trump National Golf Club in Sterling, Virginia, in which a club member was able to get a semi-automatic hand gun into the club premises while the President was present without initial detection, including but not limited to investigative reports, agents’ notes, witness interview, audio-video recordings and other records. All emails and text messages sent between members of the Presidential protective detail regarding the August 31, 2025, incident at the golf club. In April 2026, our FOIA lawsuit forced the release of records [] from the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) that show that would-be Trump assassin Thomas Crooks was reportedly involved in an altercation with a group of people and making “hateful comments” directed at President Trump at the Butler, PA, rally site before the July 13, 2024, assassination attempt. In February 2026, our lawsuit forced the release of the first FBI records [] about the Butler assassination attempt. In December 2025, we sued [] the U.S. Secret Service for communications records related to Code Pink protesters who disrupted a dinner held by President Trump at a restaurant in Washington, DC, on September 9, 2025 (Judicial Watch v. U.S. Department of Homeland Security [ (No. 1:25-cv-04408)). In September 2025, we filed a FOIA lawsuit [] for messages among top leaders of the FBI referencing social media posts of Special Agent Jeffrey Veltri, head of the Miami Field Office, which is investigating the September 15 assassination attempt against Donald Trump (Judicial Watch v. U.S. Department of Justice [] (No. 1:24-cv-02740)). In March 2025, we sued [] the U.S. Department of Homeland Security for records related to security provided for the July 13, 2024, rally in Butler, PA (Judicial Watch Inc. v. U.S. Department of Homeland Security [] (No. 1:25-cv-00704)). In August 2024, we uncovered [] documents from the district attorney’s office in Butler County, PA, detailing the extensive preparation of local police for the rally at which former President Trump was shot. The preparation included sniper teams, counter assault teams and a quick response force. In August 2024, in response to a separate open records request, we obtained [] bodycam footage of the July 13 assassination events from the Butler Township Police Department. In August 2024, following up on reports that the Biden Secret Service denied Trump’s requests for additional Secret Service protection, we filed a FOIA lawsuit [] for Secret Service and other records regarding potential increased protective services to Trump’s security detail prior to the attempt on his life at his July 13 campaign rally in Butler, PA (Judicial Watch v. U.S. Department of Homeland Security [ _(No. 1:24-cv-02495)). Until next week, [Contribute] [] [32x32x1] [] [32x32x2] [] [32x32x3] [] [32x32x3] [] Judicial Watch, Inc. 425 3rd St Sw Ste 800 Washington, DC 20024 202.646.5172 © 2017 - 2026, All Rights Reserved Manage Email Subscriptions [] |

subject: 5.1.2026 Grassroots Insider
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE REPUBLICAN PARTY OF WISCONSIN** NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE
body:
The bi-weekly collection of all things grassroots! No images? [Click here](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail20.com/t/y-e-akyfty-didthutlhu-k/) [](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akyfty-didthutlhu-r/) **May 1st, 2026** Patriot,The WisGOP State Convention is only TWO weeks away! Republicans from across the state will meet to strategize, build, and unify behind our statewide candidates.Together, we can win in November. Get involved TODAY! Go to [wisgop.org/volunteer](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akyfty-didthutlhu-y/).-Team WisGOP **Upcoming Events****5/2: Juneau County Kentucky Derby Event
5/2: Eau Claire County Nomination Signature Drive
5/2: Monroe County Conservative Breakfast
5/9: Green County Breakfast Meeting
5/14: Vilas County Meet the Candidates for Wisconsin's 7th Congressional District
5/14: Marinette County Monthly Meeting
5/19: Buffalo County Monthly Meeting
6/18: Chippewa County 9-Hole Golf Scramble
7/8: Chippewa County Northern County WI State Fair Booth

For more information, please visit: [wisgop.org/calendar/events/](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akyfty-didthutlhu-j/) ** **Monroe County**
Republicans in Monroe County hosted a Conservative Breakfast featuring Representative Nancy VanderMeer, then 15 Monroe County Republicans drove to La Crosse to support Congressman Derrick Van Orden's Grand Opening of the LAX Campaign Headquarters. Additionally, Monroe County Republicans hosted a nomination signature drive. Keep up the great work! **Lincoln County**
The Lincoln County Republican Party hosted a Coffee and Conversation event with a candidate for Congress! **Door County**
Door County had a great Lincoln Day dinner. They heard from elected officials as well as statewide and local candidates, which included Congressman Tony Wied, State Representatives, WisGOP Chairman Brian Schimming, and others. **Kewaunee County**
Kewaunee County Republicans gathered for their Lincoln Day dinner and heard from speakers including Congressman Tony Wied and Chairman Brian Schimming. **Green County**
The Green County Republican Party held its monthly breakfast meeting with another record-breaking attendance! We heard from guest speakers Senator Howard Marklein and Jon Aleckson, candidate for the 50th Assembly District. [](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akyfty-didthutlhu-t/)[](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akyfty-didthutlhu-i/)[](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akyfty-didthutlhu-d/)[](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail20.com/t/y-l-akyfty-didthutlhu-h/) | [ Share ](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail20.com/t/y-fb-akyfty-didthutlhu-u/) | [ Tweet ](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.cmail20.com/t/y-tw-akyfty-didthutlhu-o/) | [ Forward ](https://republicanpartyofwisconsin.forwardtomyfriend.com/y-didthutlhu-931243C5-akyfty-l-b)

Go to WisGOP Upcoming Events5/2: Juneau County Kentucky Derby Event 5/2: Eau Claire County Nomination Signature Drive 5/2: Monroe County Conservative Breakfast 5/9: Green County Breakfast Meeting 5/14: Vilas County Meet the Candidates for Wisconsin's 7th Congressional District 5/14: Marinette County Monthly Meeting 5/19: Buffalo County Monthly Meeting 6/18: Chippewa County 9-Hole Golf Scramble 7/8: Chippewa County Northern County WI State Fair Booth For more information, please visit: Monroe County Republicans in Monroe County hosted a Conservative Breakfast featuring Representative Nancy VanderMeer, then 15 Monroe County Republicans drove to La Crosse to support Congressman Derrick Van Orden's Grand Opening of the LAX Campaign Headquarters. Additionally, Monroe County Republicans hosted a nomination signature drive. Keep up the great work! Lincoln County The Lincoln County Republican Party hosted a Coffee and Conversation event with a candidate for Congress! Door County Door County had a great Lincoln Day dinner. They heard from elected officials as well as statewide and local candidates, which included Congressman Tony Wied, State Representatives, WisGOP Chairman Brian Schimming, and others. Kewaunee County Kewaunee County Republicans gathered for their Lincoln Day dinner and heard from speakers including Congressman Tony Wied and Chairman Brian Schimming. Green County The Green County Republican Party held its monthly breakfast meeting with another record-breaking attendance! We heard from guest speakers Senator Howard Marklein and Jon Aleckson, candidate for the 50th Assembly District. | | | ---|---|--- PAID FOR BY THE REPUBLICAN PARTY OF WISCONSIN NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE.www.WisGOP.org |

Newly leaked documents confirm government officials were planning to hand voter data to an outside political group.
| | | | | [](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001F1tllfYk_762F7gAm9zMuyqt3vy5-s6ove37G-bukbpFUriUGK0SYS4AvusmeQ-gCnlddIqf5Jq8Y7W0N7CdvMIeBtRSgf0EydiksbqrY9ljstd9TVP93zPi-AvKJRqttg9tMN9JbJk3VimXaLexZYh7g_2e1yJAb3keskwMfxbxun_IalkpB-5kOKSmuFIvExuNUviwo_rPABsPxy8mobfxsDKKnxeMWTf7-WIfr4Y=&c=iFkSWzOsv_hmGUclH4ycK4wBzIz6m6Yq7A-H9I3sFJTeTjZmVz6Owg==&ch=SxoiYX82Hr_D3urvbnROchr8BVTr3-csbyFxlNo-5rEGQ6N2EHBBsg==)

|
Peter,
Earlier this week, we sounded the alarm.
We laid out how MAGA-connected dark money groups like True the Vote, tied to MAGA operative Leonard Leo, tried to use faulty voter data to overturn election results in 2021. Courts threw out their challenge, saying their data "utterly lacked credibility."
We connected the dots on how the Trump administration's effort to weaponize DHS, a counterterrorism agency, to seize voter data in Michigan, Ohio, Georgia and other states looked like an evolution of that same plot but with more credible data.
That theory became fact:
---
| [](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001F1tllfYk_762F7gAm9zMuyqt3vy5-s6ove37G-bukbpFUriUGK0SYS4AvusmeQ-gCnlddIqf5Jq8Y7W0N7CdvMIeBtRSgf0EydiksbqrY9ljstd9TVP93zPi-AvKJRqttg9tMN9JbJk3VimXaLexZYh7g_2e1yJAb3keskwMfxbxun_IalkpB-5kOKSmuFIvExuNUviwo_rPABsPxy8mobU2j6Cdpl_35KDIGKI6c4E=&c=iFkSWzOsv_hmGUclH4ycK4wBzIz6m6Yq7A-H9I3sFJTeTjZmVz6Owg==&ch=SxoiYX82Hr_D3urvbnROchr8BVTr3-csbyFxlNo-5rEGQ6N2EHBBsg==)
---
Newly leaked documents confirm government officials were planning to hand voter data to an outside political group.
The group's identity was redacted but the plan is clear: the federal government seizes voter data then hands it to political operatives who will use it to challenge election results in 2026 and beyond. This is illegal as hell-- a clear violation of the Hatch Act.
The plot is in motion. There is only one full-proof strategy for Dems to stop it.
When these groups challenge election results using government-obtained data, those cases land in front of state Supreme Courts. If MAGA controls those courts, it's game over. If Democrats have the majority, we can prevent MAGA from stealing an election.
That is exactly why Defend Our Courts is leading the charge to build a firewall on state Supreme Courts. We won in Pennsylvania. We won in Wisconsin. Now we are coming to Georgia's aid.
On May 19, Georgia voters will elect two State Supreme Court justices. A win for Dems in both of these races paves the way to flip the entire court just months before the 2028 presidential election--a firewall against MAGA's election rigging scheme in a critical swing state.
This may be the most important election this year, and it's flying completely under the radar. Democrats must rally to Georgia's aid in the next 18 days before it's too late. That's why we are making a desperate call to Democrats everywhere:
[Please, will you rush a donation of $50, $25, or whatever you can spare to help Defend Our Courts build a firewall against MAGA's election rigging in the critical swing state of Georgia?](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001F1tllfYk_762F7gAm9zMuyqt3vy5-s6ove37G-bukbpFUriUGK0SYS4AvusmeQ-gB385VSmkDhkfT-ws3DBrccDVJt6stkkzDHiZgCWYoGVFaPzTuUKWI-LhoK-KTmmJs1KA4DSk2JCgRIneyYkFVPfjKjxkMcpEgaxad5kzihLk0JY-H8VFp0maJo5s3jwLjJ_cbqvxpFLU5pyjRtFMaTor6Crj015R9uICZr64TdkjMTjNxg0gOQ==&c=iFkSWzOsv_hmGUclH4ycK4wBzIz6m6Yq7A-H9I3sFJTeTjZmVz6Owg==&ch=SxoiYX82Hr_D3urvbnROchr8BVTr3-csbyFxlNo-5rEGQ6N2EHBBsg==)

body:
From Trump: Sign the petition to demand Voter ID & end election fraud

| | | | **24 HOUR EXTENSION AUTHORIZED.**
_We were supposed to shut this down yesterday..._
But at the request of President Trump, I’m keeping it open just for top supporters like you!
**_[Please, follow through on this request from JD Vance, Don Jr. and President Trump by completing your MAGA Census ASAP.](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zg70p0fcnfsp952q0vm5usvr4uo31esfdvgigp80_rp22sh2s8i6epr7cth6coj0c5h2bs)_**

|

Are your notifications turned off?
**FIRST,** Donald Trump Jr. reached out to you to take the **_Official MAGA Census_** , but you never responded.
**NEXT,** JD Vance followed up, but you still haven’t responded.
**NOW,** President Trump requested that we make a final email request because he needs to hear from you!
John, this is your last chance to take the **_Official MAGA Census_** before we **_SHUT IT DOWN_** to finalize our internal records.
**_[Finish it by MIDNIGHT and we’ll let President Trump, Donald Trump Jr., and JD Vance know that you completed it!](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z16p5mp6tud41nthtevid2jvslm79hmiqhfses00_rp22sh2s8i6epr7cth6coj0c5h2bs)_**

[COMPLETE CENSUS](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zo85kn3li54cnfplaferi197gugkqmnc1afunhv8_rp22sh2s8i6epr7cth6coj0c5h2bs)
There’s a reason we’re reaching out to you over anyone else in your hometown.
Reaching out directly to grassroots leaders is expensive, so we can only afford to reach out to President Trump’s most reliable supporters.
_That’s why your response will be used to represent every one of your friends and neighbors...THIS IS IMPORTANT._
**_[But we can only afford to keep your personal link live until 11:59 PM! That’s why I need you to follow this link ASAP and RESPOND TO TRUMP](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zp8ph0gapn704c58hllomul57ha3etffh9hdgb70_rp22sh2s8i6epr7cth6coj0c5h2bs)_**

name: Jamie (urgent)
subject: Election postponed!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jamie Davis for U
body:
When they can't win, they bend and break the rules.
| | | | | [](https://r6msg8hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001ci6dC5JibQbvpAmYa_b-OWk6n5BVheAmCiFs48BzMFp-wj9PZZdXCCBsSBSZ9jhuvj1b_RCR8RcbyEWY47kv4K9BfGOP91e4izjudSNdneeNXeVwrtCOvJMoaIh98mZcdSVBQlqYcHT8RcxHaChItCgbn2p4vPVCqzfHr2WMhxYkWY5CoSAOvlr4KWBbnA-PWEZmSV6X5lJQyYCZ2IsbNCIzWmr9IOCSBBuxYcO8tuM=&c=kG6cHBFYNFU9oxvXqrTbydsidOgSTL_hdxrdUlj3jZIZDcE06b26tg==&ch=sMYH6GaKnd6c-B92sigRMwEvI8oAycxygicGuQ3bpvbrrJLCbZPNLg==)
---
Peter, The MAGA Extremist Governor of Louisiana must think he is some kind of king or emperor, because he called in a meeting with Republicans to postpone our upcoming election so he can have his cronies at the state capitol redraw maps.
This is the Republican playbook: when they know they can't win, they bend, and then break, the rules.
Word on the street in New Orleans is that Gov. Jeff Landry is going to completely eliminate one or even both districts represented by Troy Carter or Cleo Fields, both Black U.S. Representatives from Louisiana.
---
| [](https://r6msg8hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001ci6dC5JibQbvpAmYa_b-OWk6n5BVheAmCiFs48BzMFp-wj9PZZdXCCBsSBSZ9jhuvj1b_RCR8RcbyEWY47kv4K9BfGOP91e4izjudSNdneeNXeVwrtCOvJMoaIh98mZcdSVBQlqYcHT8RcxHaChItCgbn2p4vPVCqzfHr2WMhxYkWY5CoSAOvlr4KWBbnA-PWEZmSV6X5lJQyYCZ2IsbNCgyNhIYxBNzgcQC9M-dhsM=&c=kG6cHBFYNFU9oxvXqrTbydsidOgSTL_hdxrdUlj3jZIZDcE06b26tg==&ch=sMYH6GaKnd6c-B92sigRMwEvI8oAycxygicGuQ3bpvbrrJLCbZPNLg==)
---
With the Voting Rights Act gutted by the recent Supreme Court decision, Republican-run states are rushing to redraw maps to eliminate Black representatives. And unfortunately, Louisiana is no stranger to racist voter suppression, so I am sad to say that the news I share comes as no surprise.
First, they came for Calvin Duncan and moved to eliminate his clerk of court seat; and now they are coming for the state's only two majority-minority districts represented by two Black men in Congress. And who are they coming for next? Black voters.
Their plan is to gerrymander this state back to the Reign of Terror.
Today, I woke up feeling like we are fighting the same fight my parents, grandparents, and great-grandparents fought, and it is this fight that makes America great. America was made great by the people who resisted, persisted, and fought for their freedom and right to a life of dignity.
We are going to fight like hell to make sure that these people who want to rig elections, gerrymander maps, take away elected black men's seats, and dilute black votes know that their time is up, and we are not going back.
I'm Jamie Davis, and I'm the Louisiana farmer and Democrat running to unseat MAGA Senator Bill Cassidy.
[If you see the gravity of this historical moment we are living in, I'm asking you to give what you can so that we make sure this reign of terror comes to an end. There have been many times when the good people of this country had to send people and resources to those fighting the good fight in Louisiana. Today is one of those days.](https://r6msg8hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001ci6dC5JibQbvpAmYa_b-OWk6n5BVheAmCiFs48BzMFp-wj9PZZdXCCBsSBSZ9jhu3ux8koTKzdX_ocDglgIVJmjDrWRZ4wzkeHV3W-Uqh8LjrY93e91uWMpVAgSxcRMl7jP_Zdw02BaL34xLBhlwzkI033UKwYzpOwoLJXyeQJ1Xfyw_DBYaSTYq06ePNypaXdiIkH5go_W8KIA3PkWhVEwyAFLFyT3AxLkQ57N_IlIJnZMaNdu_eA==&c=kG6cHBFYNFU9oxvXqrTbydsidOgSTL_hdxrdUlj3jZIZDcE06b26tg==&ch=sMYH6GaKnd6c-B92sigRMwEvI8oAycxygicGuQ3bpvbrrJLCbZPNLg==)

clean_body: When they can't win, they bend and break the rules. | | | | | == Peter, The MAGA Extremist Governor of Louisiana must think he is some kind of king or emperor, because he called in a meeting with Republicans to postpone our upcoming election so he can have his cronies at the state capitol redraw maps. This is the Republican playbook: when they know they can't win, they bend, and then break, the rules. Word on the street in New Orleans is that Gov. Jeff Landry is going to completely eliminate one or even both districts represented by Troy Carter or Cleo Fields, both Black U.S. Representatives from Louisiana. | == With the Voting Rights Act gutted by the recent Supreme Court decision, Republican-run states are rushing to redraw maps to eliminate Black representatives. And unfortunately, Louisiana is no stranger to racist voter suppression, so I am sad to say that the news I share comes as no surprise. First, they came for Calvin Duncan and moved to eliminate his clerk of court seat; and now they are coming for the state's only two majority-minority districts represented by two Black men in Congress. And who are they coming for next? Black voters. Their plan is to gerrymander this state back to the Reign of Terror. Today, I woke up feeling like we are fighting the same fight my parents, grandparents, and great-grandparents fought, and it is this fight that makes America great. America was made great by the people who resisted, persisted, and fought for their freedom and right to a life of dignity. We are going to fight like hell to make sure that these people who want to rig elections, gerrymander maps, take away elected black men's seats, and dilute black votes know that their time is up, and we are not going back. # Saturday, May 2, 2026
---|---
**Good morning!** On this day in 1933, the [Loch Ness Monster](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0D1uxCAQxfHT4A4Lj40_Coo07jZtSouYYZksBgvIIt8-2ig3SP2T3pP-H7ftpslTuL_rA1WxcQtYs8dSMG1VpzNWTNmgNp4CPjCU7_1xbSBg5EJyAZvR5K9taIyyiIB9g6qbpmnqxh7mxqkBR9CLnfcFO2ElWmtmC_YTd21htroh9VoTUkAnhISplctie7SyG_Qih3lhg0BDJab2K1IoDq2_4hNTu8ej8cqVcmbWvzFYGay11tZRLjFdL2ewFkeZG31xCvxPGKyHvjgwWH3cHQ-YMz9iyAUTz3R3BU2TlDkreU95nNgg7ocm_3tZ_tXpqeAnAAD__-FZfw8) was first sighted near Inverness, Scotland, igniting a media frenzy that included a £20,000 reward for capturing "Nessie." It's been a wild week of news, and some of the biggest stories may have flown right under your radar. President Trump hinted that a major UFO file release is coming "in the near future," engineers are testing sound waves to extinguish wildfires, and Saudi investors are pulling the plug on LIV Golf. Tune in to _The Flyover Podcast_ as Amy Hess takes an in-depth look at some of the most interesting stories of the week. [Tune in here](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykzzFuwyAUgOHTwBbr8QATDwxdsqVrR0TCI6bBYGEa17ev2iv0AP8vfR9Xd_Upp_J49wvZHqsrtG-Zeqfmdt_WulPbAvmQU6Enlf51fx4OAccT6BOgCz7lwykebCRCkpysMMYYMUo889ka8gql0HBGoGAkSDJCxzCRFONNRp7s7w00oADQaAY9TVFS1EL5SavzxBRQSL224bOm0meK-agvasO9Ljzbufd1Y_KN4YXhpdGt-RKGfDC8rDXQd29e8GbDuqec0zYapuCx-JT_-v4v9MviTwAAAP__20Rqog)! Mother's Day is right around the corner. If you haven't started shopping, start here: our [2026 Gift Guide](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LFyrCAUxvGngW4dQBApKG5jt7dNyRA46MmiOMiu49tnNk0eIPX3za_4f9zd3WPGbf7vV7AtFbfBeWRoDao7fd3LCfWI4GPGDR6wtWd4XE4wMdyYujHhosd8OUmjTQACegqWa601H3ox0sVqrmQPKegQuPGcS4jS83H8lMEMPDKK9q0xxQRnTAndKWNSD0lx6Y2SoyGSQcRWavdVcGsLpHyVF9QulJVmu7S2H6T_R8RExPQ7z5ja_MQI7x8R01raAvW4RX_RauN-Ys54DJpINq8e8w_X_tTgZcV3AAAA__8J33FU) has thoughtful, practical, and indulgent picks for every kind of mom.

There's a difference between worrying and simply being informed. Whether it's a teenage driver, a second car, or an older parent still on the road, having a clear, simple way to stay connected can make a real difference. That's why today's sponsor, [Shadow GPS, is worth a look](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkE2u2yAURleDZ47g8mcGDNpYlqI2nXaIbLgk1MRYNo2V3T8lW3jjT9_R0fl7ddcx5bTc_owPtDUWt-CxZ6wVN3eM21oO3PaAY8hpwRmX-t_PLwcUVEtlS8GFMeWXE02wERGQN2iZ1lozxaFr7tZMIRiIqLyJlFEpqfdqQlCgtZkMa5J906ikwCiVoE_SmMgxSiZGI0VniKAYUi3b6V9JS71jzK_yxO3ky6PJ9l7ruhP-g8BAYLiVus3sPREYCoHB5-RnAgPVknmMvlVcs1bwKbaT6EQbJk55xzgGmAgfMuE9LgTU6j5PlwLhPZE_z78v51_u0hPZN5sN65FyTrvSRNDbY0z5Y1O_lfBp4SsAAP__JJ9_lg).

## Trump Rejects War Powers Act Deadline
The Trump administration says [the war with Iran was ended](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LHOnDAQBOCnMZ1PZrExFC7S0P1pU6IFr382Z2xkfId4--jSpU79STOa-fU1fyFHTt8_cSdXQ54TXWekWqnMF5YjX1ROT-gjJ3pSqq_1ec-goJfKSAWzR473rBvvAhFQ15BrrbW27TsYms2NsCxogwGvu07psIShJUMajaVh0aZh90lTRkGrlAH7MOMYOgqm1TgaPYxCK_Jcc3n8zpzqRiHe-U3lsea9iW6r9ThF90PAJGCqG20c48cETFve6TNIwIR-58RnLVg5JwGTGcwwapC1vPZD_suSCyZ5YZG9kh5vuca8PuVKeFLgQgKmpjh_XBwjn70VWn3vyH9rm_pfP74d_AkAAP__uQGIdw) by the temporary April 7 truce, pausing the 60-day War Powers Act process that would have required congressional approval for the war as of yesterday's deadline. Democrats dispute that interpretation, noting the War Powers statute contains no provision for pausing the deadline. Pentagon chief Pete Hegseth [offered a different view](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2O6yAUhJ8Gd1j4AP4pKHJ15S7bbLGlRcwhYYPBAhKUt185-wjbjTTSaL7v67yctfMuXD_0hqrYuASs2WMpmJaq0x4rpmxQG-8C3jGUx3p_LcCgp0xSBovRzr8W0RhlEQF5g6obhmHoeg5jc1OXsbMT9p0eLAozjpYLWGEymouLNJI1Th1rTDLoGJMwtHKaLEcrO6EnKcaJCIbGlZja7-hCuaH1r_jE1K5xa7y6lbJnwk8EZgJzrbXdcjg6AjMG-sgE5gOKwLxH74pb3xFD0dcY6Kq9z7S4DeOj0Bho1Yn-YhOYdaKnE8DozEj4HFdnCP__7_CFNX9i2pukzF6d9y73AxHsumnn38_Kn3Q-FfwEAAD__yi2ilM) during testimony before the Senate Armed Services Committee this week, saying the clock can "pause or stop" during a ceasefire. The Senate on Thursday [rejected a sixth attempt](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOszAQhJ_GdKBlbSAuXPwNXf72SrSxl8QXY0fGF4u3P3H3CNeNNKMZffNxXa7kg4_3_7SxKWtaItc9cCmcl0r5lSrn3TG54CM_OZYv-zwWBBxbGFrAxZEPx6IaZ1ZmZNmw6adpmvpR4qV5GOkUSe2YpNK2H0drAW4abxdyIMcVGm_ONhgAe4ABp27QepW8Dr0iPaiLFgrY-ZJy95l8LA9ew5HenDubtiaYRymvXch_AmeBc621s7f9pDh9gfMpBc47Ryrc-kyxrZTbX7LW8ZZspnJGmmzcq_oQ_D5OQsF9Ix9-Vsqfrnkb_A4AAP__YxZ5Fw) by Democrats to pass a war powers resolution. Most Republicans have stood behind the president, though some have indicated they could shift after the deadline passes. Iran made a new proposal for talks, but President Trump on Friday turned it down. _"Iran wants to make a deal, but[I'm not satisfied with it,](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0DGOwyAUBNDT4M4W_gY7FBTbuMu2W1o_8IlJMFiYBOX2K-8VtplmpDfS_FyXK_rg4_0bN9LFpSVSPQKVQnmpmPdUKR-W0AYf6UmxvMzzswCHseWy5bBY9OGziMZqRwQ0NKT7aZqmfhzg0qx6BK4sOEQwgzAWlKObNDenBEkk6BuvT41LDj3nEqZOKuUGcrIXqKS4KCY4WV9S7h7Jx7KSC5_0ptyZtDVBr6XsBxu-GMwM5lprZ-LNnCWD-aQZzFye0TOYS35te5vpQaYcrc8Y253QUGsJQ7eWLTRZ2736EPwxTkzw-4Y-_G2Vfx301vAbAAD__11yeTI)"_ Trump said.
## Stocks Hit Post-Pandemic Highs in April
U.S. stocks closed out April 2026 with their [strongest monthly performance](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LFuhSAYBeCnwU0DvygyMHRxu107Gio_VyqKwf9e4ts39hW6neQk30nO12N62BDD_vy0GxryadqxnBGJME_F5iMVzKdD62LYccWdXvN6TcChr3lXc5icDfGaZOWMRwRsKzRCKaVE38JQLaYX3koxDDPnArTWOLRaSlRt36N3zlXB3BrvOAjOO1BNp7Vv0XdCWt3JQTPJ0QVKuflJYacFfbzSG3Mzp62KZiE6TtZ-MBgZjKWUZt6_57tkMN40g5HLO2sG40lpXuvN5hWppuTsVcfwxvp1OEt4NgttscrGHSXEGM5eMcmfmw3xb43-ddHbwG8AAAD__20OeUE) since the pandemic rebound in 2020. The S&P 500 surged over 10% and closed above 7,200 for the first time, while the Nasdaq Composite jumped more than 15%. Strong earnings from [major tech companies](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkb2O5CAQhJ_GztrC_Bg7cHDJZHvphRYHzcAOBh_0juW3P82N7gk261aV6lOpfn1sHyammO8_zY4r-bJlPFtCIqzbaepRTqzNoXEpZnxgpi_7uDbO-ARMAeObMzFdm-zd6hE5ih7XUWutx0nwuQ8rm_TkF5xngfPIvJJCGqG0cEqaUU--j-srjSnGR8YU14NaFi_Qq1GaRcl56SRDF6nU4bPETAF9usoT62DL3qc1EB2tEz86fuv4zcdsssXhMqGUl6Pjt93UB1Lr-K1RsY_XkeIT___w1oGKMxe4csLn13400IrB8SI2aAcoxiCb5swfyIVsgIq2VAch3kMDKmDNAb-xEewlUwBfKrxx0GK2CJxxBlyIcVRa6iHQnvq6uuOMKcU26U6y-25i-leLvrXFc-V_AwAA__-MLZ6S) fueled the rally, with Alphabet climbing 34% in April for its best month since 2004. Semiconductors were particularly dominant, with the iShares Semiconductor ETF rising about 40%. The gains came despite [volatile oil prices](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL-SsyAUxZ8GOxy8gsaC4mvs8rVbOgiXyIqQgZs4efsd9xW2O3OK8-f3dV_uJsSQHv_NgZp8XhKeNSIRluU05ZlPLNWhcTEk3DHRy-6fBQQMXCguYHEmxM8iG6c9ImDfoO7GcRy7oYdbs2kjb855A70djJp6XGU_Tl5aZ1Ha1Q1N0FeaUAI6IRSMrZom36NXnTSTkreJSYEuUC7tdw6JNvTxk99YWpuPJuqN6FlZ_4_BzGA-z_Pyj9Wk_RKteTGYTaFgI1YG8_Wv5HwwmK9eBrNQDOZX5ZWy3Sv3IYW68RUr8SMn2ngNySIHAaLd6IhN0e55hhhDHUYmxeMwIf6OoT8RfGv4CQAA__-yxoXP) tied to the U.S.-Iran conflict and economic data showing GDP growth below expectations. Hopes for easing Middle East tensions and a robust earnings season kept investor sentiment positive.
## Place Your Bets, It's Kentucky Derby Day
Twenty horses will trot into the Churchill Downs starting gate today for the 152nd Kentucky Derby, the most prestigious event in American thoroughbred racing. The two-minute race starts at 6:57 p.m. ET, and [here's how to watch](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0DHO4yAQBeDT2B0WHptgFxTbuMu2W1oExoENBgsmRr79KpH2BH87Gj297_25r3ftg4_P33pHRVtaI9YSkAjzWnU-UsVcLGobfMQXRnqb17UChxvjgnFYrfbhWsfWqg0RcGhR9VJK2d8GmFqnepRaihFmnCY54bCBBYG92OZxms3waL36pHHBoedcgOzEPG8DbqIf9SzGaW5GjtZTyt3f5CM53MKVTsydSXsblCM6SjP8amBpYKm1dvFhypEylc9HA4tLuSDL2vj4bGD5CL_Xyiixqsk4Rg7ZF_WfyCzmx8XoZMbpGDGw4E9khVDvrJDOxMjvyIpxaN8B26zsUX0IvtxkM_Lnrn34NqQfzXoq-BcAAP__7RuOqw). Renegade and So Happy are [betting favorites](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LHO2yAUxfGnsTcsjMGEgaFLtnTtaGHfS3wbAhaQIL99lUh9gm8_Ovrp_-e23BwFivff7om2-rREbCVgrZiX5vKRGuYC6CBQxAfG-toe5yK4mBlXjIsFHIVzkT1Yjyhw6tGOWms9zpO49Lv1swYDAHoWRjmjOF_RjWoc1SrlpLEn-3njiouRcyX0oIzxE3o1SmeUvJhOcgSqKQ9_E8W6ow9nemMetvTsg91rPUo3_erEtRPX1toQ160cKdfyWXTi-tX-tzPAvJ7Mv0JgnjAASwCFuejCWaiw5Bm6bWd7ygWZi8COjEBbpRRLny0cjUKgMutO8vvTUfg66o_iva34FwAA__8kQ4WS) to win the race (though odds can change up to post time), followed by The Puma, Further Ado, and Commandment. No Derby favorite has won since 2018, marking seven straight years of underdog victories. The Derby is one of the biggest gambling events of the year. Last year's race drew a [record $234 million](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0D1u7CAUxfHV4A4LLv7ABcVr3M1rU1p4uIzJYLDwzSDvPppIWUH6o590_h-35WZDDOnx3-5oyOclYT0jEmFZqi1HrlhOh9bFkPCJib7uz2sBAQMXPRewOBvitXSNMx4RUDVo5DiOoxwU6GYzoHDVoJXDblAgvdeD1N7qu5aDmpRtgnlrohcghehhbPtp8gp9Lzs79Z2eWCfQBcql_cwh0YY-XvmFpb3nvYlmIzpOpv4xmBnMtdZ2zZc7V6TzvWAw7znhxVcknhOnDfnvD-6wrBeDuSnGHTXEGM5hZJ147DbEH5_-FOVl4DsAAP__LXZ2fg) in wagers on the race alone, and $349 million bet across the full Derby Day card.

clean_body: The Trump administration says by the temporary April 7 truce, pausing the 60-day War Powers Act process that would have required congressional approval for the war as of yesterday's deadline. | | | | | | Saturday, May 2, 2026 ---|--- Good morning! On this day in 1933, the was first sighted near Inverness, Scotland, igniting a media frenzy that included a £20,000 reward for capturing "Nessie." It's been a wild week of news, and some of the biggest stories may have flown right under your radar. President Trump hinted that a major UFO file release is coming "in the near future," engineers are testing sound waves to extinguish wildfires, and Saudi investors are pulling the plug on LIV Golf. Tune in to The Flyover Podcast as Amy Hess takes an in-depth look at some of the most interesting stories of the week. ! Mother's Day is right around the corner. If you haven't started shopping, start here: our has thoughtful, practical, and indulgent picks for every kind of mom. There's a difference between worrying and simply being informed. Whether it's a teenage driver, a second car, or an older parent still on the road, having a clear, simple way to stay connected can make a real difference. That's why today's sponsor, . | Trump Rejects War Powers Act Deadline The Trump administration says by the temporary April 7 truce, pausing the 60-day War Powers Act process that would have required congressional approval for the war as of yesterday's deadline. Democrats dispute that interpretation, noting the War Powers statute contains no provision for pausing the deadline. Pentagon chief Pete Hegseth during testimony before the Senate Armed Services Committee this week, saying the clock can "pause or stop" during a ceasefire. The Senate on Thursday by Democrats to pass a war powers resolution. Most Republicans have stood behind the president, though some have indicated they could shift after the deadline passes. Iran made a new proposal for talks, but President Trump on Friday turned it down. "Iran wants to make a deal, but" Trump said. Stocks Hit Post-Pandemic Highs in April U.S. stocks closed out April 2026 with their since the pandemic rebound in 2020. The S&P 500 surged over 10% and closed above 7,200 for the first time, while the Nasdaq Composite jumped more than 15%. Strong earnings from fueled the rally, with Alphabet climbing 34% in April for its best month since 2004. Semiconductors were particularly dominant, with the iShares Semiconductor ETF rising about 40%. The gains came despite tied to the U.S.-Iran conflict and economic data showing GDP growth below expectations. Hopes for easing Middle East tensions and a robust earnings season kept investor sentiment positive. The two-minute race starts at 6:57 p.m. ET, and . Renegade and So Happy are to win the race (though odds can change up to post time), followed by The Puma, Further Ado, and Commandment. No Derby favorite has won since 2018, marking seven straight years of underdog victories. The Derby is one of the biggest gambling events of the year. name: The Maine Wire
subject: Angus King III Misses Golden Opportunity To Break Out Of The Crowded Democrat Gubernatorial Pack
body:
Here's the latest breaking news at The Maine Wire

** Angus King III Misses Golden Opportunity To Break Out Of The Crowded Democrat Gubernatorial Pack (https://mainepolicy.us9.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a975bf67c40a8d8be91ad606d&id=169de91843&e=f093fa355c)
------------------------------------------------------------

name: The Texas Flyover
subject: Texas Targets Ghost Offices, Austin Beer Wins Big, and Big Texan Turns 66
body:
Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton [opened an investigation](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0L2u4yAQhuGrwR0W4B_igmIbd9l2S2tsDxgFQwSzdnL3R3Eu4fSjV898_-7THXzw0f2FHQ29bJoiniUgEeaJ8AWFIDuk4rZUKFnrFywwKaF6Ljou1LSCD--prVYzi3WQFRqptdZy0MOt2gzi3Glhe93oFq2VyzLfFrhZrVqthG0qbz4x0QklpWikrtWgoZMr9gNagFmyVuDqKeWaNrxINrzTgble0l4FsxE9C2v-MDUyNZ7nWT8ww-eNOmXH1Oj33bsM5FNkavzSW94IpsYrx4Eo5Yhv7jBihsAfGPkTXpQijynTxr93G5czP3wBbjP8X7mPBxby7kpX2azP04fgS69ZK9wOPlxE-t2wh1E_AQAA__94spBX) into nearly 30 North Texas businesses accused of using fake "ghost office" addresses to fraudulently sponsor foreign workers through the H-1B visa program.

| | |
---
| [](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkMFu6yAURL_G3sWCi23CgkX0ntxNXKnqoksLw8VGJSGC2zj5-8r5ha7naGZ0vsZpNCGG6_JuLqjp4dN0xa1EJMI8ET5MIZMXpLKsqVDyPlgsZgIG_YF1BwaTMyE-p7Z2emZO8Ro1l1JKrqQ61qvuoQNUkuHsOy64b521wiprxbE7MqvqoPcy1jHgnAkuG1DSdNxhr9AbM_OqZegCpdzQiq9LPj7THXNj06WOeiW6lUqcKhgqGLZta8wcgVH-3oEKBjh__BN7du7exlHsnBhK-skWp-Aq8Z9WHwkdOsE-8Qbtqc7a3bYQYyi9rFq2XEyIrzn6m6S7ht8AAAD__0Bdego) | |
# Saturday, May 2, 2026
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in 1948, country singer [Larry Gatlin](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGOwyAQBNCvgS4RbGwIBcU16XLtlWgdFhsJmwhWSfz3p-Q-4eoZPY3m5xqumEve5m9cyfMr1bDRsxdiphaYXtgZ20zc56V2rinlG3UMoMAc1HhQECLmsodBRj-p6LQkr621VjvrznLx9jxosIhmGjUCnqO5GUgK0zA55U5RZv_G1KhAa3XS9gjO4qgjGUcJcdJiUBQz13bkhT6TUtnrg9rxVldZ_MJ87-L0JeAi4DIjl7xNrfJCrb8rAi4FW9sPf5FsPt6fuZTcjRWDmlfM5UPx_w54ePgNAAD__3DPcQ4) was born in Seminole. He and his brothers, Rudy and Steve, formed a vocal group known for hits like _Broken Lady_ , _All the Gold in California_ , and _Houston (Means I 'm One Day Closer to You)_. It's been a wild week of news, and some of the biggest stories may have flown right under your radar. President Trump hinted that a major UFO file release is coming "in the near future," engineers are using sound waves to extinguish wildfires, and Saudi investors are pulling the plug on LIV Golf. Tune in to _The Flyover Podcast_ as Amy Hess takes an in-depth look at some of the most interesting stories of the week. [Tune in here](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykjzuOwyAQQE9julgDsRlTUGyTLttuicZhsJFIiGCUz-1XmyvsAd7Tez_ncKZc8m37pit7eaUabvzshUW4BeEXdaG2sfRtr11qSvnCnYIBYw8wH8CESLm8w6SiXyE6rdhrRETt0C1q9xdYrMXkNGGaFgJOdl0IyGBMaOdJZf8ngxmM1nDUOBqHNOvI1nEiWvUwAccstY2y8ycplXd9cBsv9aqK30XufTh-DeY0mFPjtdEtjuU9mNO9Rn5JI62aj_dnLiV3i8ME25Vy-fDyv-uHN78BAAD__3uHbIE)! Mother's Day is right around the corner. If you haven't started shopping, start here: our [2026 Gift Guide](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0DuOMyEQBODTzGS2GoYBExD8iTP_6YaobZqHxBgLev24_crW3mDjKn0q1dfJn7DUck3_cSPHz9j8lR6jEjN1z_TEwdgT8Ui5DW4xlgsN9BKk3sG6A-kDlvryag7uDMGKmZwwxhhhjT3M2ekQV7BCLFKCWtSBVm0hLFYZXKLVai7ujcEKUghYhNlLa3AVgbSliHgWkwIKhVvfc6bPpFhf7U59f2nbXF1mvo1p-TfJ4ySPnOk3TiVy-i6B3r1JHrfGmfrYBXzN3YXbo9RahjaTgrRhqR-O_3bC3cmfAAAA__9q7XGu) has thoughtful, practical, and indulgent picks for every kind of mom. Finding a Mother's Day gift that actually feels thoughtful can be harder than it should be.
