| | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

More details in Chris's message below. See it and take preventive steps fast.

Martin

\---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Chris Graebe
Sent: Thursday, April 09, 2025 9:45 AM
America's rapidly surging debt is no secret.

But for years, Wall Street and Washington have treated our $38 trillion national debt like a problem for tomorrow.

A crisis they can just keep kicking down the road.

**However, the conflict in the Middle East over the last two weeks just violently accelerated the timeline.**

With the Strait of Hormuz locked down, oil is surging. And analysts are predicting $150 a barrel if this drags on.

When oil spikes like that, inflation roars back into the economy.

In the past, the government would try to print, cut, or borrow its way out of an inflation shock.

**But you cannot do that when you're sitting on a $38 trillion mountain of debt and paying $1 trillion a year as interest on it.**

In short, this match has just hit a powder keg.

And it's going to trigger a radical, violent shift in the U.S. stock market.

Popular household stocks that looked untouchable a month ago could get gutted. And another set of overlooked stocks could go for massive, historic runs.

That's why I rushed to get this special broadcast live this morning.

Inside, I pull back the curtain on a 100-year-old market signal.

It's the exact same data-driven signal that called the bank collapses of the 1980s, the 2008 financial crisis, and the 2020 crash.

clean_body: Martin Weiss here. Earlier today, my colleague Chris Graebe sent you the message below. I asked my team to forward it to you again tonight for one specific reason. | | | Don't want to receive our publications? | | | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18] | | | | | | Martin Weiss here. clean_body: | | | Friend, we are just ONE MONTH out from the primary that will decide if Deb becomes New Mexico’s Democratic nominee for governor. With early voting starting next week, this is an "all hands on deck" moment for our campaign. | Deb is a fierce and experienced leader, and she's got what it takes to move New Mexico toward a future where everyone can thrive. But we can't ignore the fact that her opponents are running lies and misinformation ads across the airwaves, and unlike her opponents, she can’t just write our campaign a big check to keep her ads on the air that set the record straight. In fact, her MAGA-funded Democratic opponent’s average donation is $1,670, and his average gift from Trump donors is $9,838. That should tell you everything you need to know about the difference in our campaigns: our movement is powered by people, not billionaires and insiders. I’ve said it before, and I’ll keep saying it: This is a people-powered campaign, and it always will be. And as the youngest member of Congress, I know how tight money is for so many of us — rent, groceries, rising utility bills just keep piling up. That’s exactly why our work matters. Republicans across the country are preparing to gerrymander more maps as they plan to cheat their way into keeping power.** They don’t realize that while they’re trying to rig votes, we’re organizing to earn them. Hi, it’s Jasmine Clark — microbiologist, Emory professor, mom, and Democratic State Representative in the Georgia General Assembly. I know you’ve heard from me a lot over the last few days and weeks, but I wanted to personally reach out to say **_thank you_**. Thanks to your support, we exceeded our fundraising goal in April! I am running to represent Georgia’s 13th District in the U.S. House of Representatives because I believe it’s time we had a scientific voice in Congress. I will never stop fighting for this district, and for Georgia, and I couldn’t do it without grassroots champions like you on my team. Thanks for your support, Dr. Jasmine ClarkDemocratic Candidate for Georgia's 13th District

subject: Hero cop jailed for saving lives
body:
| [ ](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=fcc958decca7472aaeb4eac771054b82&_e=KXGfc5NkIe0RdDLvZq_d-kQgngMC4mHcMSorCOV88mootAJfZ1-qd2j37PahRIKM0T9QhAU_Yq-e1TTJ7seOwRiF2G_HY1MuLm-7xaSw2FMEpXCmZzyBLA19N2vlfiAMpx3lFZKG6-MLLzFxodihYSoBTxk_kA1pYF8_BGzkccN5l_79ABTpmCvVepWB66DQUQgudzj1G65-552Qj6sVvwWgzzkyPfwinPd-babGBYZ-ahUir-cR2OCLNz7jPtELLqOukyvC0dnMa6YZ0W-5iMbymwK7O_jqEd7MhGqbRQOcrFRNv1O3AQgLbd_ZZE0B_md2PxFDtAU4jDoKWqu8uDvXn_hd2bqh2OhVvPfgZ5DywM5uaD9cAY-egi1rpm4pF3y7eYDPvsMREeU_KglLIg7Kk7kDJ0VYh3chVAfAulloMviLwFZ6GqLlMMGmhdAh)
---

Dear Friend,

Imagine this: It's a dark night in Newark, New Jersey. A young officer, just 26 years old, father of four, races to back up his brothers in blue. A dangerous chase unfolds – armed thugs in a speeding car, weaving through streets at 70 mph the wrong way, endangering innocent families. These criminals point a loaded semi-automatic right at Officer Jovanny Crespo's face. They try to mow him down like roadkill. In a split-second decision, the kind no desk-jockey prosecutor could ever understand, Officer Crespo acts to protect himself, his fellow officers, and the public. He stops the threat. Lives are saved. But what does he get in return? Handcuffs. Indictment. A sham trial where he's branded a "murderer" and "killer" by radicals who hate the badge. | [Help Free JOVANNY](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=fcc958decca7472aaeb4eac771054b82&_e=KXGfc5NkIe0RdDLvZq_d-kQgngMC4mHcMSorCOV88mootAJfZ1-qd2j37PahRIKM0T9QhAU_Yq-e1TTJ7seOwRiF2G_HY1MuLm-7xaSw2FPI63iEBWJWf3e49WccQRuRO-mCCSfyd8O6XrWCuBfmrejXOqDSLhQWO5MPVANc1DZn5oLe75Iwkjy0lP2ZNjqvvSn9g0mg1xCFjMtZeijTAAdbaAnS09vWAhw4g0uU6j89u1dp4JD7866Wy-ioB6f61fbDhgD-F11bazCybuQ-ZtcyN6Z6sizcNynwfGhy6Sluhz73FLGivxbs-OYOwhsDwufDuzh-v9B7zBJNrYZyBa19Cu5uvfZTs5BDKcO7QkLW2QQ0zBWGIcGiE-Gtb8cHTrtNySV7r2Bv5PjSqXDIb7HzBaInIKk3ArYIgj0j-jA8uT0wCeolndS72DNDHRFt)
---

_Jovanny and his children_

That's right. Officer Crespo was slapped with aggravated manslaughter charges, found guilty, and sentenced to a staggering 27 YEARS behind bars. Twenty-seven years! For doing his job. While the real criminals—drug-pushing, gun-waving menaces—get a free pass in the court of public opinion.
This isn't justice. This is a witch hunt. Anti-police zealots are weaponizing the system to destroy heroes like Jovanny. He's lost his pay, his freedom, and now fights for his life in appeals court. His four kids? They grow up without their dad because some ivory-tower elites decided cops are the enemy.
[Rush $25 to Defend Jovanny](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=fcc958decca7472aaeb4eac771054b82&_e=KXGfc5NkIe0RdDLvZq_d-kQgngMC4mHcMSorCOV88mootAJfZ1-qd2j37PahRIKM0T9QhAU_Yq-e1TTJ7seOwRiF2G_HY1MuLm-7xaSw2FMgWGlhdFVncCyUqHb1s8rizbEkcBRuJfe19ZR6oORBiNcz2CIWAr6DOj7gErTwtw13xyc4aHxTQftMWDJnPTGaRfr4Tij1hOPIUfa4l20ZLEYGatMCYv2942FuPYutG1iIXqGszhsZZUS38y26p8q9onxmv5_TEkV62PNUYJbRc2t24LuT28V6Xx9UDBJyErfauCI0y45zIXK599wvmUje8e1uTje4dhid0gAKGweuh2D7Zez5irZpRdEnLwHJt1mw6OlmDTEQWB6hBiqJFpzMJBjD9yn0hlLcTqWg9icWCr_btSmyXh7ox3rPVndSDI8rR7Xi4ywQv-UiKKsYdC8w)
---
I've seen this garbage firsthand. As America's Toughest Sheriff, I locked up the bad guys and stood tall against the liberal mob. Now, the National Center for Police Defense is stepping up for Officer Crespo – funding his appeal, supporting his family, and battling this grave injustice. But we can't do it alone.
Your donation today fuels the fight. It says, "Enough!" to the war on cops. It ensures brave officers like Jovanny get the defense they deserve, not the betrayal they endure.

name: =?UTF-8?Q?Eduardo_Ver=C3=A1stegui?=
subject: She was locked in an attic for months… until this happened
body:
A rescue team changed Kristen’s life. Another mission begins in Phoenix.

| [View in Web Browser](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e7855488/VaHR0cHM6Ly93cm8ubnVjbGV1c2VtYWlsLmNvbS9hbXBsaWZ5L3YvNWpzY3IyNDhjeQ/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNjA1MDI/LbmNsYW9CU2czbg/qP2hpZHM9bmNyR0JDZXZXRjlSMGVscw/gafYk1Q/s1p77ffe35f)
| |
---
| |
---
| GOD'S CHILDREN ARE NOT FOR SALE
---
|
---
| | Friend, No one could hear her. A young girl named Kristen was locked in an attic. No windows. No fresh air. No way out. The door was locked from the outside. For months she lived there.
Alone. There was no bathroom.
Just a bucket. No shower. No clean clothes. And the only time she left that attic was when she was sold for sex.
Over. And over. And over. **This is the reality of child trafficking.**
---
[](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
[ Send help to children waiting ](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
Millions of children are trapped in exploitation around the world. But Kristen’s story did not end in that attic. One night she knocked on a hotel room door. Her trafficker had arranged and forced her into another “date.” But instead...**she met a rescue team - Freedom Strike Force.** They were working alongside law enforcement to identify victims and traffickers. When Kristen realized she was safe... She collapsed into the arms of one of our female advocates. **Free.** Law enforcement then raided the house where she was held. Traffickers were arrested. Kristen was taken to a safe place. For the first time in months, she had a real bed and a hot meal**.**

**She took the first step toward healing.** **This rescue was possible because people chose to act.**
---
[ Send help to children waiting ](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
The **Sound of Freedom Foundation** exists to _stop_ ___the sale_ _of God’s children._ Inspired by the film _Sound of Freedom_ , the Foundation works around the world to **prevent trafficking, support rescue efforts, educate families, raise awareness, and push for stronger laws that protect children.** Our mission is simple. **To stop the sale of God’s children.** And right now we are preparing a new mission, happening in just a few weeks in Phoenix. Our **Freedom Strike Force** partner will train local law enforcement for two days, teaching them how to identify traffickers and victims hidden in plain sight. Then on day three...they go into the field. **Real operations. Real arrests. Real rescues.**
---
| | [](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
| [](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
| | Children like Kristen are waiting. But these missions cannot happen without support from people who care.
---
[ Send help to children waiting ](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
That is why I am reaching out to you today. [](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)[](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.) **[Would you consider making your first gift of $30, $60, $130 or whatever you can do right now to help rescue children from trafficking?](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)** Your donation today can help:
---
* Train officers to find trafficked children
* Support rescue operations
* Provide food, clothing, and care for survivors
* Help children begin the journey to healing

---
When I first heard the stories of trafficked children, I asked myself a question. _What if it were my child?_ I could not stay silent.
And you do not have to either. **Friend, will you please help rescue the next child today?** Your gift — whatever God places on your heart — will help bring freedom to children still waiting in the dark. **Together we can make sure more children hear the Sound of Freedom.** With gratitude and prayer,
---
[](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
Eduardo Verástegui
Sound of Freedom, Producer
For the Sound of Freedom Foundation
---
**P.S.** The cost to staff, equip, and execute a **Freedom Strike Force** **Training Camp** is $25,000. To raise these funds, I need Freedom Fighters like you to rally in support and sponsor this mission.

**Please pray about what you can do to help and donate today.**
---
[ Support the Sound of Freedom Foundation ](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.)
---
6671 Southwest Freeway, Suite 700, Houston TX 77074
---

This email was sent to **[dpwillis67@gmail.com](mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com)** as part of your subscription to **America First War Room**

108 S Washington St. Suite 200
Alexandria, VA 22314

[Unsubscribe](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e7855488/VaHR0cHM6Ly93cm8ubnVjbGV1c2VtYWlsLmNvbS9hbXBsaWZ5L3N1YnNjcmlwdGlvbnMvdW5zdWJzY3JpYmU/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNjA1MDI/LbmNsYW9CU2czbg/qP2hpZHM9bmNyR0JDZXZXRjlSMGVscw/gafYk1Q/s1ib5b3a319)

Hey folks,
This one's a must-watch if you're trying to understand the cracks forming inside MAGA media right now. Adam Mockler and I go deep on the meltdown happening across the right-wing ecosystem--and why it's accelerating fast. We break down what's really driving the collapse, and why independent media is stepping into the void.
* **[00:01:35]** The MAGA media meltdown: how collapsing narratives and a failing war effort are creating chaos across right-wing outlets
* **[00:03:00]** The Daily Wire's decline and subscriber losses--what the numbers reveal about a shifting media landscape
* **[00:07:01]** The question no one in MAGA can answer: what has the Iran war actually achieved?
* **[00:10:03]** Why MAGA figures crumble under pressure--and what happens when they're challenged outside their bubble
* **[00:21:02]** The political stakes ahead: Senate control, Supreme Court implications, and why this moment matters more than people think

This conversation pulls back the curtain on a media ecosystem that's losing its grip--and shows exactly why independent voices are gaining ground. If you want clarity in the chaos and real talk without the spin, this is essential viewing.

body:
Worst-case scenario
| MAJOR HOUSE MAJORITY SHAKEUP
We could be headed toward a complete disaster in the Midterms based on these 3 critical updates:
1. Democrats are targeting 35 vulnerable GOP seats across the country, even in deep-red MAGA territory.
2. National Democrats have a $50+ MILLION warchest to target Conservatives in key battleground races.
3. President Trump himself warned all Conservatives: "If we don't win the midterms... they'll find a reason to impeach me."
We're pleading with you to take this seriously. Democrats are regrouping faster than anyone could've predicted, and they're working to obliterate our pro-Trump House Majority.
DON'T FORGET: In nearly every election since 2024, Democrats outperformed, outraised, and outmaneuvered Conservatives.
DON'T FORGET: The Generic Ballot has Democrats 4+ points over Republicans.
DON'T FORGET: If we lose the House, Democrats will immediately move to impeach President Trump and his entire Cabinet in one fell swoop.
Democrats are betting you ignore our warning - every second we wait, Democrats are only picking up momentum, soaring in the polls, and raising millions more dollars to destroy everything President Trump worked so hard for.
Kellyanne Conway, Senator John Kennedy, VP Vance, Newt Gingrich, and nearly a dozen other MAGA Patriots all reached out warning you to take this seriously. name: Gary Sinise, American Veterans Center
subject: A historic opportunity to honor veterans this Memorial Day
body:
I believe that while we can never do enough to express our gratitude to our defenders, veterans, and first responders, we can always do a little mor

| | |
---
Friend,
---
It's Gary Sinise here. You may know me from my roles in movies like Apollo 13 _ _ and The Green Mile or the CBS television series CSI: NY - but it was in 1994 playing the role of Lt. Dan in Forrest Gump that changed my life and inspired my enduring connection to our defenders, veterans, and first responders. I've served as an Honorary Grand Marshal for the National Memorial Day Parade in Washington, D.C. nearly every year since American Veterans Center revived it in 2005. **But I've never been more excited to serve as an Honorary Grand Marshall than I am this year - the year of America's 250****th**** birthday.**
---
[](https://email.updates.conservativeinnovation.com/c/eJyUkb2O3DAMhJ9G7nahv7XXhYogiwVS3LUpBZri2UQsyZFkB_f2gS9XprmO4AyH-DA_X_wL8MppfoVIDg_2pXpYuLZcGPO25dL2xO295SUnr6XuL_J2kfr06S44DL3RHTk1DMNgRqttt7jpbkYFZCc9EpGapmnEu30b--Et2En2HbszSd6kVr2R2lwD3TGoGwx3MBaVElbuW4BG9Yo5VSoHND6IU8rnlNMVc-xWt7S2VWG-Cf0U-lkJ90JXSBRyOx1CPyFSYYR0OahRgVQvSKlROaUDP4j8DOXdR4q5MKzCPGveC5LHHEiYx_cfr0qqm5ZC93uLPlLgPQrzoAi8fi7_nQjzWKAEqL884O9PCSFuwHMS5vH_j11xYfvD68q1H4SV85n7Adi-Xsnh9N8AAAD__6gLn0g)
---
Friend, this Memorial Day marks the first major national act of remembrance of the veterans who made the ultimate sacrifice to defend America from its enemies and ensure it has remained the Land of the Free for the last 250 years. And this year's National Memorial Day Parade will be a special edition - the biggest parade yet - where national memory meets living history as veterans from every military generation celebrate America and honor the service and sacrifice of their fallen brethren. **Friend, the National Memorial Day Parade is ENIRELY FUNDED by private contributions, including from generous patriots like you.** **[In light of America's 250th birthday and the special opportunity we have to honor veterans this year, will you chip in today to help support the parade with a gift of $25, $50, $75, $150, or more?](https://email.updates.conservativeinnovation.com/c/eJyUkb2O3DAMhJ9G7nahv7XXhYogiwVS3LUpBZri2UQsyZFkB_f2gS9XprmO4AyH-DA_X_wL8MppfoVIDg_2pXpYuLZcGPO25dL2xO295SUnr6XuL_J2kfr06S44DL3RHTk1DMNgRqttt7jpbkYFZCc9EpGapmnEu30b--Et2En2HbszSd6kVr2R2lwD3TGoGwx3MBaVElbuW4BG9Yo5VSoHND6IU8rnlNMVc-xWt7S2VWG-Cf0U-lkJ90JXSBRyOx1CPyFSYYR0OahRgVQvSKlROaUDP4j8DOXdR4q5MKzCPGveC5LHHEiYx_cfr0qqm5ZC93uLPlLgPQrzoAi8fi7_nQjzWKAEqL884O9PCSFuwHMS5vH_j11xYfvD68q1H4SV85n7Adi-Xsnh9N8AAAD__6gLn0g)**
---

The 2026 National Memorial Day Parade is a once-in-a-lifetime moment where we need to do all we can to honor our country and our veterans. _Bigger_ crowds. _More_ veterans. _More _young people learning what courage, service, and sacrifice really mean.
---
[](https://email.updates.conservativeinnovation.com/c/eJyUkb2O3DAMhJ9G7nahv7XXhYogiwVS3LUpBZri2UQsyZFkB_f2gS9XprmO4AyH-DA_X_wL8MppfoVIDg_2pXpYuLZcGPO25dL2xO295SUnr6XuL_J2kfr06S44DL3RHTk1DMNgRqttt7jpbkYFZCc9EpGapmnEu30b--Et2En2HbszSd6kVr2R2lwD3TGoGwx3MBaVElbuW4BG9Yo5VSoHND6IU8rnlNMVc-xWt7S2VWG-Cf0U-lkJ90JXSBRyOx1CPyFSYYR0OahRgVQvSKlROaUDP4j8DOXdR4q5MKzCPGveC5LHHEiYx_cfr0qqm5ZC93uLPlLgPQrzoAi8fi7_nQjzWKAEqL884O9PCSFuwHMS5vH_j11xYfvD68q1H4SV85n7Adi-Xsnh9N8AAAD__6gLn0g)
---
Historically, AVC has been able to do this at a fraction of what other major public events cost to put on each year. In fact, _the entire parade_ costs about as much as just THREE balloons in the Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade or TWO floats in the Rose Bowl Parade! _That means that every dollar that's contributed by a generous American like you makes a difference for this important event._ **But I want to reiterate that American Veterans Center does not take ANY federal funding for this event. It is funded ****_entirely_**** by private citizens like you who want to honor those who have sacrificed so much for our country.** **[Friend, as we look ahead to this historic event, can I count on your generous gift of $25, $50, $75, $150, or more?](https://email.updates.conservativeinnovation.com/c/eJyUkb2O3DAMhJ9G7nahv7XXhYogiwVS3LUpBZri2UQsyZFkB_f2gS9XprmO4AyH-DA_X_wL8MppfoVIDg_2pXpYuLZcGPO25dL2xO295SUnr6XuL_J2kfr06S44DL3RHTk1DMNgRqttt7jpbkYFZCc9EpGapmnEu30b--Et2En2HbszSd6kVr2R2lwD3TGoGwx3MBaVElbuW4BG9Yo5VSoHND6IU8rnlNMVc-xWt7S2VWG-Cf0U-lkJ90JXSBRyOx1CPyFSYYR0OahRgVQvSKlROaUDP4j8DOXdR4q5MKzCPGveC5LHHEiYx_cfr0qqm5ZC93uLPlLgPQrzoAi8fi7_nQjzWKAEqL884O9PCSFuwHMS5vH_j11xYfvD68q1H4SV85n7Adi-Xsnh9N8AAAD__6gLn0g)[](https://email.updates.conservativeinnovation.com/c/eJyUkb2O3DAMhJ9G7nahv7XXhYogiwVS3LUpBZri2UQsyZFkB_f2gS9XprmO4AyH-DA_X_wL8MppfoVIDg_2pXpYuLZcGPO25dL2xO295SUnr6XuL_J2kfr06S44DL3RHTk1DMNgRqttt7jpbkYFZCc9EpGapmnEu30b--Et2En2HbszSd6kVr2R2lwD3TGoGwx3MBaVElbuW4BG9Yo5VSoHND6IU8rnlNMVc-xWt7S2VWG-Cf0U-lkJ90JXSBRyOx1CPyFSYYR0OahRgVQvSKlROaUDP4j8DOXdR4q5MKzCPGveC5LHHEiYx_cfr0qqm5ZC93uLPlLgPQrzoAi8fi7_nQjzWKAEqL884O9PCSFuwHMS5vH_j11xYfvD68q1H4SV85n7Adi-Xsnh9N8AAAD__6gLn0g)**

[ **Support the National Memorial Day Parade**](https://email.updates.conservativeinnovation.com/c/eJyUkb2O3DAMhJ9G7nahv7XXhYogiwVS3LUpBZri2UQsyZFkB_f2gS9XprmO4AyH-DA_X_wL8MppfoVIDg_2pXpYuLZcGPO25dL2xO295SUnr6XuL_J2kfr06S44DL3RHTk1DMNgRqttt7jpbkYFZCc9EpGapmnEu30b--Et2En2HbszSd6kVr2R2lwD3TGoGwx3MBaVElbuW4BG9Yo5VSoHND6IU8rnlNMVc-xWt7S2VWG-Cf0U-lkJ90JXSBRyOx1CPyFSYYR0OahRgVQvSKlROaUDP4j8DOXdR4q5MKzCPGveC5LHHEiYx_cfr0qqm5ZC93uLPlLgPQrzoAi8fi7_nQjzWKAEqL884O9PCSFuwHMS5vH_j11xYfvD68q1H4SV85n7Adi-Xsnh9N8AAAD__6gLn0g)

[](https://email.updates.conservativeinnovation.com/c/eJyUkb2O3DAMhJ9G7nahv7XXhYogiwVS3LUpBZri2UQsyZFkB_f2gS9XprmO4AyH-DA_X_wL8MppfoVIDg_2pXpYuLZcGPO25dL2xO295SUnr6XuL_J2kfr06S44DL3RHTk1DMNgRqttt7jpbkYFZCc9EpGapmnEu30b--Et2En2HbszSd6kVr2R2lwD3TGoGwx3MBaVElbuW4BG9Yo5VSoHND6IU8rnlNMVc-xWt7S2VWG-Cf0U-lkJ90JXSBRyOx1CPyFSYYR0OahRgVQvSKlROaUDP4j8DOXdR4q5MKzCPGveC5LHHEiYx_cfr0qqm5ZC93uLPlLgPQrzoAi8fi7_nQjzWKAEqL884O9PCSFuwHMS5vH_j11xYfvD68q1H4SV85n7Adi-Xsnh9N8AAAD__6gLn0g)
---
| | Your support of the American Veterans Center not only helps the parade, but also supports great programs like:
---
* Record hundreds of oral histories of veterans from World War II to today before we lose these stories forever.

* Hold our annual American Veterans Conference, which allows young cadets a chance to hear first-hand from veterans from WWII, Korea, Vietnam, and our modern wars.

* Produce veteran-focused T.V. documentaries and specials that remind Americans the price of our freedom.

Dan in Forrest Gump that changed my life and inspired my enduring connection to our defenders, veterans, and first responders. I've served as an Honorary Grand Marshal for the National Memorial Day Parade in Washington, D.C. nearly every year since American Veterans Center revived it in 2005. name: Ximena, United We Dream
subject: We got each other
body:
We’re powered by fierce determination and unrelenting resistance. | | | Peter,
Wage theft and labor exploitation often affect young immigrants. Those that work in low-wage industries like garment manufacturing, agriculture, food services, and domestic work are more exposed to these issues. Undocumented workers face subminimum wages, unpaid overtime, and employer intimidation, including threats of deportation. DACA recipients have seen the benefits they have fought hard for and won, such as access to higher education, jobs, and health care, being systematically weakened and put at risk. At the same time, agencies that safeguard workers’ rights are losing funding, making their work to fight for worker protections, rights, wages, and safety more difficult. Workers who speak up or try to report workplace abuses are threatened with retaliation and punished. All to boost the billionaire class. **Whether you’re an immigrant or a citizen, if you’re a working person in this country, it is up to us to fight with and for each other.** The sooner we come together and realize that we, as working people, have always been on the same side, the sooner we can fight back united. _They’re betting on us playing it small and staying divided. But we got each other._ The strategy is clear. If they can keep us down, burdened by debt and struggling to make ends meet, we won’t be able to see the bigger picture: that there are millions of us going through the exact same situation — on the same team — all across the country, and they’re watching us suffer over breadcrumbs. **We’ll keep fighting.** United We Dream is bringing working people together — especially young people — who are ready to meet this moment with courage to create a better future for each other, where we can all be free to thrive. **Your support powers our work to protect and uplift immigrant communities, resist budget cuts, and build the economy and future we need.** **[We know what our people need, and we’re organizing our communities to fight for it. If you’re inspired by what we can accomplish together for immigrant workers, will you make a donation today?](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqswjENXoYacFGtlMLXh6NAeIdicNPDEjB8U81Sf47SIVCDx-HE-ebYoA0wSAVuKxM59fV67dJ-i2vYlM3L6zC_cSkSRiYWDhZ5xU76c-tuTMeKm3Or6yEXF77DNsNQkPyiIHRl5Cl29YvSrqXGAg3J4d7SBdh6kU5XC9VtHFnVrnjUGsY5R_eQe4Qw7xaCL3_NKva7BJ92TQXN0ZSKxeSNeObNa1gBGs-CZ7Pr7MMpxi1NlDVD-wDITb10nXcwdIOG63bF9LU6FoUg7CcklkZsiK4kl5w0Anon9zsRoiiKM_8kyGNQsu3irvNtPpJIdeXhxaRy41QfhsPWZq3cZsjB1TUU0vX0SyR07o3sJidOqkze1owtdB2WuOk0QR8vMBYI/4qa/gTjgVz2tQd2xySFzpnjzkQ/h0/h001.X5R5AJ5qh5EarJa0BwuWVTBAAZ7BJrE0CEpNcR2qlYQ)** [****](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqswjENXoYacFGtlMLXh6NAeIdicNPDEjB8U81Sf47SIVCDx-HE-ebYoA0wSAVuKxM59fV67dJ-i2vYlM3L6zC_cSkSRiYWDhZ5xU76c-tuTMUflZG30_FxqOovBppuoHXk-6K7qdBXtJsfFzj3IGDgzGBgnxF3TlsYgbXvL-TxU1HD3eAENTfMJHPAYgsRfgxifSZH2IZs6DFSDc6mTrPd1KDuT_B1SY_L_FnkJeloFGwCQkcFtj3dOn38HuufoR8NPti9iu0P3y-yFQKGI4f6NNLfu-7CrmTmNf1cwbXLULeeaPQfcX-3hiF7fM4inTAI_c_kMACmYf4x5w-q5Erl4sHRRb5aJgLsjXNf9LhJljrUZftAix1s8j8hPtJmhHzE/4qa/gTjgVz2tQd2xySFzpnjzkQ/h1/h001.yyl-Cu0mXMdobxfqewDbHhImqPFBLuLvitl6CF6oKa0) _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | | [express donate: $10](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqswjENXoYacFGtlMLXh6NAeIdicNPDEjB8U81Sf47SIVCDx-HE-ebYoA0wSAVuKxM59fV67dJ-i2vYlM3L6zC_cDqUyIwCvt_DMbDjHJAPU8g8aqj1q57V23yiGtwKxPxcufeJ-m0nfrCUqtv2Mycl0LF6aoMVFYJK-pN79wb3ma-vIpOONaLxbH4znEN3GkTHPMeZPS5FIaSQ-Fclpia1uI5EHcgb81Y_nFvfdDklLAIuuDkJ2eqVGkIaAUTTF8cj7LC04CDpp_Je5bWW_iHXQVRKiYZzGi-J36Rd140XcxE8DV11kVIEP95Zjw8V_FZUiTvradG3i0u7E9RLj4T-vjY6lgT5xWFIJ17hhsHVBgbAx_zaQ2P5as_L3HTJ_41Jc9iK5VPRKA-0IpGOqrR9kOuEJqv_k9cTp_RsKXi8hpA/4qa/gTjgVz2tQd2xySFzpnjzkQ/h2/h001.nPNxgaLsIStS_zSNi8nHNB2sx8ml6XPJuSEyLOZ4GeM)

clean_body: We’re powered by fierce determination and unrelenting resistance. | | | Peter, Wage theft and labor exploitation often affect young immigrants. Those that work in low-wage industries like garment manufacturing, agriculture, food services, and domestic work are more exposed to these issues. Undocumented workers face subminimum wages, unpaid overtime, and employer intimidation, including threats of deportation. DACA recipients have seen the benefits they have fought hard for and won, such as access to higher education, jobs, and health care, being systematically weakened and put at risk. At the same time, agencies that safeguard workers’ rights are losing funding, making their work to fight for worker protections, rights, wages, and safety more difficult. Workers who speak up or try to report workplace abuses are threatened with retaliation and punished. All to boost the billionaire class. Whether you’re an immigrant or a citizen, if you’re a working person in this country, it is up to us to fight with and for each other. The sooner we come together and realize that we, as working people, have always been on the same side, the sooner we can fight back united. They’re betting on us playing it small and staying divided. But we got each other. The strategy is clear. If they can keep us down, burdened by debt and struggling to make ends meet, we won’t be able to see the bigger picture: that there are millions of us going through the exact same situation — on the same team — all across the country, and they’re watching us suffer over breadcrumbs. We’ll keep fighting. United We Dream is bringing working people together — especially young people — who are ready to meet this moment with courage to create a better future for each other, where we can all be free to thrive. Your support powers our work to protect and uplift immigrant communities, resist budget cuts, and build the economy and future we need. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | | | | | | | | Thank you, Ximena, United We Dream Follow us on: | | | | |

body:
# [](https://act.mcmorrowformichigan.com/go/685?refcode=&t=1&akid=4604%2E898137%2EfyCqSX)
Hey there,
April was a tough fundraising month. We haven’t been able to keep the pace we need at this point in the election.
Meanwhile, Mike Rogers and his MAGA billionaire backers? _They’re airing ads on TV, spending millions to win in November._
This isn’t the news I wanted to share today. But we can turn this around. We just need everyone to step up right now. [**Can you donate $5 today to help us start May on the right foot?**](https://act.mcmorrowformichigan.com/go/6387?t=2&akid=4604%2E898137%2EfyCqSX)
I know, I know — $5 doesn't sound like much compared to the millions Rogers is spending.
But here's why it matters: $5 from you + $5 from the person reading this after you + $5 from thousands of supporters = how we fight back against the MAGA money pouring into Michigan.
The polls are clear: We CAN beat Mike Rogers and keep this seat blue. But it’s going to be a nail-biter.
When we have the resources to reach every voter in Michigan with our message, we win. _But only if we don 't fall further behind. _
[**Can you donate $5 (or whatever works for you) right now? If everyone reading this contributes, we 'll be back on track.**](https://act.mcmorrowformichigan.com/go/6387?t=3&akid=4604%2E898137%2EfyCqSX)

clean_body: Hey there, April was a tough fundraising month. We haven’t been able to keep the pace we need at this point in the election. Meanwhile, Mike Rogers and his MAGA billionaire backers? They’re airing ads on TV, spending millions to win in November. This isn’t the news I wanted to share today. But we can turn this around. We just need everyone to step up right now. I know, I know — $5 doesn't sound like much compared to the millions Rogers is spending. But here's why it matters: $5 from you + $5 from the person reading this after you + $5 from thousands of supporters = how we fight back against the MAGA money pouring into Michigan. The polls are clear: We CAN beat Mike Rogers and keep this seat blue. But it’s going to be a nail-biter. When we have the resources to reach every voter in Michigan with our message, we win. But only if we don 't fall further behind. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | Let's do this. name: James Carville
subject: Good, bad, ugly
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Chris Pappas for Senate
body:
I got three things to tell ya
Carville again.
I got three things to tell ya, so listen up:
**THE GOOD:** The brainiacs at _Cook Political_ shifted Ohio's Senate race to TOSS UP, and now all-star Sherrod Brown's chances of winning are even stronger!! All I can say to that is _hell yes!_
**THE BAD:** Trump lackey Jon Husted still has an itty-bitty edge in the polls.
**THE UGLY:** The GOP moneybags are propping Husted up with $79 MILLION BUCKS – and we still got a long way to go, y'all.
We got a once in a blue moon shot to boot Jon the Con outta power, and I'm first in line to help do it! But there's no Y'ALL without ALL – so that means you gotta join me.

name: Team Demand Justice PAC
subject: Cronies, loyalists, and political puppets
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Demand Justice PAC
body:
[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/54940?refcode=em20260502-61417&t=1001&akid=61417%2E1407376%2E5tnHF8 ]Demand Justice PAC[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/54940?refcode=em20260502-61417&t=1002&akid=61417%2E1407376%2E5tnHF8 ]Demand Justice PAC

Trump’s court-packing
scheme is already in motion.

From federal nominees all the way to the Supreme Court, Trump and the GOP
are working to reshape the judiciary with judges who don’t reflect
independence or commitment to the rule of law.

These are his personal lawyers, loyalists, and political puppets installed
to do Trump’s bidding — to shield his abuses of power, protect his allies,
and bend the courts to serve him at any cost.

Those are the stakes, and it’s exactly why Demand Justice PAC exists.
We’re building the infrastructure to expose and stop this scheme at every
level — including the Supreme Court.

We’ve launched a $3 million campaign to block Trump’s next Supreme Court
nominee before a seat even opens up. Because once the nomination is made,
the fight becomes harder.

We’re meeting this moment early, aggressively, and with the full force of
a coordinated movement.

Because the truth is, this fight won’t be decided by a single hearing or a
vote. It will be decided by whether we organize early enough, push hard
enough, and refuse to let the courts become just another tool of political
power.

If you’re sick of watching the rules get bent to serve Trump’s agenda,
this is your moment to step in.

[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/55459?c0=dj-em-20260502.vRVzTK&refcode=em20260502-61417&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1003&akid=61417%2E1407376%2E5tnHF8 ]Chip in $5 today to help us fight back, hold the line on

**3\. Here’s what has me on edge: new polling shows this race is neck and neck.**

Falling short right now gives my MAGA opponent a massive opening to surge ahead of us in the polls. [**_That’s why we’re racing to raise $5,342 before midnight. Will you chip in $10 or $25 – or even just $1 – to ensure we keep Wisconsin blue in November?_**](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/7039?refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2026.05.02_Short-EOM_B1_F1_S1_C1_30DO30DC_N-T0&t=8&akid=6118%2E196590%2EtKgmPi)

name: Haley Stevens
subject: School picture day photos, anyone?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Haley Stevens for Senate
body:
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. [To unsubscribe from this email list, please click here.](https://act.haleyformi.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?&t=1&akid=2901%2E49239%2EJ0Lvm0)
| | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/17798?t=2&akid=2901%2E49239%2EJ0Lvm0)
---
null, I want to share a bit about my story – and some fun pics! – but I'll give you a heads-up that this is a bit of a longer one. So if you don't have time to read now, I have to ask:
**[_Will you rush $5 to help me win this race, flip the Senate, and fight for working families in Michigan? With Donald Trump watching this race like a hawk, it couldn't be more important._](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/17799?amount=5&t=3&akid=2901%2E49239%2EJ0Lvm0)**
| | [RUSH $5 NOW](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/17799?amount=5&t=4&akid=2901%2E49239%2EJ0Lvm0)
---
[](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/17800?t=5&akid=2901%2E49239%2EJ0Lvm0)
See that little girl in the photo? That's me! Way before I had the honor of serving Michiganders in Congress and launching this battleground campaign to be Michigan's next U.S. senator. I was about 12 or 13 years old there.
But don't be fooled by school picture day (_and my trendy beaded necklace_). Growing up as the daughter of small-business owners – Mom and Dad had their own landscaping business – I was always around a lot of dirt, grease, grime, and heavy machinery. **I wouldn't have had it any other way.**
My parents taught me the value of hard work – a value that binds all of us in Michigan together. After all, we're the state that built America. The state that built the middle class.
In fact, do you know what one of my favorite memories is from growing up? When I got the keys to my first car, a used Oldsmobile Cutlass Supreme. That car represented the newfound freedom of my teenage years. **It wasn't quite the showstopper you usually see at the Woodward Dream Cruises, but it got the job done, and it took me all over our beautiful state.**
[](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/17800?t=6&akid=2901%2E49239%2EJ0Lvm0)
**I love Michigan. I love the people here. And I love that I've been able to fight for my state in Congress.
**
But as I've traveled our state recently, it's clear that the people I love are afraid. They're afraid because of the chaos and uncertainty that Donald Trump and his MAGA allies are inflicting upon our nation.
All across our state, I hear concerns about rising costs, unaffordable health care, and the future of Social Security and Medicare.

And you better believe people are outright disturbed by Trump's attacks on our democracy and personal freedoms. **This fear, uncertainty, and chaos – all of it is**** _unacceptable._**
That's why I'm running for U.S. Senate. I'm ready to fight for the people I love and help block Trump's dangerous agenda. But the path to the Senate majority runs right through Michigan.
**null, we can't show any weakness here.** Donald Trump and his megadonor allies are waiting to pounce, and they'll throw millions at this race the second they see an opening.
**We can't give them one. It's crucial that we continue making a strong showing, so I have to ask: _[Will you rush $5, $25, or whatever you can before midnight to help me flip the senate?](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/17800?t=7&akid=2901%2E49239%2EJ0Lvm0)_**
_Please use the links in this email to start a monthly donation through ActBlue._ | | | [Donate $5](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/17801?t=8&akid=2901%2E49239%2EJ0Lvm0)
---
| | [Donate $25](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/17802?t=9&akid=2901%2E49239%2EJ0Lvm0)
---
| | [Donate $50](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/17803?t=10&akid=2901%2E49239%2EJ0Lvm0)
---
| | [Donate Another Amount](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/17800?t=11&akid=2901%2E49239%2EJ0Lvm0)
---
Thank you,

Haley Stevens
|
* * *
**A note from Haley Stevens:** null, I'm proud to be a lifelong Michigander and the daughter of small-business owners. That's why I've fought so hard for working families my entire life. Now, I'm running for Michigan's open Senate seat to continue fighting for the people and communities I love. The path to the Senate majority runs right through Michigan, which means this race is shaping up to be the most competitive and expensive in the entire country. The GOP is already coming after me as a top target for defeat, so I'm counting on your grassroots support to fight back and win. [**If you're in this fight with me, will you add your name to endorse my campaign?**](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/17804?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=12&akid=2901%2E49239%2EJ0Lvm0) | | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/17798?t=13&akid=2901%2E49239%2EJ0Lvm0)
---
You can also text “JOIN” to 80242 to become a core member of our team and receive exclusive, real-time updates from my campaign.
Thank you,
Haley

name: Alex Kelloff
subject: The Kelloff Chronicle Newsletter
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alex Kelloff for Colorado
body:
https://alexkelloff.com/ [https://alexkelloff.com/]A Letter from Alex
I am writing this on April 30, 2026 which marks one year since I launched my campaign for Colorado’s 3rd Congressional District (CO-3). I started 18 months before the midterm election because I don’t show up for work late. I start early. I wanted to listen to, and learn from, the constituents of this massive 53,000 square mile district.
This experience has allowed me to meet thousands of Democrats, Independents and Republicans from Craig to Grand Junction, Durango to Pueblo to La Junta, and literally everywhere in between. It has provided me with insight into the everyday concerns of our neighbors and has helped to shape and inform my platform. To prioritize the people of our district, I am running to:
* Create good jobs through encouraging small business formation so that people can live where they work and continue to pursue the American Dream
* Lower the cost of living through building and maintaining the critical infrastructure our communities rely on (housing, utilities, transportation, telecommunications, water) and ensuring affordable healthcare for all
* Protect our public lands and water , because once these are gone we will never get them back
* Uphold the rule of law and ensure that Congress fulfills its obligation as a co-equal branch of government
Recently, I have increasingly been asked: “What have you learned from campaigning over the last 12 months?” This question has been very powerful to me in focusing my thoughts and allowing me to reflect. But the answer is easy:
I have learned that we do not have representation in Washington D.C. that reflects rural American interests and values.
My Republican opponent, Jeff Hurd, voted for the One Big Beautiful Bill which guts Medicaid, among other programs. In CO-3, we have 13 rural hospitals which rely on Medicaid to provide care. Without adequate funding for Medicaid, we do not have farms and ranches that put food on America’s tables, nor do we maintain self-sufficient food production. Why? Because our farmers and ranchers – like everyone else in these rural communities – rely on rural hospitals for healthcare. Without rural hospitals, our farms and ranches will eventually cease to exist.
Most Americans value our public lands and water. But in CO-3, they are critical to our livelihood and way of life. Outdoor recreation is the largest economic driver in CO-3, so it is critical that we have elected representatives who fight for these issues. Instead, Republican Jeff Hurd, upon taking office in early 2025 proposed his “Productive Public Lands Act.” His Act would significantly increase resource development on public lands and, more importantly, its language was taken straight from “Project 2025,” the MAGA playbook. And while I’m not against the natural resources industry per se – as it is important for our country's economy – I am against Jeff Hurd giving away our public lands at the expense of the American people simply because he has been bought by special interest groups who have donated millions of dollars to his campaign.
Clearly, we don’t have an elected representative in Washington D.C. right now who is fighting for our rural interests and values. This is exactly what I have learned and been reminded of over and over again in the last 12 months of campaigning. And this is why I am focused on bringing real leadership – uninfluenced by special interest groups and corporate PAC money – to Washington to represent all constituents of Colorado’s 3rd Congressional District.
Together we can flip this seat in November. Together, we can get back to real, pragmatic leadership that will put our district, state, and country back on a path to prosperity for all. Please join me in making it happen.
— Alex
April Recap:
April was another big month for our campaign - and it’s clear the momentum is real.
Alex continues to travel across Colorado’s 3rd District and build strong momentum on the ground. As we move toward the summer, our priority stays the same: show up on time, put in the effort, and earn every vote.
This month also marked major milestones in the campaign calendar, including our one-year anniversary since launching this campaign . Since the start of this campaign a year ago, we have achieved a great deal in this time including visiting all 27 counties in the district and talking with thousands of people, and listening to their stories.
Alex traveled to Carbondale, Grand Junction, Rifle, Pueblo, Basalt, Glenwood Springs, Walsenburg, Alamosa, Pagosa Springs, Antonito, Mancos, and Montrose, meeting with and listening to constituents across CO-03.
Everywhere we go in the district, the cost of living, healthcare, protecting our public lands and water, and upholding the rule of law are among the top concerns.
http://secure.actblue.com/donate/2605-q2-ak? [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/2605-q2-ak?]
Alex also stepped up to speak out on an issue that matters deeply across the district - accountability and transparency around ICE detention facilities.
He delivered public comment in Alamosa calling for the closure of the ICE detention facility, and later spoke at the Glenwood Springs Planning and Zoning Commission meeting, where officials were considering whether to revoke the facility’s special use permit due to code violations. This facility has been a key hub for ICE operations on the Western Slope - so the outcome of that meeting carried major implications.
We’re proud to report that the commission voted to revoke ICE’s special use permit.
It’s a powerful example of what happens when communities show up, speak out, and demand accountability - and it’s exactly the kind of leadership Alex is committed to bringing to Congress.
http://secure.actblue.com/donate/2605-q2-ak? [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/2605-q2-ak?]
This month, Alex was also proud to earn the endorsement of the National Organization for Women (NOW PAC).
In their endorsement, NOW PAC recognized Alex’s commitment to standing up for women and the LGBTQ+ community, fighting to expand access to healthcare, and advancing equality and justice for all.
It’s a strong affirmation of the values at the core of this campaign - and the leadership Alex will bring to Congress. Alex is also proud to have earned endorsements from leaders across the District from small towns and large towns alike.
http://secure.actblue.com/donate/2605-q2-ak? [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/2605-q2-ak?]
Holding Hurd Accountable
The Disappearing Act: Despite his frequent claims of being a "workhorse" for the district, Jeff Hurd still refuses to hold open, public town halls where he can be held accountable by his constituents. Instead, he has limited his outreach to private roundtables and highly curated meetings with hand-picked stakeholders. It was only after Lauren Boebert’s protege, Ron Hanks, officially launched a primary challenge that Hurd suddenly began reaching out to groups like Crestone Indivisible. This sudden interest in meeting with the center and left is not a change of heart, but rather a desperate move by a representative who is terrified of losing his primary and is looking for a political life raft. A representative who only listens when his job is on the line is not representing Colorado, he is merely campaigning for himself.
The Big Ugly Bill’s Toll on Rural Health: Jeff Hurd has spent the last few months on a photo-op tour of hospitals from Cortez to the San Luis Valley. He visited Southwest Memorial Hospital in Montezuma County and local clinics in La Jara and Manassa to pose with medical staff, but he carefully avoids talking about how his vote for H.R. 1, the Big Ugly Bill, is breaking our rural healthcare system. We are already seeing the results of this legislation across the district. Delta Health was recently forced to close its labor and delivery unit because of financial strain, and recent reports list both Southwest Memorial and Conejos County Hospital as being at high risk of shutting down entirely. For families in our community, this translates to skyrocketing insurance premiums in counties like Montrose and Garfield while local care options disappear. Hurd's support for the Big Ugly Bill was a direct hit on the survival of our local clinics and ensures that we will keep paying more for less care.
Accountability? Not on Hurd’s Watch: Jeff Hurd spent this April handing more power to Washington while ignoring the need for oversight back home. He voted for a budget resolution that serves as a multi-billion dollar blank check for Homeland Security. By choosing this budget path, Hurd is intentionally bypassing the accountability rules and body camera requirements that would keep our communities safe and transparent. He also voted to keep the FISA Section 702 spying program alive, which is a direct violation of our civil liberties- something Trump himself acknowledged in 2024! Hurd shows time after time that when push comes to shove, he will vote with party leadership and in favor of the lawless actions of this administration.
What’s Coming Soon
The next few weeks are packed with opportunities to connect with folks across the district. We will be announcing our By the People Tour soon that will start in early May! Stay tuned for specific times and locations. Our By the People Tour will not be your typical poltical event. It will be a series of workshops across the district where neighbors come together, build community and have a role and voice in writing the plan that we want for our future. We are continuing to add new events across the district to learn and listen. Stay tuned for specific times and locations - and be sure to check our emails and social media accounts as new events are added. We look forward to seeing you on the trail!
* Rifle: Cinco de Mayo Festival
May 1st
* Parachute: MayFest
May 2nd
* Grand Junction: Conversation on Public Lands and Water
May 7th
* Carbondale: Dandelion Days
May 9th
* Montrose Meet and Greet
May 10th
* Glenwood Springs: Garfield County Democrats Annual Fundraiser
May 17th
* Pueblo West: Meet and Greet
May 18th 6:00-7:00pm, Pueblo West Library Room: Jerry King B
* Boulder: Meet and Greet
May 29th, 6:00-7:00pm, Avanti Roof
* Marble: Marble Democrats Candidate Forum
June 2nd
* Denver: DemFest and the Obama Gala
June 6th
* Grand Junction: Mesa Dems Spring Fling
June 13th
In addition to these events, we’ll be hosting fundraisers and continuing our tour of public events all across the district - showing up, listening, and making sure every community has a voice.
If you’d like to host a Meet & Greet to meet Alex, ask questions, and share your thoughts, please email campaign@alexkelloff.com [campaign@alexkelloff.com] and a member of our team will follow up quickly to discuss scheduling. We’d also be happy to schedule a virtual meet and greet.
We’d love to hear from you - it’s incredibly important that Alex hears from the people he hopes to represent.
What You Can Do
1. FOLLOW ON SOCIAL MEDIA: Stay up to date with my team at Facebook, [https://www.facebook.com/alexkelloffco/] X [https://x.com/alexkelloffco] , Instagram [https://www.instagram.com/alexkelloffco/] , TikTok [https://www.tiktok.com/@alexkelloffco] , Bluesky [https://bsky.app/profile/alexkelloffco.bsky.social] and Substack [https://substack.com/@alexkelloffco?]

2. VOLUNTEER: Sign up to get involved with my campaign » [https://alexkelloff.com/volunteer]

3. FINANCIALLY SUPPORT: Donate to my campaign today » [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2605-q2-ak?]

4. HOST A MEET & GREET: Contact campaign@alexkelloff.com [campaign@alexkelloff.com] , and we'll quickly follow up with you to discuss hosting an event.

5. INTRODUCE ALEX TO VOTERS IN THE DISTRICT: Contact field@alexkelloff.com [field@alexkelloff.com] for more details.

But the answer is easy: I have learned that we do not have representation in Washington D.C. that reflects rural American interests and values. My Republican opponent, Jeff Hurd, voted for the One Big Beautiful Bill which guts Medicaid, among other programs. In CO-3, we have 13 rural hospitals which rely on Medicaid to provide care. Without adequate funding for Medicaid, we do not have farms and ranches that put food on America’s tables, nor do we maintain self-sufficient food production. Why? Because our farmers and ranchers – like everyone else in these rural communities – rely on rural hospitals for healthcare. Without rural hospitals, our farms and ranches will eventually cease to exist. Most Americans value our public lands and water. But in CO-3, they are critical to our livelihood and way of life. Outdoor recreation is the largest economic driver in CO-3, so it is critical that we have elected representatives who fight for these issues. Instead, Republican Jeff Hurd, upon taking office in early 2025 proposed his “Productive Public Lands Act.” His Act would significantly increase resource development on public lands and, more importantly, its language was taken straight from “Project 2025,” the MAGA playbook. And while I’m not against the natural resources industry per se – as it is important for our country's economy – I am against Jeff Hurd giving away our public lands at the expense of the American people simply because he has been bought by special interest groups who have donated millions of dollars to his campaign. Clearly, we don’t have an elected representative in Washington D.C. right now who is fighting for our rural interests and values. This is exactly what I have learned and been reminded of over and over again in the last 12 months of campaigning. And this is why I am focused on bringing real leadership – uninfluenced by special interest groups and corporate PAC money – to Washington to represent all constituents of Colorado’s 3rd Congressional District. Together we can flip this seat in November. Together, we can get back to real, pragmatic leadership that will put our district, state, and country back on a path to prosperity for all. Please join me in making it happen. — Alex April Recap: April was another big month for our campaign - and it’s clear the momentum is real. Alex continues to travel across Colorado’s 3rd District and build strong momentum on the ground. As we move toward the summer, our priority stays the same: show up on time, put in the effort, and earn every vote. This month also marked major milestones in the campaign calendar, including our one-year anniversary since launching this campaign . Since the start of this campaign a year ago, we have achieved a great deal in this time including visiting all 27 counties in the district and talking with thousands of people, and listening to their stories. Alex traveled to Carbondale, Grand Junction, Rifle, Pueblo, Basalt, Glenwood Springs, Walsenburg, Alamosa, Pagosa Springs, Antonito, Mancos, and Montrose, meeting with and listening to constituents across CO-03. Everywhere we go in the district, the cost of living, healthcare, protecting our public lands and water, and upholding the rule of law are among the top concerns. ? [?] Alex also stepped up to speak out on an issue that matters deeply across the district - accountability and transparency around ICE detention facilities. He delivered public comment in Alamosa calling for the closure of the ICE detention facility, and later spoke at the Glenwood Springs Planning and Zoning Commission meeting, where officials were considering whether to revoke the facility’s special use permit due to code violations. This facility has been a key hub for ICE operations on the Western Slope - so the outcome of that meeting carried major implications. We’re proud to report that the commission voted to revoke ICE’s special use permit. It’s a powerful example of what happens when communities show up, speak out, and demand accountability - and it’s exactly the kind of leadership Alex is committed to bringing to Congress. ? [?] This month, Alex was also proud to earn the endorsement of the National Organization for Women (NOW PAC). In their endorsement, NOW PAC recognized Alex’s commitment to standing up for women and the LGBTQ+ community, fighting to expand access to healthcare, and advancing equality and justice for all. It’s a strong affirmation of the values at the core of this campaign - and the leadership Alex will bring to Congress. Alex is also proud to have earned endorsements from leaders across the District from small towns and large towns alike. ? [?] Holding Hurd Accountable The Disappearing Act: Despite his frequent claims of being a "workhorse" for the district, Jeff Hurd still refuses to hold open, public town halls where he can be held accountable by his constituents. Instead, he has limited his outreach to private roundtables and highly curated meetings with hand-picked stakeholders. It was only after Lauren Boebert’s protege, Ron Hanks, officially launched a primary challenge that Hurd suddenly began reaching out to groups like Crestone Indivisible. This sudden interest in meeting with the center and left is not a change of heart, but rather a desperate move by a representative who is terrified of losing his primary and is looking for a political life raft. A representative who only listens when his job is on the line is not representing Colorado, he is merely campaigning for himself. The Big Ugly Bill’s Toll on Rural Health: Jeff Hurd has spent the last few months on a photo-op tour of hospitals from Cortez to the San Luis Valley. He visited Southwest Memorial Hospital in Montezuma County and local clinics in La Jara and Manassa to pose with medical staff, but he carefully avoids talking about how his vote for H.R. 1, the Big Ugly Bill, is breaking our rural healthcare system. We are already seeing the results of this legislation across the district. Delta Health was recently forced to close its labor and delivery unit because of financial strain, and recent reports list both Southwest Memorial and Conejos County Hospital as being at high risk of shutting down entirely. For families in our community, this translates to skyrocketing insurance premiums in counties like Montrose and Garfield while local care options disappear. Hurd's support for the Big Ugly Bill was a direct hit on the survival of our local clinics and ensures that we will keep paying more for less care. Accountability? Not on Hurd’s Watch: Jeff Hurd spent this April handing more power to Washington while ignoring the need for oversight back home. He voted for a budget resolution that serves as a multi-billion dollar blank check for Homeland Security. By choosing this budget path, Hurd is intentionally bypassing the accountability rules and body camera requirements that would keep our communities safe and transparent. He also voted to keep the FISA Section 702 spying program alive, which is a direct violation of our civil liberties- something Trump himself acknowledged in 2024! Hurd shows time after time that when push comes to shove, he will vote with party leadership and in favor of the lawless actions of this administration. What’s Coming Soon The next few weeks are packed with opportunities to connect with folks across the district. We will be announcing our By the People Tour soon that will start in early May! Stay tuned for specific times and locations. Our By the People Tour will not be your typical poltical event. It will be a series of workshops across the district where neighbors come together, build community and have a role and voice in writing the plan that we want for our future. We are continuing to add new events across the district to learn and listen. Stay tuned for specific times and locations - and be sure to check our emails and social media accounts as new events are added. We look forward to seeing you on the trail! Rifle: Cinco de Mayo Festival May 1st Parachute: MayFest May 2nd Grand Junction: Conversation on Public Lands and Water May 7th Carbondale: Dandelion Days May 9th Montrose Meet and Greet May 10th Glenwood Springs: Garfield County Democrats Annual Fundraiser May 17th Pueblo West: Meet and Greet May 18th 6:00-7:00pm, Pueblo West Library Room: Jerry King B Boulder: Meet and Greet May 29th, 6:00-7:00pm, Avanti Roof Marble: Marble Democrats Candidate Forum June 2nd Denver: DemFest and the Obama Gala June 6th * Grand Junction: Mesa Dems Spring Fling June 13th In addition to these events, we’ll be hosting fundraisers and continuing our tour of public events all across the district - showing up, listening, and making sure every community has a voice. If you’d like to host a Meet & Greet to meet Alex, ask questions, and share your thoughts, please email campaign@alexkelloff.com [campaign@alexkelloff.com] and a member of our team will follow up quickly to discuss scheduling. We’d also be happy to schedule a virtual meet and greet. We’d love to hear from you - it’s incredibly important that Alex hears from the people he hopes to represent. What You Can Do 1. FOLLOW ON SOCIAL MEDIA: Stay up to date with my team at Facebook, [] X [] , Instagram [] , TikTok [] , Bluesky [] and Substack [?] 2. VOLUNTEER: Sign up to get involved with my campaign » [] 3. FINANCIALLY SUPPORT: Donate to my campaign today » [?] 4. HOST A MEET & GREET: Contact campaign@alexkelloff.com [campaign@alexkelloff.com] , and we'll quickly follow up with you to discuss hosting an event. 5. INTRODUCE ALEX TO VOTERS IN THE DISTRICT: Contact field@alexkelloff.com [field@alexkelloff.com] for more details. [] [] [] [] ? [?] Alex Kelloff for Colorado P.O. clean_body: I want Peter to win it next! | | | | This is your President, Donald J. Trump. Out of all my supporters, you've gone the extra mile! Without your support, I wouldn’t be where I am today. Making America GREAT again is a team effort, and I can’t thank you enough. Vice President JD Vance and I are constantly combing through lists of our top supporters, and you’ve been at the top EVERY TIME! After careful consideration, I want to present you with the MAGA GOLD MEDAL award! It’s the highest honor I can bestow upon such an important supporter. For all you’ve done, it’s the least I can do. Thank you for making America Great Again! Thank you! Donald J. Trump | | | | | | | | | Contributions to Trump National Committee JFC Inc. are not deductible for federal income tax purposes. All contributions are subject to the limits and prohibitions of the Federal Election Campaign Act. Contributions from corporations, foreign nationals (i.e., without “green cards”), federal government contractors, and other federally impermissible sources are strictly prohibited. Contributions made in the name of, or refunded by, any other person are unlawful. | Paid for by Trump National Committee JFC, Inc., P.O. Box 509, Arlington, VA 22216. Not Authorized by any Candidate or Candidate’s Committee.

TV and digital ads, and mailers may get voters attention, but it’s those personal conversations that leave a lasting impression and make sure we can check in on our neighbors. That’s why, five months from Election Day, our campaign team is already out there knocking on doors and connecting with people directly. [****](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm3-JL95hRMRjX122SkPIfLx8Grb712BdLQ9n_roV_ea4MEdM0BM9NaWJEteu3ycG0n2aA4Wct6_biu9qNniknyNk2TXPoamzCo-X0Q6LM432Um5fO-0ls9Iiylytq-_PM_nLJbKaXST3S8yKr1HOtbrBYEm4WzNvOjPIIdNBr3g0qMjvDdemxe5MJrzh9S93QyfeMQW7tWapWjLPR3x3tuQ2ge51gnMD7K_lDEt0IPjWRpWwiTRo3HDxNgjd75JFyiI8ga6msxiZQ0lyc6bYIU1MHUUGM753gnHrd-z587LkdxJC03p9jlnUFR72JJ1z3gPz8h4QyIVSsWp8c7-vJevUvgmOpFp9fUhNSTFJh8EDi9u3onJu4bm5EZu5cf1PFerEjOnBuq-EgqnT0KadbK2TI119Khv21ugk4hsrgr078TWOeV2H_uJA9iamAvD1n6iWwPd1fRp9boXHr1u-Yheo/4qa/qAD2HJoSQ7CVY2R0O-fnmg/h1/h001.dWaD1GOxXzpLo-Lq-LV46ZLHLvbUq_QJyWhn01p_5HI) **Last week we learned that in our upcoming Democratic primary election, we will have not one, but two opponents.** We expect them to be well funded by billionaire special interests. In recent Democratic primaries, we’ve seen outside groups spend millions of dollars to try and install candidates who represent their interests, not the interests of local residents.
While Rashida is fighting for the right to affordable utilities, including water and electricity, fighting back against greedy corporations that put profits over workers and pollute our environment, and fighting for human rights at home and abroad, special interests will spend virtually unlimited resources to manipulate the conversation and prop up their handpicked candidates.

Together, we’re bringing Rashida’s message to all corners of the 12th congressional district and countering special interest money with grassroots organizing. Thank you, Team Rashida Rashida Tlaib for Congress PO Box 32777 Detroit, MI 48232 | Paid for by Rashida Tlaib for Congress | | Sent via . To update your email address, change your name or address, or to stop receiving emails from Rashida Tlaib for Congress, please .

name: DNC Polling Alert
subject: We need to act quickly, Derek.
body:
---
Derek, In case you haven’t heard, the **Florida congressional map is in danger** as Republicans continue to attempt to rig the midterms in their own favor. **It’s up to us to act quickly.** That’s why we’re reaching out to you today: [**Will you join our focus group to share your thoughts on redistricting?**](https://ak.democrats.org/signup/20260502_redistricting_survey?refcode=em_20260502_6976_fr&akid=6976%2E22930333%2EoZkRkL) **[TAKE THE SURVEY](https://ak.democrats.org/signup/20260502_redistricting_survey?refcode=em_20260502_6976_fr&akid=6976%2E22930333%2EoZkRkL)** Democrats in states like California and Virginia have been fighting back. California’s Prop-50 passed on election night last year, and Virginia’s redistricting referendum passed in a special election just last month, successfully redrawing their districts to even the playing field with Republicans’ attempts to draw themselves into a majority this year. [**Today, we want to hear from our grassroots supporters about what they think. We’re only asking the most engaged Democrats in Pennsylvania to respond, so can we count on you to take just a minute and fill out this quick survey?**](https://ak.democrats.org/signup/20260502_redistricting_survey?refcode=em_20260502_6976_fr&akid=6976%2E22930333%2EoZkRkL) **[TAKE THE SURVEY](https://ak.democrats.org/signup/20260502_redistricting_survey?refcode=em_20260502_6976_fr&akid=6976%2E22930333%2EoZkRkL)** Thank you, The Democrats

_If you gave recently via check or mail, our system may not have yet processed your gift. We are appreciative and thank you greatly for your support._

name: John Fetterman
subject: The Hot Rotisserie Chicken Act 🍗
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fetterman for PA
body:
re: America’s best (and most delicious) affordability play
# [](https://act.johnfetterman.com/go/51612?refcode=jtk540-su-fl-header&t=1&akid=10736%2E434096%2Em9Xf1a)
| | |
---
Derek,
America’s best (and most delicious) affordability play is Costco’s $4.99 rotisserie chicken. It’s one of my family’s favorites.
But right now, the 38 million Americans on SNAP aren’t able to use their benefits to purchase this hot, healthy meal for reasons that don’t make any damn sense. The rules are outdated, and we’ve got to change them.
**That’s why I’m proud to introduce the Hot Rotisserie Chicken Act 🍗 with my colleagues in the Senate. SNAP recipients here in PA + across the country should have the option to put this quick, nutritious dinner on the table when they need it.**
**[If you agree that folks on SNAP should be allowed to buy hot foods, will you sign on to my new petition by midnight? I’m looking for 20,000 signatures, and I’m counting on yours to be one of them.](https://act.johnfetterman.com/go/53269?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&code=jtk540-su-fl&t=2&akid=10736%2E434096%2Em9Xf1a)**

John
