clean_body: Derek, it’s been almost six months since the 2025 elections, which means that in Virginia, New York, and New Jersey, Abigail Spanberger, Zohran Mamdani, and Mikie Sherrill have reached the 100-day mark in their terms. We wrote to you a lot in the lead up to last year’s elections about the work that these Democrats hoped to do to better the lives of their constituents, so we wanted to share an update on their first 100 days in office. In her first 100 days, Governor Spanberger signed bills to lower the price of insulin, establish the first paid family and medical leave in the South , and stop middlemen from inflating prescription drug costs. She’s invested billions of dollars in Virginia businesses, created more than 3,000 jobs, and raised the minimum wage to $15 an hour. Governor Sherrill opened her term by signing a series of more than a dozen executive orders focused on making life more affordable for New Jerseyans. She’s made improvements to New Jersey’s public transportation system. She sued the Trump administration for withholding infrastructure funding and signed executive orders to drive efficiency, transparency, and accountability across state government. In his first 100 days, Mayor Mamdani has secured $1.2 billion in funding for universal child care , won over $34 million in damages on behalf of tenants, and filled more than 100,000 potholes. In his first 100 days, New York City reached record-low crime rates. He has invested millions of dollars in small businesses and appointed a “Mom-and-Pop” czar. This is what Democrats can accomplish in 100 days. Derek, one year from now, we will have reached the 100th day of the 120th Congress. That could mean 100 more days of unchecked power for an even more empowered Donald Trump. Or we could have 100 days of beginning to unravel the damage done by the Trump administration. 100 days of reimplementing the checks and balances that keep our democracy running. 100 days of Democratic majorities in the House and Senate. We know which option we’d prefer — and we think you do, too. If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly. | | | | | | | | | | ** Thank you for your support, The Democrats If you gave recently via check or mail, our system may not have yet processed your gift. We are appreciative and thank you greatly for your support._

---|---|---
This week on _Amped Up_ , Cliff and David Shuster sit down with **Kait Justice** --the Substack investigator earning the nickname "the Epstein whisperer"--and the conversation goes where anyone in corporate media would start wetting their pants like a Russian insider staying in a hotel room with a balcony on the 5th floor.
The Supreme Court just gutted the Voting Rights Act in _Louisiana v. Callais_. Three weeks earlier, on April 9, Melania Trump delivered an unprompted six-and-a-half-minute statement at the White House: denying her own ties to Epstein, calling for survivors to testify under oath before Congress, and pointedly never defending her husband. And one of Trump's longtime confidants, a man who allegedly trafficked his own ex-wife out of Brazil--sits inside the current administration. Kait connects every dot. _Cliff and Shuster eagerly encourage the dots to be compliant to Kate 's demands._
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/99953856-72c1-4bd9-898b-7a730f18f71d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The pattern she uncovers is the entire predator-class playbook in one frame: a corrupt court protecting itself, a billionaire-and-spook network reaching back to the 1953 Iran coup, and a current conflagration that just happens to enrich the Saudi royal court, Jared Kushner's affinity fund, Howard Lutnick, and a string of no-bid defense contracts to the _right_ billionaires.
Trump tired of waking each morning via exposure to light, or with cardiac paddles, so so at least he has the massive corruption to help him rise each new day. [Because he's losing just about everything else](https://substack.com/redirect/0d3c52c3-4cb0-43be-b397-a7d006374a0e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and that doesn't include that he has to admit to being Eric's dad.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/99953856-72c1-4bd9-898b-7a730f18f71d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Kait walks through what she found out about Bill Barr's family, and when combined with Cliff's personal experiences in New York with those connected to the Barrs--and his background in history of U.S. intelligence--they put some serious puzzle pieces in place. You'll never look at the Trump's corrupt-as-Tammany-Hall-on-meth DOJ the same way again.
Because they go By the time the conversation reaches Epstein's so-called "suicide," Kait offers a third option nobody on in monopoly media will say out loud. Ever.
_This is the episode you send to the friend who still thinks "it's all just a coincidence."_
Friends, this is a conversation that doesn't happen in corporate news--because they're owned by the predator class Kait calls out so they seemingly are on a daily mission to [to pretend Trump's reality is actual reality](https://substack.com/redirect/4fb24c09-027d-40be-b5b4-a0659226045e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)...and there's the corner office and no way is Joe-Blow Journalist's risking that! **Blue Amp Media** --or BAM, as we're affectionately known--is independent, un-captured,[ ](https://substack.com/redirect/99953856-72c1-4bd9-898b-7a730f18f71d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[and only here because](https://substack.com/redirect/99953856-72c1-4bd9-898b-7a730f18f71d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** _**[you](https://substack.com/redirect/99953856-72c1-4bd9-898b-7a730f18f71d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_**[fund it](https://substack.com/redirect/99953856-72c1-4bd9-898b-7a730f18f71d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**.

name: Cori Bush
subject: Not today, AIPAC
disclaimer_text: Paid for by National Black
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter,

Cori Bush here. In case you missed it, I’m running to represent Missouri’s
first congressional district again, because I refuse to let billionaires
and corporations call the shots in our communities. Can I count on you to
[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20260502-cb.HJPmqI&refcode=em20260502-37739&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1002&akid=37739%2E1203867%2EX-nY9D ]split a donation between my campaign and Justice Democrats right now to
make sure we have the resources to push back?

A new dark-money group has elbowed their way into our race. In a democracy
where the people choose, billionaires and corporations are doing
everything they can to get their way.

They think they can play St. Louis like we won’t notice what’s really
going on, but they are mistaken. This group calls itself the National
Black Empowerment Action Fund. But here’s the truth:

There is nothing empowering about a group run by operatives who have spent
decades on AIPAC’s payroll. It’s the same people who have tried again and
again to silence voices like mine.

Now they’re pouring money into this race to take control away from the
people.

They’re not here to “empower” voters. They’re here to manipulate our
community and buy this seat for the same right-wing interests that have
been attacking our progressive movement from day one.

Just look at the billboard they put up in St. Louis:

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20260502-cb.HJPmqI&refcode=em20260502-37739&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1003&akid=37739%2E1203867%2EX-nY9D ]Photo of billboard that says U.S. Representative Wesley Bell thank you
for your leadership and service to St. Louis. Paid for by National Black
Empowerment Action Fund

This is the playbook:

1. Put a friendly face on a dark-money operation.

2. Pretend to speak for our community.

3. Use Republican megadonor cash to influence a Democratic primary.

They did it last election. AIPAC spent over $9 million attacking our
movement and backing my opponent. Now they’re gearing up to do it again,
with new front groups who won’t have our backs.

Peter, this is
exactly why we need a strong, people-powered response today.

If we don’t answer this attack now, they will define the conversation
before we can even speak.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20260502-cb.HJPmqI&refcode=em20260502-37739&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1004&akid=37739%2E1203867%2EX-nY9D ]Will you rush a $10, $25, or $50 donation to my campaign and Justice
Democrats to help us push back against AIPAC’s dark-money groups and
protect this seat for the people of St. Louis?

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20260502-cb.HJPmqI&refcode=em20260502-37739&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1023&akid=37739%2E1203867%2EX-nY9D ]Contribute $5

This race has never been about me. It’s about who gets to decide the
future of St. Louis: the people, or the Republican megadonors who think
they can buy our district’s voice.

Let’s show them our community cannot be bought.

In solidarity,

Cori Bush

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20260502-37739-footer&t=1024&refcode2=37739_1203867_X-nY9D&akid=37739%2E1203867%2EX-nY9D

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1025&akid=37739%2E1203867%2EX-nY9D

PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.
JusticeDemocrats.com
1390 Columbia Ave #261
Lancaster, PA 17603
Email us: us@justicedemocrats.com
